<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732475 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03126: (2026x) FMU import

- page_id: `272732475`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732475/2026x+FMU+import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:39.194+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Importing External Simulation Models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**32**

FMU can be used for:

- Creating model structure with SysML v1 elements.
- Co-simulating the imported model with help of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='MMA'] (CST). To learn more, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation of SysML models' space='MMA'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SSP Export - Technology Preview' space='MMA'] .

Simulation of FMU 3.0 and merged arrays using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.

##### Understanding the FMU Import Options dialog

The **FMU Import Options** dialog enables users to customize the FMU file import into the model. For example, you can specify whether parameters from the FMU file should be imported as ports or value properties. Additionally, you can change the name of the Block (the FMU model name is used by default) and automatically attach the file to the project upon import.

The **FMU Import Options** dialog has the following options:

| Option name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Direction | The direction of the port: In or Out. Inputs and Outputs are selected to be imported as ports by default. |
| Name | The name of the property/parameter. |
| Type | The type of the property, for example, value property. |
| Description | The textual description of the property. |
| As Port | Click to select whether properties should be represented as Flow or Proxy ports. Select None to import the selected properties as value properties. |
| Interface Block | Click to select a compatible interface Block or create a new one. <NEW> is selected by default.The Interface Block column is active only if Proxy Port is selected in the As Port column. |
| Redefine | Click to redefine inherited value or part properties. |
|  | Click to select all listed properties at once. |
|  | Click to deselect all listed properties at once. |
|  | The name of the Block created in the model upon import. By default, the name of the FMU model is used, but you can set the one you like. |
|  | If the checkbox is selected, the imported FMU file is automatically attached to the model.If the Attach file to the project is checked in the FMU Import Options dialog, a previous version of the attached file is replaced with a new one upon import. |
|  | If the checkbox is selected, the FMU properties are split according to dot notation. The FMU properties are represented as Flow or Value properties, owned by an Interface Block. For structured properties, As Port and Interface Block columns are inactive. |
|  | If the checkbox is selected, the arrays (properties with the same name and index) are merged. When the arrays are merged, a single property is created with a multiplicity set. The checkbox is available only when arrays are detected in an imported FMU file.Co-simulation of the merged arrays is not supported. |
|  | When clicked, displays information about the imported FMU. The following parameters are displayed in the expanded section.Format - Always displayedFMI version - Always displayedPlatforms - Always displayedVariables - Always displayedGeneration Date - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)Generation Tool - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)Description - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)Documentation- Dynamic (Displayed, if available) |

##### Importing FMU

To import the FMU file into the model

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click File > Import From > FMU File . [ATTACHMENT filename='fmu_file_import.png']
2. Select the FMU file on your file system and click Open . [ATTACHMENT filename='Import dialog.png']
3. In the **FMU Import Options** dialog, specify which properties from the FMU file should be imported into your model as values and ports and Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on diagram

Alternatively, you can import the FMU model by dragging and dropping the selected FMU file directly onto the Block Definition (BDD) and/or Internal Block (IBD) diagrams.

To import the FMU file into the model using BDD/IBD

1. Locate the FMU file on your file system or the Containment tree.
2. Do either:
  - Drag and drop the file from your file system or the Containment tree onto the BDD diagram. A Block with the applied «FMU» stereotype is created in the model after customizing the FMU import via the FMU Import Options dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_fmu_block_bdd.png']
  - Drag and drop the file from your file system or the Containment tree onto the IBD diagram. A Part property is created in the model after customizing the FMU import via the FMU Import Options dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_fmu_block_ibd.png']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on existing Block

You can drag and drop the FMU file directly onto the Block as implementation as well. Once the FMU file is dropped, the**FMU Import Options** dialog opens. This dialog allows redefining value and part properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Redefining properties using FMU Import Options dialog

In this case, a new Block is created as a subtype of the existing Block together with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generalization' space=''] relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dropping FMU on an existing Block

**In/Out properties** in the **FMU Import Options** dialog are by default imported into the model as ports of a Flow type.

###### Updating existing FMU Block

An existing FMU Block can be updated by dragging and dropping a FMU file directly on it. This is useful in those cases when there is a need to quickly import properties that were initially left out.

To update a FMU Block

1. Locate the FMU file on your file system or the Containment tree.
2. Drag and drop it on the shape of your FMU Block.
3. In the opened FMU Import Options dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on the existing FMU Block

Alternatively, you can update the FMU Block directly from the shortcut menu.

To update a FMU Block from the shortcut menu

1. Right-click the Simulink Block.
2. Select Tools > Update from FMU file from the shortcut menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_from_fmu.png']
3. In the opened FMU Import Options dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_fmu.png']
4. Click OK .

Already imported properties are grayed out and cannot be edited in the **FMU Import Options**dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Structured Import

If you select the Structured Import checkbox, then the FMU properties are split according to dot notation. The FMU properties are represented as Flow or Value properties, owned by an Interface Block.For structured properties, **As Port**and **Interface Block** columns are inactive.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### FMU properties split according to dot notation.

##### Merging arrays

If the FMU file has arrays (properties with the same name and index), then selecting the Merge Arrays checkbox in the FMU Import Options dialog merges all the arrays into a single property with the multiplicity set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All the arrays present in the FMU file merged into a single property with the multiplicity set.

##### Viewing FMU details

You can view the details about the imported FMU file in the FMU Import Options dialog. Once you click the **Details**button, the details are displayed in the expanded section of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Details about the imported FMU file displayed in the expanded section of the FMU Import Options dialog.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation of SysML models' space='MMA']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="028bbd9c-4927-48bc-97d1-da077c11d0f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell>Functional Mock-up Interface, also known as FMI, is a tool-independent open-source standard that supports both model exchange and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[co-simulation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of dynamic models in a standardized format. The modeling tool supports all the major FMI versions (1.0, 2.0 and 3.0). A component that implements FMI is called a Functional Mock-up Unit (FMU). The FMU stores a simulation model that adheres to the FMI standard. To be more specific, the FMU file contains an XML description file and implementation in a binary form. <p>FMU can be used for:</p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Creating model structure with SysML v1 elements.</span></li><li>Co-simulating the imported model with help of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Simulation Toolkit]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (CST). To learn more, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="SSP Export - Technology Preview" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SSP export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a30479d1-caf0-4396-8340-e855210f637b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Simulation of FMU 3.0 and merged arrays using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Understanding the FMU Import Options dialog</h3><p>The <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog enables users to customize the FMU file import into the model. For example, you can specify whether parameters from the FMU file should be imported as ports or value properties. Additionally, you can change the name of the Block (the FMU model name is used by default) and automatically attach the file to the project upon import. </p><p>The <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog has the following options:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 68.2727%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 29.2712%;" /><col class="" style="width: 70.6837%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Option name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Direction</strong></td><td>The direction of the port: <em>In</em> or <em>Out</em>. <em>Inputs</em> and <em>Outputs </em>are selected to be imported as ports by default.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>The name of the property/parameter.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Type</strong></td><td>The type of the property, for example, value property.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Description</strong></td><td>The textual description of the property.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>As Port</strong></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click to select whether properties should be represented as Flow or Proxy ports. Select None to import the selected properties as value properties.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Interface Block</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to select a compatible interface Block or create a new one. <span>&lt;NEW&gt; is selected by default.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01905682-2985-4a08-b900-bef8b85c3585"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Interface Block column is active only if <strong>Proxy Port</strong> is selected in the <strong>As Port</strong> column.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Redefine</strong></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click to redefine inherited value or part properties.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="25" ac:width="73"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_all.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to select all listed properties at once.</td></tr><tr class=""><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="24" ac:width="88"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_none.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to deselect all listed properties at once.</td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="33" ac:width="195"><ri:attachment ri:filename="block_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>The name of the Block created in the model upon import. By default, the name of the FMU model is used, but you can set the one you like.</td></tr><tr class=""><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="25" ac:width="141"><ri:attachment ri:filename="attach_file_to_the_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>If the checkbox is selected, the imported FMU file is automatically attached to the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac48cca1-eeec-4efb-8aab-1a26cb98b185"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Attach file to the project</strong> is checked in the <strong>FMU Import Options dialog</strong>, a previous version of the attached file is replaced with a new one upon import.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="26" ac:width="124"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structured Import_Checkbox.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the checkbox is selected, the FMU properties are split according to dot notation. The FMU <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">properties are represented as Flow or Value properties, owned by an Interface Block. </span>For structured properties, <strong>As Port </strong>and <strong>Interface Block</strong> columns are inactive.</span></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="25" ac:width="98"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Merge Arrays.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>If the checkbox is selected, the arrays (properties with the same name and index) are merged. When the arrays are merged, a single property is created with a multiplicity set. The checkbox is available <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">only when arrays are detected in an imported FMU file.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="463bed82-7d06-4011-b58f-b15dd70163b6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Co-simulation of the merged arrays is not supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="19" ac:width="68"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Details.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When clicked, displays information about the imported FMU. The following parameters are displayed in the expanded section.<br /></span></span><ul><li>Format - Always displayed</li><li>FMI version - Always displayed</li><li>Platforms - Always displayed</li><li>Variables - Always displayed</li><li>Generation Date - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)</li><li>Generation Tool - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)</li><li>Description - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)</li><li>Documentation- Dynamic (Displayed, if available)</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Importing FMU</h3><p>To import the FMU file into the model</p><hr /><ol><li>In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Import From </strong>&gt; <strong>FMU File</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu_file_import.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the FMU file on your file system and click <strong>Open</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Import dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p>In the <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog, specify which properties from the FMU file should be imported into your model as values and ports and Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="FMU Import Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h4>Dragging and dropping FMU on diagram</h4><p>Alternatively, you can import the FMU model by dragging and dropping the selected FMU file directly onto the Block Definition (BDD) and/or Internal Block (IBD) diagrams.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import the FMU file into the model using BDD/IBD</p><hr /><ol><li>Locate the <strong>FMU file</strong> on your file system or the Containment tree. </li><li>Do either:<br /><ul><li>Drag and drop the file from your file system or the Containment tree onto the BDD diagram. A Block with the applied «FMU»  stereotype is created in the model after customizing the FMU import via the<strong> FMU Import Options</strong> dialog.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_fmu_block_bdd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Drag and drop the file from your file system or the Containment tree onto the IBD diagram. A Part property is created in the model after customizing the FMU import via the<strong> FMU Import Options</strong> dialog.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_fmu_block_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li></ol><h4>Dragging and dropping FMU on existing Block</h4><p>You can drag and drop the FMU file directly onto the Block as implementation as well. Once the FMU file is dropped, the<strong> FMU Import Options</strong> dialog opens. This dialog allows redefining value and part properties.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefining_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">Redefining properties using FMU Import Options dialog</span></h6><p>In this case, a new Block is created as a subtype of the existing Block together with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization" /></ac:link> relationship. </p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu_implementation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dropping FMU on an existing Block</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4836eded-870c-4976-bba3-1c26597c5727"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>In/Out properties</strong> in the <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog are by default imported into the model as ports of a Flow type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Updating existing FMU Block</h4><p>An existing FMU Block can be updated by dragging and dropping a FMU file directly on it. This is useful in those cases when there is a need to quickly import properties that were initially left out. </p><p>To update a FMU Block</p><hr /><ol><li>Locate the FMU file on your file system or the Containment tree.</li><li>Drag and drop it on the shape of your FMU Block.</li><li>In the opened <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_existing_fmu_block.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dragging and dropping FMU on the existing FMU Block</h6><p>Alternatively, you can update the FMU Block directly from the shortcut menu. </p><p><br /></p><p>To update a FMU Block from the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Simulink Block. </li><li>Select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> Update from FMU</strong> <strong>file </strong>from the shortcut menu.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_from_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the opened <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b627fb5d-7372-4afc-9f42-465555b849ba"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Already imported properties are grayed out and cannot be edited in the <strong>FMU Import Options</strong><span> dialog.<br /></span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="greyed_out_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Structured Import</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If you select the Structured Import checkbox, then the FMU properties are split according to dot notation. The FMU <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">properties are represented as Flow or Value properties, owned by an Interface Block. </span>For structured properties, <strong>As Port </strong>and <strong>Interface Block</strong> columns are inactive.<br /><br /></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structured FMU_Enabled.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">FMU properties split according to dot notation.</h6><h3>Merging arrays</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the FMU file has arrays (properties with the same name and index), then selecting the Merge Arrays checkbox in the FMU Import Options dialog merges all the arrays into a single property with the multiplicity set.<br /><br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Merge Array result_Comparison.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All the arrays present in the FMU file merged into a single property with the multiplicity set.</h6><h3>Viewing FMU details</h3><p>You can view the details about the imported FMU file in the FMU Import Options dialog. Once you click the <strong>Details </strong>button, the details are displayed in the expanded section of the dialog.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Details_displayed.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Details about the imported FMU file displayed in the expanded section of the FMU Import Options dialog.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729124 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03127: (2026x) Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog

- page_id: `272729124`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729124/2026x+Forecast+Kinds+in+Time+Periods+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:51.076+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Dialogs > (2026x) Time Periods dialog
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

These set of instructions will explain how to choose**Forecast Kinds**and how to specify their values.

There are three types of Forecast Kind:

- - to divide the forecast into equal periods.
- - to divide the forecast into specific dates.
- **Specified** **Periods** - to divide the forecast into specific time periods.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

periodicTo specify Periodic Forecast Kind

1. Select **Periodic** as the **Forecast Kind.**
2. Specify From Date by doing one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='alt+down.png'] , or Alt+Down, and select an already existing date from the list.
  - Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select a date from the opened**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**dialog.
3. Specify forecast periods in the **Number of Periods** field.
4. Select **Length Unit:** Years, Months, or Days.
5. Specify **Period Length** of the forecast according to the **Length Unit.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Example of a Periodic Forecast Kind.

All data defined in the**Time Periods** dialog is displayed automatically in columns for each period.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Periodic Forecast Kind in the Model

#### NOTE: Specified Dates

Specified Dates

For Specified Dates Forecast Kind, you only need to specify the Forecast Dates. The previous fields are uneditable.

specifieddatesTo specify Specified Dates Forecast Kind

1. Select Specified Dates as the Forecast Kind.
2. Under Forecast Dates, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_edit_small.png'] to open the Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_forecast_dates_property.png']
3. Select the existing ISO8601DateTime element (date) from the List. You can create a new one by taking the following steps.
  - In the **Select ISO8601 Date Time** dialog, make sure **Creation Mode** is on.
  - Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new **ISO8601DateTime** element (date).
  - Click the Create button. The new date element's specification window opens automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_ISO8601_date_time_dialog.png']
  - In the Value field, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] , and select an appropriate date from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Date and Time Settings dialog' space=''] dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_value_property_of_ISO8601DateTime.png']
  - Click Close, or create more dates by repeating steps c. and d.
4. Click**OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Example of a Specified Dates Forecast Kind.

All data defined in the **Time Periods** dialog are displayed in columns in the table. In this case, three dates (2016-09-23, 2017-09-23, and 2018-03-23) were automatically divided into two columns (from 2016-09-23 to 2017-09-23, and from 2017-09-23 to 2018-03-23).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Specified Dates Forecast Kind in the Model

specifiedperiodsTo specify Specified Periods Forecast Kind

1. Select Specified Periods as the Forecast Kind.
2. In the Time Periods field, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_edit_small.png'] to open the Select Time Period dialog.
3. In the opened dialog, you can do the following:
  - Click the Tree [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.59.07 AM.png'] button and expand the tree until you find an existing Timeline element ( [ATTACHMENT filename='timeline_icon.png'] ) with existing [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and double-click on each one. Skip to Step 4.
  - Click the List [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.56.13 AM.png'] button and double-click each existing Time Periods ( [ATTACHMENT filename='time_periods_icon.png'] ). Skip to Step 4.
  - Create a new Timeline element with Time Periods . Here's how:
    - Ensure that element Creation Mode is turned on.
    - Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new Timeline element.
    - Click the Create button. The Specification window of the newly created Timeline opens automatically.
    - Type a name for the new Timeline, and click Close .
    - Select the newly created Timeline element.
    - Click the Create button, and select Time Period . The Specification window of the newly created Time Period opens automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_time_periods.png']
    - Specify a Name, Start Date, and End Date. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_time_period_start_and_end_dates.png']
    - Click Close.
4. Click OK.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Specified Periods Forecast Kind.

All time periods defined in the **Time Periods** dialog are displayed in the table as columns. In this case, three periods (2016 period, 2017 period, and 2018 period) with their specific start and end dates were defined and displayed as columns in the SV-9 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Specified Periods Forecast Kind in the Model

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Skip to Step 4.</span></li><li>Click the <strong>List </strong><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:width="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.56.13 AM.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and double-click each existing Time Periods (<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="time_periods_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>). The Specification window of the newly created Timeline opens automatically.</li><li>Type a name for the new Timeline, and click <strong>Close</strong>.</li><li>Select the newly created Timeline element.</li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button, and select <strong>Time Period</strong>. In this case, three periods (<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">2016 period, 2017 period, and 2018 period</span>) with their specific start and end dates were defined and displayed as columns in the SV-9 table.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The forecast is divided into three time periods and displayed as three columns in the the SV-9 table after specifying them in the Time Periods dialog. " ac:alt="The forecast is divided into three time periods and displayed as three columns in the the SV-9 table after specifying them in the Time Periods dialog. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="specified_periods_forecast_kind_in_sv-9_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Example of Specified Periods Forecast Kind in the Model</span></h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734642 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03128: (2026x) Formatting symbols

- page_id: `272734642`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734642/2026x+Formatting+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:11.996+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2038795777

2038795781

2038795780

| Definition | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Symbol | The term "symbol" means a visual representation of some model elements in the diagram. Symbols are subdivided into shapes and paths (lines in the model, for displaying various relationships). |
| Symbol properties | Every symbol may have its own style: color, font, size, and so forth. Symbol properties may be defined for the concrete symbol, for all symbol of one element, or according to the diagram type. |

You may define the symbol properties in the following ways:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog .
- In the Symbol tab on the Properties panel at the bottom of the Model Browser .
- In the Project Options dialog. Using this dialog you can change all available symbol properties, create your own style for the project, apply different symbol properties for different diagrams, define stereotype properties that may be bound to the symbol, and more.
- On the diagram toolbar. Using this toolbar you can change the color, font, path style of a symbol, and paste the symbol properties.

2038795776

**Related pages**

true****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737307 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03129: (2026x) Formatting template code

- page_id: `272737307`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737307/2026x+Formatting+template+code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:29.971+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Report Wizard template language
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports the **Tab** key to help you format template code at ease. The **Tab** key is useful to indent velocity code. You can press the **Tab** key after the symbol "#" or "$" followed by the velocity code. The **Tab** key can help you format the template code, but it does not affect the output of a generated report. See figure below for example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The velocity code in the figure above uses the **Tab** key to indent code. However, the generated report does not include those indents. The**Tab** key support is available in *RTF*, *DOCX*, and *ODT* format files.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard supports the <strong>Tab</strong> key to help you format template code at ease. The <strong>Tab</strong> key is useful to indent velocity code. You can press the <strong>Tab</strong> key after the symbol &quot;#&quot; or &quot;$&quot; followed by the velocity code. The <strong>Tab</strong> key can help you format the template code, but it does not affect the output of a generated report. See figure below for example.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Indented Template Code " ac:alt="Indented Template Code "><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 59.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Formatting template code" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The velocity code in the figure above uses the <strong>Tab</strong> key to indent code. However, the generated report does not include those indents. The<strong> Tab</strong> key support is available in <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX</em>, and <em>ODT</em> format files.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732637 space=MT version=10 -->
## PAGE 03130: (2026x) Forward compatibility

- page_id: `272732637`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732637/2026x+Forward+compatibility
- version_number: 10
- version_date: `2026-06-26T11:51:29.786+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The cross-version compatibility checking mechanism is triggered when a project with new feature usages is loaded with an older version of the modeling tool. The mechanism tracks feature usages of the 2026x Refresh1 and later Refresh versions, which results in the appearance of a **Missing features detected** notification in earlier modeling tool versions. The following table shows tracked features and the version they appear in. Projects can be committed/saved without any consequences.

- There are no 2026x Refresh1 tracked features for Magic Systems of Systems Architect, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Cameo Systems Modeler, Magic Software Architect, and MagicDraw.
- For SysML v2 2026x Refresh1 tracked features, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Forward compatibility' space='SYSML2P'] page.

**Compatibility of profiles**

While the tool is backward compatible, we highly recommend for every user working on a particular project to use the same version of the tool. For example, if you use an older version of the tool and open a project created with a later version, a balloon notification pops up:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To avoid this:

- Make sure that every user working on the same project uses the same version of the tool.
- Do not migrate profiles unless every user working on the same project is informed and ready for migration.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The cross-version compatibility checking mechanism is triggered when a project with new feature usages is loaded with an older version of the modeling tool. The mechanism tracks feature usages of the 2026x Refresh1 and later Refresh versions, which results in the appearance of a <strong>Missing features detected</strong> notification in earlier modeling tool versions. The following table shows tracked features and the version they appear in. Projects can be committed/saved without any consequences. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="482b4c36-0d0d-4d3b-977a-1eb26341d852"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="752298ee-926b-4ee4-afaf-0ddf666f041a">There are no 2026x Refresh1 tracked features for Magic Systems of Systems Architect, Cameo Enterprise Architecture, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Cameo Systems Modeler, Magic Software Architect, and MagicDraw.</li><li data-uuid="61e2d8e1-e68e-402b-8a8a-3fb5a9083e1b">For SysML v2 2026x Refresh1 tracked features, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSML2P" ri:content-title="(2026x) Forward compatibility" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br />Compatibility of profiles</strong></p><p>While the tool is backward compatible, we highly recommend for every user working on a particular project to use the same version of the tool. For example, if you use an older version of the tool and open a project created with a later version, a balloon notification pops up:</p><p><ac:image ac:height="200"><ri:attachment ri:filename="balloon_error.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To avoid this:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e7e89b3e-0a0d-472d-ba60-d50fce62428d">Make sure that every user working on the same project uses the same version of the tool.</li><li data-uuid="b84c81c5-e05d-49b5-b8e4-e8cb81862319">Do not migrate profiles unless every user working on the same project is informed and ready for migration.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735413 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03131: (2026x) Forward traceability - realization

- page_id: `272735413`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735413/2026x+Forward+traceability+-+realization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:21.958+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Traceability > (2026x) Predefined traceability rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The forward traceability ensures that all specified artifacts are covered by elements from the lower abstraction level.

| Property Name | Description | Applied for | Reference through... | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Manifested By Artifacts | The property shows artifacts that physically render the current component. | Component | Relationships: Manifestation | Artifact | Example |
| Realizing Component | The property shows the components representing the class realization in the Implementation model. | Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Component | Example |
| Realizing Classifier | The property shows classifiers that realize components through component realization. | Component | Relationships: Component Realization, Realization | Classifier | Example |
| Realizing Class | The property shows the classes representing the use case realization in the Design model. | Use Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Class | Example |
| Realizing Use Case | The property shows the realizing use cases of the current use case in the lower level of abstraction thus demonstrating how the use case is implemented. For example, the Requirements Use Case realizes the Business Use Case. | Use Case | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Realizing Classifier | The property shows the classifiers conforming to the contract specified by the interface and related with this interface through the interface realization relationship. | Interface | Relationships: Interface Realization | Classifier | Example |
| Realizing Element, All Realizing Elements.Available in Enterprise and Architect editions only. | The Realizing Element property gathers the source elements of the abstraction relationship.The All Realizing Elements property gathers recursively (performs a realizing element search for the result) source elements of the abstraction relationship. | Element | Relationships: Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization. | Element | Example |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729219 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03132: (2026x) FractionDigits

- page_id: `272729219`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729219/2026x+FractionDigits
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:34:28.063+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) XML schemas > (2026x) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDfractionDigits. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: fractionDigits XML representation summary

fractionDigits XML representation summary

```text
<fractionDigits
```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = nonNegativeInteger
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</fractionDigits>
```

#### PANEL: fractionDigits XML code sample

fractionDigits XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which could be used to represent the magnitude of a person's body temperature on the Celsius scale. This definition would appear in a schema authored by an end-user and shows how to define a datatype by specifying facet values which constrain the range of the base type.
```

```text
<simpleType name='celsiusBodyTemp'>
```

```text
<restriction base='decimal'>
```

```text
<totalDigits value='4'/>
```

```text
<fractionDigits value='1'/>
```

```text
<minInclusive value='36.4'/>
```

```text
<maxInclusive value='40.5'/>
```

```text
</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDfractionDigits. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;">value – to Attribute initial value.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e240d4b-ee6e-40da-8542-80976a19b276"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">fractionDigits XML representation summary</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;fractionDigits</pre><pre>	fixed = boolean : false</pre><pre>	id = ID</pre><pre>	value = nonNegativeInteger</pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}&gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?)</pre><pre>&lt;/fractionDigits&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1fc8b07-dd35-4015-995b-05ee11bcbb9d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">fractionDigits XML code sample</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which could be used to represent the magnitude of a person's body <br />temperature on the Celsius scale. This definition would appear in a schema authored by an end-user and shows how to define <br />a datatype by specifying facet values which constrain the range of the base type.<br /><br /></pre><pre>&lt;simpleType name='celsiusBodyTemp'&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;restriction base='decimal'&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;totalDigits value='4'/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;fractionDigits value='1'/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;minInclusive value='36.4'/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;maxInclusive value='40.5'/&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/restriction&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/simpleType&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272728993 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03135: (2026x) Functional analysis

- page_id: `272728993`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272728993/2026x+Functional+analysis
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:15.102+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Functional analysis is necessary for a user to understand in detail how a system works or should behave. Functional analysis is a continuation of Use Case scenario refinements, using diagrams describing the behavior of a system, e.g., SysML Activity, SysML Sequences, or SysML State Machine diagrams. A new SysML Activity diagram should be created for every function of a Use Case allocated to a particular block or part. Although there are two or even more Swimlane partitions, you should choose only those functions that are nested under the partition representing the Block which captures your system.

The following chapters describe each feature's functionality.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Functional analysis is necessary for a user to understand in detail how a system works or should behave. <span>Functional analysis is a continuation of Use Case scenario refinements, using diagrams describing the behavior of a system, e.g.,  <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e9ec4ca5-0256-4bda-ae4e-c09b1643a9c2">SysML Activity, SysML Sequences, or SysML State Machine diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker>. A new SysML Activity diagram should be created for every function of a Use Case allocated to a particular block or part. Alt<span>hough there are two or even more <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6012bc6c-658a-4ec4-9287-2455da85980f">Swimlane</ac:inline-comment-marker> partitions, you should choose only those functions that are nested under the partition representing the Block which captures <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="89bf5843-7dd2-4454-bd1b-d6039794885f">your system</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span>. </span></span></p><p><span>The following chapters describe each feature's functionality.</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="22934b67-10a8-40e3-b5c9-7c155401a4ec" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734316 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03136: (2026x) Functions of Specification window

- page_id: `272734316`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734316/2026x+Functions+of+Specification+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:45.568+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Specification window
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1291722198

1291722200

1291722199

Using Specification window you can perform various actions that are necessary when working with model elements. Every model element has its own specification.

The following table describes only common functions of the Specification window.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add/modify modelelement propertiesand inner elements | Choose the desired property group and specify its properties in the general specification pane. |
| Open referencedelementspecifications | From the shortcut menu, select Open Specification or click . When another element specification is open, you can work with both specifications in the same window. |
| Navigate betweenopen specifications | To navigate between open specifications, use the Back and Forward buttons. |
| Manage relationships | View all relationships in which the element participates. Modify the name of the relationship.View/change the direction of the relationship.Modify the target element.Create new outgoing or incoming relationships. |
| Add/edit elementdocumentation | Add element description, describe use case scenarios, or add other relevant information that can be used for generating reports or technical documentation. Documentation also can be written in HTML using the . |
| Manage elementhyperlinks | Open, edit, add, or remove from the selected model element to a file, address, other element / symbol, or requirements. |
| Manage element tags and tagged values | Create tag definitions, default tagged values. |
| Manage elementconstraints | to an element. |
| Track elementsymbol usage in diagrams | Find out in which diagrams the symbol is used. Open these diagrams directly from the specification window. |
| Select modelelement in theContainment tree | From the shortcut menu, choose Select in Containment Tree or click . |
| View element traceability | Monitor element changes and the impact of those changes using t. |

Select a specification property and from the shortcut menu, select the actionyou want to perform.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729179 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03137: (2026x) Gateways

- page_id: `272729179`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729179/2026x+Gateways
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:34:11.200+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Gateway allows you to control the flow of a process through a sequence flow. The term Gateway implies that there is a gating mechanism that either allows or disallows passage through the Gateway. Tokens that arrive at the gateway can be merged as inputs and/or split as outputs.

If the flow of a process does not need to be controlled, process does not need a gateway.

Types of Gateways are as follows:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Gateway allows you to control the flow of a process through a sequence flow. The term Gateway implies that there is a gating mechanism that either allows or disallows passage through the Gateway. Tokens that arrive at the gateway can be merged as inputs and/or split as outputs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35c3d694-56d8-480d-b108-857840b84e81"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the flow of a process does not need to be controlled, process does not need a gateway.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">Types of Gateways are as follows: </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ec3547c1-14ec-4061-bf1f-4aeac661b320" /></p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487850766

1487850766

1487850766

**Description**

A Generalization link shows that one class provides grouping criteria for sets of other classes. Generic class may define sets of properties, that are applicable to all the specific classes. The Generalization link can be among classes.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487850765

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="154ce8be-cd7b-4d74-ac17-b32b20fc0390"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487850766</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2754eedd-fd8b-4024-afd5-ddeb2cb87a0e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487850766</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e3032ec-58c7-4b0b-9549-41c96ef14d25"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487850766</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Generalization link shows that one class provides grouping criteria for sets of other classes. Generic class may define sets of properties, that are applicable to all the specific classes. The Generalization link can be among classes.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b5fe219-54c3-4059-8cd3-b9a20e0588fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1487850765</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Class">Class</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Business+Data+Diagram">Business Data Diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739350 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03140: (2026x) Generalization and specialization

- page_id: `272739350`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739350/2026x+Generalization+and+specialization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:18.950+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) Entity-relationship (ER) modeling and diagrams
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An ER diagram has a support for generalization / specialization modeling. Generalization and Specialization are really the same relationship, with a different direction of classification. Generalization is bottom-up, while specialization is top-down. Thus, they use the same model element.

Generalizations can be joined into generalization sets (trees of generalizations), which allow specifying additional properties on a group of generalizations - such as disjointness and completeness constraints.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of generalization in ER diagram.

Disjointness and completeness constraints are specified using the **Is Disjoint**(*true*for disjoint, *false*for overlapping specialization) and **Is Covering**(*true*for total, *false*for partial specialization) properties. They can be set in either the relationship shortcut menu or the Specification window.

There are 4 combinations of these two settings. The “breadloaf” symbol joining generalizations into a tree shows these 4 variations (See the following figures).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of overlapping and partial specialization in ER diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of overlapping and total specialization in ER diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of disjoint and partial specialization in ER diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of disjoint and total specialization in ER diagram.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

UML terminology (covering / not covering) is used for the completeness property name in the Specification window. Other names, more familiar for data modelers, are total / partial and complete / incomplete. These terms are analogous and can be used interchangeably.

In the specialization hierarchies, there can be several ways to assign an entity instance to a specific entity subtype. You can determine it when you decide to which subtype a given instance belongs (user-defined specialization). You can also determine it by actual data values of an entity instance (attribute-defined specialization). The latter case can be further subdivided into two subcases - simple attribute-based discrimination (when discrimination is performed by doing simple attribute value comparison) and more complex predicate-based discrimination (when discrimination is specified using more complex, explicitly specified conditions).

Examples of these two cases are shown in the following figures.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of attribute-based discriminator in ER diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a predicate-based discriminator in an ER diagram.

Discriminators are modeled as special constraints placed on individual generalization relationships. The easiest way to access them is from the shortcut menu of the generalization.

The predicate-based discriminator is simpler; just fill in the **Specification**field of the predicate with an appropriate expression text.

The attribute-based discriminator is more complex. First, specify the columns by which you will sort the entities into the corresponding subclasses. This is done by filling in the **Discriminator**field of the generalization set (you can specify one or several columns there). Then, fill in the **Template**field of the predicate. This template field holds an instance specification used as a template or etalon to differentiate the entity instances into appropriate subclasses. Fill in the slots for the same columns you indicated on the generalization set.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The category (also known as union) concept is currently not explicitly supported. Total (but not partial) categories can be “simulated” using the total specialization tree, just visually reversed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An ER diagram has a support for generalization / specialization modeling. Generalization and Specialization are really the same relationship, with a different direction of classification. Generalization is bottom-up, while specialization is top-down. Thus, they use the same model element.</p><p>Generalizations can be joined into generalization sets (trees of generalizations), which allow specifying additional properties on a group of generalizations - such as disjointness and completeness constraints.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 3 Generalization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of generalization in ER diagram.</h6><p>Disjointness and completeness constraints are specified using the <strong>Is Disjoint </strong>(<em>true </em>for disjoint, <em>false </em>for overlapping specialization) and <strong>Is Covering </strong>(<em>true </em>for total, <em>false </em>for partial specialization) properties. They can be set in either the relationship shortcut menu or the Specification window.</p><p>There are 4 combinations of these two settings. The “breadloaf” symbol joining generalizations into a tree shows these 4 variations (See the following figures).</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 4 Overlapping and Partial Specialization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of overlapping and partial specialization in ER diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 5 Overlapping and Total Specialization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of overlapping and total specialization in ER diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 6 Disjoint and Partial Specialization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of disjoint and partial specialization in ER diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 7 Disjoint and Total Specialization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of disjoint and total specialization in ER diagram.</h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b45fd844-fae4-459e-b0a9-d0e26c8f51dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>UML terminology (covering / not covering) is used for the completeness property name in the Specification window. Other names, more familiar for data modelers, are total / partial and complete / incomplete. These terms are analogous and can be used interchangeably.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the specialization hierarchies, there can be several ways to assign an entity instance to a specific entity subtype. You can determine it when you decide to which subtype a given instance belongs (user-defined specialization). You can also determine it by actual data values of an entity instance (attribute-defined specialization). The latter case can be further subdivided into two subcases - simple attribute-based discrimination (when discrimination is performed by doing simple attribute value comparison) and more complex predicate-based discrimination (when discrimination is specified using more complex, explicitly specified conditions).</p><p style="text-align: left;">Examples of these two cases are shown in the following figures.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 8 Attribute-based Discriminator in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of attribute-based discriminator in ER diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 9 Predicate-based Discriminator in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a predicate-based discriminator in an ER diagram.</h6><p>Discriminators are modeled as special constraints placed on individual generalization relationships. The easiest way to access them is from the shortcut menu of the generalization.</p><p>The predicate-based discriminator is simpler; just fill in the <strong>Specification </strong>field of the predicate with an appropriate expression text.</p><p>The attribute-based discriminator is more complex. First, specify the columns by which you will sort the entities into the corresponding subclasses. This is done by filling in the <strong>Discriminator </strong>field of the generalization set (you can specify one or several columns there). Then, fill in the <strong>Template </strong>field of the predicate. This template field holds an instance specification used as a template or etalon to differentiate the entity instances into appropriate subclasses. Fill in the slots for the same columns you indicated on the generalization set.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3aee853b-c72e-474b-b837-0e4f2ab6fede"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The category (also known as union) concept is currently not explicitly supported. Total (but not partial) categories can be “simulated” using the total specialization tree, just visually reversed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730821 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03141: (2026x) Generate Output pane

- page_id: `272730821`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730821/2026x+Generate+Output+pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:37:39.301+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Report Wizard dialog
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Generate Output** pane in Report Wizard allows you to configure report files by selecting the report files’ output location and image format, and displaying the report in the viewer.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Generate Output pane.

The table below describes the function of each option in the **Generate Output** pane of Report Wizard.

| Component Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Report file | Shows the report file’s location and name. The default report location will be \data\template_folder\reports\. The default report name will be the same as the report name defined by the user. |
|  | Opens the Select Location dialog in order to locate the report file. |
| Report image format | Selects an image format for your report: JPG, PNG, SVG, EMF, or WMF.Note:Use *.JPG and *.PNG for any template format.Use *.SVG for text, HTML, DOCX, and DocBooK templates.Use *.EMF and *.WMF for text and Microsoft Office templates (RTF, DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX). |
| Auto image size | Changes the size and orientation of an image before inserting it into a document. There are four options available:No Resize-Image will not be resized or rotated.Fit image to paper (large only)-Large image will be fit to within the paper size.Fit and rotate (clockwise) image to paper (large only)-Large image will be fit towithin the paper size and rotated clockwise.Fit and rotate (counter-clockwise) image to paper (large only)- Large image will be fit to within the paper size and rotated counter-clockwise. |
| Display empty value as | Empty text-Leave an empty value of the report output blank.Custom text as-Enter a custom value for an empty value. The default value is NA. |
|  | Displays a complete report in the viewer. Otherwise, the report will be created and kept in a selected location. |
|  | Generates a report. |
|  | Cancels the report generation process and closes the Report Wizard dialog. |
|  | Provides the Help content. |

Displaying an empty value with a text feature was deprecated. Or, you can select one of the following two options:

- Use a template code to replace empty value with text. For example: Using normal 
codeUsing macro 
code#if (!$var || $var == "")Empty#end#macro (replaceNullValueWithText $var)#if (!var || $var == "")EMPTY#end#end

- Use report wizard environment option to configure empty.

Add the property “template.output.on.blank.field” and value in the Report Wizard Environment option to enable this feature. The value of this property will be inserted in the generated output when the variable is empty (see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard Environment options' space='']*).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

There are four options available:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li><strong>No Resize-</strong>Image will not be resized or rotated.</li><li><strong>Fit image to paper (large only)-</strong>Large image will be fit to within the paper size.</li><li><strong>Fit and rotate (clockwise) image to paper (large only)-</strong>Large image will be fit to<br />within the paper size and rotated clockwise.</li><li><strong>Fit and rotate (counter-clockwise) image to paper (large only)-</strong> <br />Large image will be fit to within the paper size and rotated counter-clockwise.</li></ol></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Display empty value as</td><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Empty text-</strong>Leave an empty value of the report output blank.</li><li><strong>Custom text as-</strong>Enter a custom value for an empty value. The default value is <strong>NA</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayinviewer_option.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Displays a complete report in the viewer. Otherwise, the report will be created and kept in a selected location.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Generates a report.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Cancels the report generation process and closes the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Help_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1">Provides the Help content.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ae31dea-c121-4d00-b575-7d8b1cd6fe74"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Displaying an empty value with a text feature was deprecated. Or, you can select one of the following two options:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use a template code to replace empty value with text. For example:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Using normal <br class="_mce_tagged_br" />code</p></th><th><p>Using macro <br class="_mce_tagged_br" />code</p></th></tr><tr><td><pre class="_mce_tagged_br">#if (!$var || $var == &quot;&quot;)</pre><pre class="_mce_tagged_br"> Empty</pre><pre class="_mce_tagged_br">#end</pre></td><td><pre>#macro (replaceNullValueWithText $var)</pre><pre> #if (!var || $var == &quot;&quot;)</pre><pre> EMPTY</pre><pre> #end</pre><pre>#end</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li>Use report wizard environment option to configure empty.</li></ul><p>Add the property “template.output.on.blank.field” and value in the Report Wizard Environment option to enable this feature. The value of this property will be inserted in the generated output when the variable is empty (see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard Environment options" /></ac:link></em>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736101 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03142: (2026x) Generating a differences report

- page_id: `272736101`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736101/2026x+Generating+a+differences+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:39.225+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Model Merge > (2026x) Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['report', 'reporting', 'generating-report', 'report-templates', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1056652507

1056652509

1056652508

When you're done reviewing differences and wish to see them *on paper*, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.

To generate a differences report using a predefined template

1. Click the Report button in the toolbar that is located at the top of the Merge window. [ATTACHMENT filename='merge_toolbar_report.png']
2. Choose the preferred template – Full Report for a more detailed overview, including the entire Merged Result tree with all changes, or Diagrams Report . [ATTACHMENT filename='reports_shortcut_menu.png']
3. The Save Report dialog opens. Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click Save .

If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.

However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.

##### Creating custom report templates

All the .docx files located in *<configuration files directory>\data\merge reports* are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.

You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the **Report** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Template Creation Tutorial' space='']**Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial**.

1056652506

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Analyzing and managing merge results' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Configuration files' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.</p><p>However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.</p><h3>Creating custom report templates</h3><p>All the .docx files located in <em>&lt;configuration files directory&gt;\data\merge reports</em> are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.</p><p>You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the <strong>Report</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Creation Tutorial" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1953b4d9-a38b-49fc-b2f7-5323ba96c83d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1056652506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Analyzing and managing merge results" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configuration files" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
