<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739588 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03143: (2026x) Generating a project comparison differences report

- page_id: `272739588`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739588/2026x+Generating+a+project+comparison+differences+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:34.362+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Comparing projects > (2026x) Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['report', 'reporting', 'generating-report', 'report-templates', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you're done reviewing differences and wish to see them *on paper*, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.

To generate a differences report using a predefined template

1. Click the Report button in the Difference Viewer toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='difference_viewer_toolbar.png']
2. Choose the preferred template – Full Report for a more detailed overview, including the entire Differences tree with all changes, or Diagrams Report . [ATTACHMENT filename='reports_shortcut_menu.png']
3. The Save Report dialog opens. Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click Save .

If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.

However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.

The difference reports cannot be customized/extended with the model information using the usual Report Wizard helper tools.

##### Creating custom report templates

All the .docx files found in the Merge Reports directory are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the **Report** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Template Creation Tutorial' space='']**Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you're done <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6b3fcb5e-da83-4e54-aeba-d442a9f9fd5f">reviewing</ac:inline-comment-marker> differences and wish to see them <em>on paper</em>, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate a differences report using a predefined template</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Report </strong>button in the<strong> Difference Viewer</strong> toolbar. <br /><ac:image ac:height="55"><ri:attachment ri:filename="difference_viewer_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating a project comparison differences report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Choose the preferred template – <strong class="inline-comment-marker"> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7df30601-2a98-4df1-8211-0c279e157cad">Full Report</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> for a more detailed overview, including the entire <strong>Differences</strong> tree with all changes, or <strong>Diagrams Report</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Report shortcut menu" ac:alt="Report shortcut menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reports_shortcut_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating a project comparison differences report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>The <strong>Save Report</strong> dialog opens. Specify the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53902d52-44e4-4948-8f05-d0d42f82be77">location</ac:inline-comment-marker> in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you need to slightly modify the report, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="324c69e9-de1f-43ec-8efe-11d97dd22b80">for example,</ac:inline-comment-marker> to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.</p><p>However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="626a2b05-8cea-47db-8802-64511a6c44ee">customize</ac:inline-comment-marker> report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ecf6af8d-98f7-44ac-93ea-3c950744d202">scope</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="952e0da7-91d5-4333-8c14-ce12ca3b69bd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The difference reports cannot be customized/extended with the model information using the usual Report Wizard helper tools.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating custom report templates</h3><p class="inline-comment-marker">All the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3465320f-d47a-4a5f-9ed7-cfb98dcf9920">.docx</ac:inline-comment-marker> files found in the Merge Reports directory are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2a2129a8-5c36-4e55-b3b6-e628f7a13a83">then save it as your custom report template with a different name. </ac:inline-comment-marker>You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the <strong>Report</strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cde43cac-8ca1-45be-9b08-081268e85b1a">button</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>You can read more about <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b42a1619-fb27-4189-ae45-6bbc8d5716cd">creating</ac:inline-comment-marker> and modifying report templates in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Creation Tutorial" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738805 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03144: (2026x) Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server

- page_id: `272738805`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738805/2026x+Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:45.240+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Generating reports from the command line
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1300364630

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1300364632

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1300364631

INLINE

Apart from the mandatory commands described in [using the Generate command to generate reports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Using+the+Generate+command+to+generate+reports), you can use the following command arguments in the command line to generate a report from the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) server.

- -server , -login , and -password are key arguments to log on to TWCloud server. You will be prompted for a password when trying to enter -server and -login without a password.
- -pversion is a key argument to specify a TWCloud project version. If either you do not specify any version, or the version you specify does not match any version in the TWCloud repository, Report Wizard will generate the latest project version of the specified branch.
- -branch option is used to retrieve a particular project branch. If you do not specify any branch, the trunk would be specified.
- -updatemodule A Boolean parameter. True for updating the used module of the project to the latest version. False by default, when the user does not specify this option in the command.
- **-tag** is an option to specify a Teamwork project. You can specify the tagName to search for a project version. The returned project version may be more than one. You may use this command argument with "**-incremental true**". If you use this command with **-branch,**it will return a matching project version in the specified branch.
- **-projectpassword** is required to specify a password to open a password-protected Teamwork Cloud project. The password is required for the project to generate a report.
- **-usagepackage** specifies the name of one or more packages in Project Usages of a TWCloud project to be included in the report. Package entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).
- **-usageelement** specifies the name of one or more elements in the Project Usages of TWCloudproject to be included in the report. Element entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;). NoteFor TWCloud projects, **-package** / **-element** is used to select one or more packages/elements only in the Primary Model of TWCloud projects. To select packages/elements in Project Usages, specify **-usagepackage** / **-usageelement**. You can also set **-includeAuxiliary** as true if you want to make auxiliary resources available for selection. This **-usagepackage** / **-usageelement** argument can be used with **-package** / **-element** to select packages/elements in both Primary Model and Project Usages.

If, for example, you want to generate a Web Publisher 2.0 report from a TWCloud project by getting the latest project version of the trunk branch, type the following:

```text

```

The following code generates a Class Specification Report report from the TWCloud project by getting the project version number 10 from MyBranch branch of the MyProject project.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific project version by specifying tagName.

```text

```

The following code generates a project without specifying its branch.

```text

```

The following code generates a project from a branch release.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific version of a project without specifying its branch.

```text

```

The following code generates a specific version of a project from a branch release.

```text

```

The following code generates a set of reports from a specific tag.

```text

```

The following code generates a report through an SSL secured connection.

```text

```

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

The specified project version would be used to generate the report as a first priority, in case the specified project version does not belong in the specified branch.

#### WARNING: Note

Note

If the project version specified is not the latest, the **-updatemodule**option would not affect the **generate** command.

1300364629

INLINE

Element entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be995a6d-4ed9-42c1-884b-eb5c3f74649a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>For TWCloud projects, <strong>-package</strong> / <strong>-element</strong> is used to select one or more packages/elements only in the Primary Model of TWCloud projects. This <strong>-usagepackage</strong> / <strong>-usageelement</strong> argument can be used with <strong>-package</strong> / <strong>-element</strong> to select packages/elements in both Primary Model and Project Usages.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>If, for example, you want to generate a Web Publisher 2.0 report from a <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">TWCloud</span> project by getting the latest project version of the trunk branch, type the following:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53949471-b76f-485d-bfd2-cb06bc32f615"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -servertype "twcloud" -project "MyProject" -package "SamplePackage" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The following code generates a Class Specification Report report from the TWCloud project by getting the project version number 10 from MyBranch branch of the MyProject project.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5cc1b800-0b39-449e-bfe5-ba4d982c46cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -servertype "twcloud" -project "MyProject" -branch "MyBranch" -pversion "10" -package "SamplePackage" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific project version by specifying tagName.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2dfab75-3939-4e1a-a12b-b8c927f9024a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -branch "TestingLine" -tag "Finish" -incremental true -output "C:/output/out.docx" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "SamplePackage" -server "localhost" -servertype "twcloud" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a project without specifying its branch.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ee12cf15-5bf9-4ede-b971-8d33027950e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a project from a branch release.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ed35b12a-2ffc-4a6f-8a06-4c5ee2481ba6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject##release" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific version of a project without specifying its branch.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89f0dbc4-ad1d-4a8a-b341-47c56585c0bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -pversion "2" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a specific version of a project from a branch release.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecb64675-b957-4281-a946-f304f4ed54d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject##release" -pversion "2" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a set of reports from a specific tag.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3722217c-7af2-4045-a2b2-6285ef6362d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -tag "Finish" -incremental true -output "C:/output/output.docx" -template "Class Specification Report" -package "Model" -server "localhost" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The following code generates a report through an SSL secured connection.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="210bd89b-d369-4eee-9b3c-ca83ca4388f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[generate -project "MyProject" -package "Model" -template "Web Publisher 2.0" -output "D:\output\output.html" -server "localhost:1111" -login "Administrator" -password "Administrator" -ssl "true"]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f0749cc-3915-449e-a973-8cf4f323074c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The specified project version would be used to generate the report as a first priority, in case the specified project version does not belong in the specified branch.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff858fc3-8f37-4343-9afc-b16bacf07a7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If the project version specified is not the latest, the <strong>-updatemodule </strong>option would not affect the <strong>generate</strong> command.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5aaa2cc5-47bf-43a8-9869-6e1e7b233c49"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1300364629</ac:parameter><ac:parameter <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738711 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03145: (2026x) Generating an Actual Measurements table from a Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `272738711`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738711/2026x+Generating+an+Actual+Measurements+table+from+a+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:29.868+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Measurements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with Typical Measurements table' space='']Typical Measurements table can be instantiated by creating an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Actual Measurements table' space='']Actual Measurementstable.

To generate anActual Measurements table from a Typical measurements table

1. Create a Typical Measurements table with at least one measurable Property Set added.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. A new Actual Measurements table is created.
3. Add actual values for all Actual Measurements . The Actual Property Set is created and the rows representing its slots defined by Measurements are added to the Actual Measurements table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span class="confluence-link">A </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with Typical Measurements table" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Typical Measurements table</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> can be instantiated by creating <span class="confluence-link">an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Actual Measurements table" /><ac:link-body>Actual <span class="confluence-link">Measurements </span>table</ac:link-body></ac:link></span>.</p><p>To generate an<span class="confluence-link"> Actual Measurements table</span> from a <span class="confluence-link">Typical measurements table</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Typical Measurements table with at least one measurable<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Property Set </span>added.</li><li>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button. A new Actual Measurements table is created.</li><li class="confluence-link">Add <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">actual values for all Actual Measurements</span>.<br /><span>The Actual Property Set is created and the rows representing its slots defined by Measurements are added to the Actual Measurements table. </span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739359 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03146: (2026x) Generating Code

- page_id: `272739359`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739359/2026x+Generating+Code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:19.053+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Code engineering
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Demo**

- Code Generation

You can generate code for the selected and prepared set as well as directly for model elements.

##### Code Generation for a Set

Start code generation once the set or sets are prepared. For more details about creating and editing sets, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Code Engineering Sets' space=''].

- Choose **Generate** from the **Code Engineering Sets** item shortcut menu. It allows code generation for all created sets.
- Choose **Generate** from the selected set shortcut menu. It allows code generation only for the selected set.

The **Code Generation Options** dialog appears.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Code Generation Options dialog

The **Code Generation Options** dialog allows you to specify the way your code will be generated.

Once you specify generating options for the set, the code can be generated.

| Box Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Output Directory | Type the directory where the generated files will be saved. |
|  | The Set Output Directory dialog appears. Select the directory for the generated files. |
| Set as Working Directory | The output directory is set as a working directory and files are saved to the working directory. |
| Reverse before generation | Changes your model according to changes in the existing code.Exercise caution when selecting the Reverse before generation check box. If the model differs from the code, all differences in the model will be discarded. In these cases, you will lose some of your work. |
| If element deleted from model | To influence the structure of generated code, click one of the following option buttons:Delete code. The representation of deleted entities will be deleted from the code file.Comment code. Deleted entities will be commented in the code file. |
| Use spaces in place of tabs | When selected, spaces (instead of tabs) will be written to the code file. |
| Number of spaces | Specify the number of spaces to be written. |
| OK | The Notification Window appears, displaying how code files are being generated. The Notification Window informs you about problems and errors (mainly file access and syntax errors) found in the code generation process and generation summary. You are also prompted to confirm that you wish to overwrite the file if the output directory already contains one with the same name. |
| Cancel | Closes the dialog without saving changes. |
| Help | Displays Help |

##### Code Generation for a Model Element

All the classes contained in the component will be written into one file. However, code for the class can be generated in a different way. Select the class to generate in the browser Data package and click Generate in the class shortcut menu. For packages and components, you can also select **Generate**, but you will not be able to specify the generation options. All the options related to that task will be set according to the default values.

If you choose framework generation for a single class or for packages, the **Code Generation Options** dialog does not appear. The code is generated according to the default values.

If no errors occurred, you can view the results with your favorite file viewer or programming environment. Look for the files in the directory you specified as your **Working directory** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Code Engineering Sets' space='']**Round Trip Set** dialog or in the **Project Options** dialog. Additional sub-directories could be created.

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Code engineering' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Demo</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="d63cc319-25db-4e63-9445-fde54a8e8c0a"><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P-T1Kw78Yh0">Code Generation</a></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can generate code for the selected and prepared set as well as directly for model elements.</span></p><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Code Generation for a Set</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Start code generation once the set or sets are prepared. For more details about creating and editing sets, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Code Engineering Sets" /></ac:link>.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="dcf74b35-b1c0-4ace-8f6c-17185fde68d7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Choose <strong>Generate</strong> from the <strong>Code Engineering Sets</strong> item shortcut menu. It allows code generation for all created sets.</span></li><li data-uuid="3753e704-33e6-4321-a60e-b207d495a725"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Choose <strong>Generate</strong> from the selected set shortcut menu. It allows code generation only for the selected set.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The <strong>Code Generation Options</strong> dialog appears.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="code_generation_option.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Code" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Code Generation Options dialog</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The <strong>Code Generation Options</strong> dialog allows you to specify the way your code will be generated. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Once you specify generating options for the set, the code can be generated.</span></p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 12.5137%;" /><col class="" style="width: 87.4863%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Box Name</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Output Directory</strong></p></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Type the directory where the generated files will be saved.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="22"><ri:attachment ri:filename="three-dot-btn1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Code" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The <strong>Set Output Directory</strong> dialog appears. Select the directory for the generated files.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Set as Working Directory</strong></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The output directory is set as a working directory and files are saved to the working directory.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Reverse before generation</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Changes your model according to changes in the existing code.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df8010cd-1810-44f8-802b-11c000c53b82"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Exercise caution when selecting the <strong>Reverse before generation</strong> check box. If the model differs from the code, all differences in the model will be discarded. In these cases, you will lose some of your work.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>If element deleted from model</strong></td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To influence the structure of generated code, click one of the following option buttons:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="2cd11ef8-0348-4c1d-95ea-b2b46db13c84"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Delete code. The representation of deleted entities will be deleted from the code file.</span></li><li data-uuid="f6f64d92-b40a-4233-be45-68744fe9c86a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Comment code. Deleted entities will be commented in the code file.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Use spaces in place of tabs</strong></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When selected, spaces (instead of tabs) will be written to the code file.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Number of spaces</strong></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Specify the number of spaces to be written.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>OK</strong></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Notification Window appears, displaying how code files are being generated. ## PAGE 03147: (2026x) Generating DARS report
````

- page_id: `272739549`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739549/2026x+Generating+DARS+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:30.730+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['dars-template', 'generate-dars-report-in-av-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before generating an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) AV-1 Overview and Summary Information' space='']([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DoDAF 2.0' space='']), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Summary and Overview' space=''] ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UAF 1.3' space='']) DARS report, you must load the DARS template in these diagrams and provide the template with relevant data.

To load the DARS template

1. Select the Architecture Description from the Containment tree and open its Specification window.
2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='load_dars_template.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the DARS report. [ATTACHMENT filename='Architectural_description_specification_window.png']
4. Click **Close**.

To generate DARS report use standard [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating document based reports' space=''] procedure.

To load the DARS template in A1

2. Click the Load DARS Template button to load DARS template. [ATTACHMENT filename='DARS_template.png']
3. Fill in the extension property values for the A1 DARS report.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739938 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03149: (2026x) Generating document based reports

- page_id: `272739938`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739938/2026x+Generating+document+based+reports
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:15.444+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Managing reports
- labels: ['create-report', 'quick-generate-report', 'report-wizard', 'generate-document-based-reports', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216474442

216474445

216474441

**On this page**

216474444

The modeling tool report handling functionality allows you to store UAF report data in a UAF project. With this feature you can easily manage multiple reports with different properties and quickly print a report directly from the model. Another benefit from storing your report data within a project is that you can easily pass the report on to other users by passing the project. 
 UAFreports can be handled similarly as any other artifacts: diagrams, matrices, and tables. There is a report data element created by default in a project template for each UAF report. A report data element is filled in with predefined default data.

##### Creating a new report data element

You can create a new report data element from the shortcut menu of a viewpoint or view package. 
 
 To create a report data element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a package for which you want create a report data element .
2. Choose **Generate Report**, select a required report group and a report template. A new report data element that stores the default data for a particular report is created.

The newly created report data element is filled in with default data for successful generation of the report. To see variables and data of the report, expand the report element node in the Containment tree.

##### Changing the data stored within a variable

If you need to change data stored within a variable, open the variable's Specification window and then edit the **Value** property value. 
 
 You can store not only variables as data within a report data element, but the editable default report properties as well. The following table lists available editable default report properties and their descriptions.

| Property | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Name | To specify the name of the report data element. |
| Generate Recursively | Set to true to generate the report, recursively including all inner packages within a defined report scope. |
| Data | To select one or more packages and / or elements to define a scope for generating the report. |
| Auto Image Size | To specify an image size in the report. |
| Empty Text | To specify a value to be inserted when data contains no value. |
| Image Format | To select an image format for all the images in the report. |
| Template | To select a report template.A report template is not a part of a project. |
| Variables | To add, remove, and order report input variables. |

To edit a report property, open the report data element's Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Generating a report from the report data

You can generate a report directly from the report data.

To generate a report from the report data element

1. Right-click a report data element and click **Quick** **Generate** **Report**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Specify the location to save the generated report.
3. Select whether or not to open the generated report directly after the generation.

You can create several reports with different data for each type of the report.

##### Generating a report using the Report Wizard

You can also use the **Report** **Wizard** for report generation. Using this wizard, you can generate a report either from existing report data elements (after loading them to the wizard) or from external report data.

To open the **Report** **Wizard**

- Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard** and select the report templates for the UAF views.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 03150: (2026x) Generating report documents

- page_id: `272732238`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732238/2026x+Generating+report+documents

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can generate the report documents from the models. This allows you to provide a specific information from the system model to stakeholders and domain engineers.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can generate the report documents from the models. This allows you to provide a specific information from the system model to stakeholders and domain engineers.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4f45ba8b-49c4-47c3-8396-df645740850e" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735282 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03151: (2026x) Generating Reports from Report Wizard

- page_id: `272735282`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735282/2026x+Generating+Reports+from+Report+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:40.019+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1261051786

1261051787

1261051787

In addition to default document templates, Report Wizard allows you to create customized specification document templates. Templates may contain your own custom fields not related to model elements, as well as fields that correspond to model elements. Once customized, a Report Wizard template can be used with data from any project.

You can also format your template to create the style you want, including tables of contents, headers and footers, and page numbers. You can apply most character and paragraph formatting available from your rtf editor to the rtf template or specify with html tags in the HTML template, including keywords. Once the template has been completed, you can run the Report Wizard to create a report in a format that corresponds to the template.

Report Wizard templates can be in *txt, rtf, html, odt, odf, odp, docx, xlsx, pptx*, and *xml* for DocBook or FO files. If you prefer, you can work in the Report Wizard Template Editor until you have finished the template file and use the Report Wizard template windows to save it in the template folder. The default templates are located in the *<modeling tool home>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard/data* folder.

All built-in rtf report templates are combined into the RTF (deprecated) report template category.

##### Synchronizing an attached template with a server project

You can [attach a local template](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Template+Management+Pane) to a server project. Then any user who opens this server project can generate a report with the attached template.

Each time the attached template is updated and changes are committed to the server project, the changes will affect everyone using the attached template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To update the locally attached template from a server project

1. Go to **Options > Environment** menu.

2. Select the **Report Wizard** pane.

3. In the Configure report properties table, set the **synchronizeAttachedTemplate** value to **true.**

This way, the locally attached template will be synchronized with the latest attached template of the server project each time a report is generated from the attached template.

To update the server project with a locally attached template

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='CM'].

2. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog.

3. Select the attached template and click **Open**.

4. Edit the attached template and click **Save**.

5. Click **Synchronize**.

6. Commit changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1261051785

**Related pages**

Then any user who opens this server project can generate a report with the attached template.</p><p>Each time the attached template is updated and changes are committed to the server project, the changes will affect everyone using the attached template.</p><p> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2022-2-28_9-0-27.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To update the locally attached template from a server project</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. Go to <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Options &gt; Environment</strong> menu.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. Select the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> pane.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">3. In the Configure report properties table, set the <strong>synchronizeAttachedTemplate</strong> value to <strong>true.</strong></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This way, the locally attached template will be synchronized with the latest attached template of the server project each time a report is generated from the attached template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To update the server project with a locally attached template</p><hr /><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">1. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock an attached template for editing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">2. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735255 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03153: (2026x) Generating reports from the Containment tree

- page_id: `272735255`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735255/2026x+Generating+reports+from+the+Containment+tree
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:09.742+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After creating a Report Data in your model, you can generate a report directly from the Containment tree. You can right-click either Model or Report Data to generate it.

- .
- .

You must specify the template and data in the report data to generate a report from the **Report Data** in the Containment tree.

###### GenerateFromModel

To generate a report from Model in the Containment tree

1. Right-click a package in the Containment tree, select Generate Report , and then select a template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Generating a report from Model in the Containment tree.

2. Select the save location and type the filename. A dialog will open asking if you want to open the report in the default viewer after the report generating process is complete.

3. Click either [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can specify the diagram export options, such as dimensions and building time (the latter applies only to tables and matrices), in the **Project Options** dialog. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram image export' space=''] page to learn more.

###### GenerateFromReportData

To generate a report from Report Data in the Containment tree

1. Create a project.
2. Click File > Use Project > Use Local Project . The Use Project dialog will open.
3. Select the predefined path: <install.root>\profiles** and the predefined profile: Report Profile
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Finish_button.png'] . The Report Profile will be added to your project in read-only mode.
5. Right-click Model and select Create Element .
6. Select **Report Data**. **Report Data** will be created under Model. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating report data in the Model in the Containment tree.
7. Right-click the Report Data in the Containment tree, and select Specification to open its Specification dialog.
8. Select a template file, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a template file for the Report Data using the Specification dialog.
9. Right-click the Class element in the Containment tree, and select **Quick Generate Report**. The **Select Location** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Generating a report from Report Data in the Containment tree.
10. Either create a new file, or select an existing one (the content of the generated report will override that of the selected file).

- To open the Report Wizard dialog and modify the data or output properties, click the Report Wizard on the shortcut menu.
- By default, the Quick Generate Report command will select the default settings from the last changes saved to the Report Data .

#### Glossary

- Model The package that is placed at the highest level of the Containment tree.

- Report data A collection of variables in a report in Report Wizard.

- Report template A file that serves as a starting point for a new report.

#### More information about generating reports in Report Wizard

To learn more about generating reports using Report Wizard, you can visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space=''].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272728931 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03154: (2026x) Generating requirement reports

- page_id: `272728931`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272728931/2026x+Generating+requirement+reports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:32.591+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can generate Requirement reports in the .docx and .html/.htm formats directly from your model.

##### Generating the Word document report

#### INFO: Report customization

Report customization

If you want to customize the report or create your own template for a requirement report, you may use the **[Report Wizard](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview)**.

****

To create a .docx format report by using the **Report Wizard**

1. In the Tools menu, click Report Wizard .
2. In the Select Template area, select **Requirements**, and choose one of the following report type: 
-**Requirement Dependencies Report**. This report contains tables of requirement dependencies, that is separate tables for Copy, Derive, Refine, Satisfy, Trace, and Verify relations.-**Requirement Diagram**. This report lists all requirement diagrams of a selected scope. Diagram elements and dependencies are described in tables under each diagram.-**Requirement Report**. This is a standard detailed requirement report, containing all requirements attributes. This template may be used as a requirement specification report.-**Requirements Table Diagram Report**. This report lists all requirement tables of a selected scope.
3. Click**Next**.
4. In the Select Report Data area, you can then select a predefined report data for the selected template (default = Built-in). Click Next .
5. In the Select Element Scope area use:
  - The Add button to add an element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add All button to add all elements directly owned by the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add Recursively button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove button in to remove the selected element from the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove All button in to remove all selected elements from the Selected objects area.
6. Click Next .
7. In the Output Options area, define the appropriate options.
8. Click Generate . Your report is generated and automatically opens.

###### Generating requirement report from the Containment tree

To generate Requirement reports from the Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the selected requirement specification (Package) or Requirement.
2. From the shortcut menu, click Generate Report > Requirements and select a desired requirement template.
3. In the Select Location dialog, specify a report location and specify a report file name.
4. Click Save . The report is generated.

##### Publishing projects to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud

Publishing models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloudallows you to present them in a simplified form for users who do not know modeling languages, such as UML or SysML v1.Publishing is the beginning step of the model review workflow, as displayed in the following figure:

#### NOTE: Prerequisites

Prerequisites

To publish documents to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you must [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin' space='MCS'] in your modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Publishing a model is the first step in the workflow for reviewing models and providing feedback.

You can republish a model each time you update it whilereacting to the reviewer's feedback. When republishing the model, you can either create a new document with a different name or update the one already stored in Teamwork Cloud. The updated document includes all the comments from the previous version unless the comments are disabled when republishing.

To learn more, see the following topics:

#### NOTE: Publishing documents from synchronized projects

Publishing documents from synchronized projects

If you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the project must already have the Cameo Collaborator profile used in it. In addition, the commenting and model editing capabilities in such a document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cross-cluster resource synchronization' space='MCS'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can generate Requirement reports in the .docx and .html/.htm formats directly from your model.</p><h3>Generating the Word document report</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91ef625e-776a-440b-91b3-83a82eaf0d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Report customization</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to customize the report or create your own template for a requirement report, you may use the <strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview">Report Wizard</a></strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong> </strong></p><p>To create a .docx format report by using the <strong>Report Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="966bebda-385e-4a0e-b813-718d6de9b27b">In the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, click <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="c54aa60e-7986-49e2-9315-06a53a9f55be"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Select Template area, select <strong>Requirements</strong>, and choose one of the following report type:<br /><span>- </span><strong>Requirement Dependencies Report</strong><span>. This report lists all requirement diagrams of a selected scope. Diagram elements and dependencies are described in tables under each diagram. <br /></span><span>- </span><strong>Requirement Report</strong><span>. This is a standard detailed requirement report, containing all requirements attributes. This template may be used as a requirement specification report.<br /></span><span>- </span><strong>Requirements Table Diagram Report</strong><span>. element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Add Recursively</strong> button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Remove</strong> button in to remove the selected element from the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Remove All</strong> button in to remove all selected elements from the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="d86a2d2a-a06f-41c8-a453-dfd7220319ee">Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="df5d9150-5676-4ee8-aff7-5fb765fdbfbe">In the <strong>Output Options</strong> area, define the appropriate options.</li><li data-uuid="d7cbb87b-707f-47b8-837c-99dc9bbb083e">Click<strong> Generate</strong>.<br />Your report is generated and automatically opens.</li></ol><h4>Generating requirement report <span>from the Containment tree</span></h4><p><br /></p><p>To generate Requirement reports from the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="468243ce-73c5-43f9-bf68-2708d9e93aed">In the Containment tree, right-click the selected requirement specification (Package) or Requirement.</li><li data-uuid="3bbda056-3134-4901-896e-9094aa5e27df">From the shortcut menu, click <strong>Generate Report</strong> &gt; <strong>Requirements</strong> and select a desired requirement template.</li><li data-uuid="0d62e5fd-6db6-412d-bff8-6accf34e477b">In the <strong>Select Location</strong> dialog, specify a report location and specify a report file name.</li><li data-uuid="94406b3c-8384-40a6-a68c-56a27a459893">Click <strong>Save</strong>.<br />The report is generated.</li></ol><h3>Publishing projects to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Publishing models to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">allows you to present them in a simplified form for users who do not know modeling languages, such as UML or SysML v1. </span>Publishing is the beginning step of the model review workflow, as displayed in the following figure:<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd518cb5-3be9-4ff7-9fc3-911d561a6cfb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Prerequisites</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To publish documents to Cameo Collaborator for Teamwork Cloud, you must <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Installing Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin" ri:space-key="MCS" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[install the Cameo Collaborator Publisher plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in your modeling tool.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="main_workflow_publishing.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="line-height: 1.66667;text-align: center;">Publishing a model is the first step in the workflow for reviewing models and providing feedback.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can republish a model each time you update it while </span>reacting to the reviewer's feedback<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. When republishing the model, you can either create a new document with a different name or update the one already stored in Teamwork Cloud. The updated document includes all the comments from the previous version unless the comments are disabled when republishing.<br /></span></p><p>To learn more, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1af390c3-7a92-4d14-bb6a-49fc02d6d986" /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f646a71d-6e9b-4f95-a76f-ab6b5b220655"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Publishing documents from synchronized projects</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to publish a Cameo Collaborator document from a synchronized project on the target Teamwork Cloud server, the project must already have the Cameo Collaborator profile used in it. In addition, the commenting and model editing capabilities in such a document will be disabled so that the synchronized project on the target server cannot be modified. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Cross-cluster resource synchronization" ri:space-key="MCS" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about cross-cluster resource synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732249 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03155: (2026x) Generating simulation files

- page_id: `272732249`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732249/2026x+Generating+simulation+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:23.289+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Sample models > (2026x) Creating a simple model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a simulation file, right-click the *Block* corresponding the system being simulated (see the image below). The shortcut menu displays the menu item **Tools******that contains**Export to Modelica** and **Export to Simulink**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To export to Modelica and Simulink

1. Depending on the selected option (either Export to Modelica or Export to Simulink ), do one of the following:
  - Specify options such as output directory, how to export unannotated SysML v1 properties, and whether diagram layout annotations should be generated on export. [ATTACHMENT filename='modelica_export_options_.png']
  - Specify options such as format, output directory, S-Function or Simscape, Simscape port libraries, and whether to apply the <<SimulinkBlock>> stereotype. [ATTACHMENT filename='simulink_export_new_dialog (1).png']
2. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To generate a simulation file, right-click the <em>Block</em> corresponding the system being simulated (see the image below). <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2387eaaa-4a8e-4557-b468-104bdfdf01b6">The shortcut menu displays the menu item <strong>Tools</strong></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2387eaaa-4a8e-4557-b468-104bdfdf01b6"><strong> </strong>that contains<strong> Export to Modelica</strong> and <strong>Export to Simulink</strong>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Export_To_Simulink_And_Modelica.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating simulation files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To export to Modelica and Simulink</p><hr /><ol><li>Depending on the selected option (either <strong>Export to Modelica</strong> or <strong>Export to Simulink</strong>), do one of the following:<ul><li>Specify options such as output directory, how to export unannotated SysML v1 properties, and whether diagram layout annotations should be generated on export.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelica_export_options_.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating simulation files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Specify options such as format, output directory, S-Function or Simscape, Simscape port libraries, and whether to apply the &lt;&lt;SimulinkBlock&gt;&gt; stereotype. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulink_export_new_dialog (1).png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating simulation files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737750 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03156: (2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports

- page_id: `272737750`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737750/2026x+Generating+Use+Case+Description+Reports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:41.181+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### Use Case Report Template

Report Wizard provides a Use Case description document template that is designed to capture all Use Case-related artifacts, e.g. Use Cases, actors, Use Case descriptions, in a report. It is used to describe systems functionality and actors in simple format. This template includes all of the Use Case diagram-related keywords. When you select the highest level of the packages (in the modeling tool, Model is the highest level of the packages) as the scope of the report, only Use Case diagrams and their related elements will be added to the document.

To generate a Use Case description document

1. Open the *Magic Library.mdzip* sample model from the directory. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] This step is necessary for illustration purposes, as well as other steps within this scenario.
2. Click Tools > Report Wizard to open the Report Wizard dialog.
3. Select a template, for example, **Default Template** > **Use Case (Simple),** and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. You can choose to: You can create a new report variable, and modify or delete an existing one through the **Report Variable** dialog.
  1. Select the built-in Report Data. You can modify or delete some variables of the Built-in Report Data in the Report Variable dialog by clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='Variable_button.png'] or
  2. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new one. Once you have created a new Report Data, for example, MyReportData, you will see it in the Select Report Data pane. You can select it and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create its report variables. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the Built-in report data. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting a custom report data . [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Report Variable dialog.
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Next_button.png'] once you have completed modifying or creating the report variables. The Select Element Scope option will open in the Report Wizard dialog.
6. Select the scope of the report from the package tree on the left-hand side. In this case, it will be the MagicLibrary Requirements[MagicLibraryRequirements.mdzip] model package, as all Use Case requirements are stored in this package.
7. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The model package will be moved to the **Selected objects** tree on the right-hand side. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The output options pane will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting the location to generate the report, the image format and size, and other output options before generating the report.
9. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Browse_bigbutton.png'] to specify the location in which you want to save the report.
10. Select the file location, enter the filename, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The report file format varies depending on the template file format. For example, if the template file format is **.rtf*, the report file format will be **.rtf* as well.
11. Select the report image format, **.png*, **.jpg*, **.emf*, or**.wmf,*and select an image size option. TipIn some cases, a query may return an empty value that causes blank fields in the report. The **Display empty value as** option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.
12. Select either the **Empty text** or **Custom text** option to display empty value information. If you select the latter, type the information you want to display for every empty value.
13. Select [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to open the report document with a default editor. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
14. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Generate_button.png'] .

The Use Case template is one of the default templates from which you can use and select in the Report Wizard dialog. You can see all of the default templates on this page [Default templates](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Default+templates).

#### Glossary

- Use case A Use Case is a list of actions or event steps of interactions between a system and the users to achieve certain goals.

- Model Model is the highest-level package in the Containment tree.

- Report data Report Data is a collection of report variables that can be included in a report.

#### Sample model

[ATTACHMENT filename='Magic Library.mdzip']

#### More Information on Generating Reports

You can generate different types of reports with the Report Wizard. To learn more, see [Web Publisher 2.0 Report](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Web+Publisher+2.0+Report).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

