It is used to describe systems functionality and actors in simple format. This template includes all of the Use Case diagram-related keywords. When you select the highest level of the packages (in the modeling tool, Model is the highest level of the packages) as the scope of the report, only Use Case diagrams and their related elements will be added to the document.</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To generate a Use Case description document</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="ee750f38-d6c6-4241-9399-8afb53927165"><p>Open the <em>Magic Library.mdzip</em> sample model from the directory.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="magic-lib.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cafde055-4be2-48bd-9c97-db483f26c062"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This step is necessary for illustration purposes, as well as other steps within this scenario.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="443bc5d5-b6c8-4c7a-a072-844b79dc9673">Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard </strong>to open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</li><li data-uuid="79643997-d041-4951-8fdc-a2bad028dab7"><p>Select a template, for example, <strong>Default Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Case (Simple),</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UseCase_template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li data-uuid="e75efa34-51d3-43ea-833a-09b4e7b3d358">You can choose to:<br /><ol><li>Select the <strong>built-in</strong> Report Data. You can modify or delete some variables of the <strong>Built-in</strong> Report Data in the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog by clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />or</li><li><p>Click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create a new one. Once you have created a new Report Data, for example, MyReportData, you will see it in the Select Report Data pane. You can select it and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to create its report variables.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting the Built-in Report Data" ac:alt="Selecting the Built-in Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="report-wizard-dialog-two.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting the Built-in report data.</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting a Custom Report Data" ac:alt="Selecting a Custom Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rep-wiz-step-two.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting a custom report data .</h6><p><ac:image ac:title="The Report Variable Dialog" ac:alt="The Report Variable Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage-report-variables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Report Variable dialog.</h6></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3e62168-5bb4-403d-81cb-0df77361b8c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create a new report variable, and modify or delete an existing one through the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="9fb4e513-bd26-4e88-99b4-2cc85e5af23d">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> once you have completed modifying or creating the report variables. The <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> option will open in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</li><li data-uuid="3d106159-108c-449a-b187-640db8b4b747">Select the scope of the report from the package tree on the left-hand side. In this case, it will be the <em>MagicLibrary Requirements[MagicLibraryRequirements.mdzip]</em> model package, as all Use Case requirements are stored in this package.</li><li data-uuid="20716567-14cd-42b6-8ce8-7d3bd199ef7b"><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The model package will be moved to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> tree on the right-hand side.</p><h6><ac:image ac:title="Selecting the Scope of the Report" ac:alt="Selecting the Scope of the Report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select-element-scope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6></li><li data-uuid="ea66f5c3-77fb-46ef-abd9-9b233e1a7c7a"><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The output options pane will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLibrary_test1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Selecting the location to generate the report, the image format and size, and other output options before generating the report.<br /><br /></h6></li><li data-uuid="f5456283-b2d6-44b4-bfec-41e78cf40a71">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to specify the location in which you want to save the report.</li><li data-uuid="57781d70-1e5f-4549-b0ef-2d7c4a58d554"><p>Select the file location, enter the filename, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The report file format varies depending on the template file format. For example, if the template file format is <em>*.rtf</em>, the report file format will be <em>*.rtf</em> as well.</p></li><li data-uuid="d7f57bf1-f831-4000-a983-ffb4725bf5c7"><p>Select the report image format, <em>*.png</em>, <em>*.jpg</em>, <em>*.emf</em>, or<em> *.wmf, </em>and select an image size option.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78c70840-9f0c-4cda-8798-2fde5f502217"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In some cases, a query may return an empty value that causes blank fields in the report. The <strong>Display empty value as</strong> option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="a7695818-3ef7-4b28-961f-f9a0980d2240"><p>Select either the <strong>Empty text</strong> or <strong>Custom text</strong> option to display empty value information. If you select the latter, type the information you want to display for every empty value.</p></li><li data-uuid="5111774a-77ef-467a-a1eb-113c69538bd7"><p>Select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayinviewer_option.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open the report document with a default editor.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="MagicLibrary_test1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li data-uuid="61acf04c-7442-4889-b7fe-37b1bc374aa0">Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p>The Use Case template is one of the default templates from which you can use and select in the Report Wizard dialog. You can see all of the default templates on this page <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Default+templates">Default templates</a>.</p><h2><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Glossary</span></h2><ul><li data-uuid="ebec7bd6-2ff7-45cf-b20b-d038b1eb42d6"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Use case</span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><br />A Use Case is a list of actions or event steps of interactions between a system and the users to achieve certain goals.<br /></span></li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="7a9bfa41-a167-432e-b395-3423e009a28f"><strong>Model</strong><br />Model is the highest-level package in the Containment tree.</li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="9a230b0e-c270-440d-9361-95f06f647985"><strong>Report data</strong><br />Report Data is a collection of report variables that can be included in a report.</li></ul><h2>Sample model</h2><p> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Magic Library.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Use Case Description Reports" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></p><hr /><h2>More Information on Generating Reports</h2><p>You can generate different types of reports with the Report Wizard. To learn more, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Web+Publisher+2.0+Report">Web Publisher 2.0 Report</a>.</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732240 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03157: (2026x) Generating Word document report

- page_id: `272732240`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732240/2026x+Generating+Word+document+report
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:46.697+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Generating report documents
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a .docx format report

1. In the Tools menu, click Report Wizard .
2. In the Select Template area, select SysML, and choose the report type. Click Next .
3. In the Select Report Data area, you can then select a predefined report data for the selected template (default = Built-in). Click Next .
4. In the Select Element Scope area use:
  - The Add button to add an element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add All button to add all elements directly owned by the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add Recursively button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove button in to remove the selected element from the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove All button in to remove all selected elements from the Selected objects area.
5. Click Next .
6. In the Output Options area, define the appropriate options.
7. Click Generate . Your report is generated and automatically opens. See the example: [ATTACHMENT filename='coverage_analysis.docx'] .

#### INFO: Report customization

Report customization

If you want to customize your report, use the **[Report Wizard](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview)**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><br /></p><p>To create a .docx format report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, click <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</li><li>In the Select Template area, select SysML, and choose the report type. Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Report Data</strong> area, you can then select a predefined report data for the selected template (default = Built-in). Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Element Scope</strong> area use:<br /><ul><li>The <strong>Add</strong> button to add an element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Add All</strong> button to add all elements directly owned by the element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Add Recursively</strong> button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Remove</strong> button in to remove the selected element from the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li><li>The <strong>Remove All</strong> button in to remove all selected elements from the <strong>Selected objects</strong> area.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Output Options</strong> area, define the appropriate options.</li><li>Click<strong>Generate</strong>.<br />Your report is generated and automatically opens. See the example: <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="coverage_analysis.docx"><ri:page ri:content-title="Predefined templates in Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="91ef625e-776a-440b-91b3-83a82eaf0d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Report customization</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to customize your report, use the <strong><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview">Report Wizard</a></strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734817 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03158: (2026x) Generic numbering mechanism

- page_id: `272734817`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734817/2026x+Generic+numbering+mechanism
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:30.701+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements
- labels: ['numbering', 'numbering-scheme', 'numbering-customization', 'numbering-property', 'element-numbering', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Our modeling tools provide a generic numbering mechanism that can be applied to any elements created in the modeling tool. The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:

- Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.
- Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.
- Show an element's place in a hierarchy.

With this feature you can easily:

- Customize currently applied numbering formats.
- Create and apply your own numbering formats.
- Change the numbering format while creating numbered elements.

The Generic numbering mechanism feature description is organized in two sections:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with generic numbering mechanism' space=''] describes how to number elements, apply numbering formats, and modify numbers.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element Numbering dialog' space=''] explains GUI elements, used while numbering elements.

Before starting to describe the generic numbering mechanism, get acquainted with used concepts:

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Numbering customization | A customization class which specifies the element types that will be numbered, the numbering format that will be used to number elements, and the property to which the created number will be assigned. The numbering format should be defined prior to numbering elements. Usually the system administrator . |
| Numbering property | Indicates an element property where the element number will be stored and defines a numbering scheme that will be used for the element numbering. |
| Numbering scheme | A set of rules describing which numbering formats will be applied on the concrete elements. The Numbering scheme is defined while creating the numbering customization. |
| Prefix | An affix placed before the element number. |
| Separator | A symbol used to separate the different level numbers. The Separator is defined in the numbering scheme. |

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65425aa5-3f20-454b-b736-4c6f49dcbaf0"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Our modeling tools provide a generic numbering mechanism that can be applied to any elements created in the modeling tool. The generic numbering mechanism allows you to:</p><ul><li>Automatically assign a unique number to the elements in the model when they are created, moved, or your model is refactored.</li><li>Easily identify and find an element in the model when communicating with colleagues or stakeholders.</li><li>Show an element's place in a hierarchy.</li></ul><p>With this feature you can easily:</p><ul><li>Customize currently applied numbering formats.</li><li>Create and apply your own numbering formats.</li><li>Change the numbering format while creating numbered elements.</li></ul><p>The Generic numbering mechanism feature description is organized in two sections:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Working with generic numbering mechanism" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with Generic numbering mechanism]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> describes how to number elements, apply numbering formats, and modify numbers. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Element Numbering dialog" /></ac:link></span> explains GUI elements, used while numbering elements.</li></ul><p>Before starting to describe the generic numbering mechanism, get acquainted with used concepts:</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="row">Concept</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><th scope="row"><p>Numbering customization</p></th><td><p>A customization class which specifies the element types that will be numbered, the numbering format that will be used to number elements, and the property to which the created number will be assigned. The numbering format should be defined prior to numbering elements. Usually the system administrator <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Numbering Customizations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creates the numbering customization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><th scope="row"><p>Numbering property</p></th><td><p>Indicates an element property where the element number will be stored and defines a numbering scheme that will be used for the element numbering.</p></td></tr><tr><th scope="row"><p>Numbering scheme</p></th><td><p>A set of rules describing which numbering formats will be applied on the concrete elements. The Numbering scheme is defined while creating the numbering customization.</p></td></tr><tr><th scope="row"><p>Prefix</p></th><td>An affix placed before the element number.</td></tr><tr><th scope="row"><p>Separator</p></th><td><p>A symbol used to separate the different level numbers. The Separator is defined in the numbering scheme.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="a45e48f4-242a-47e7-a18c-67ba18058c6c" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 03159: (2026x) Generic table

- page_id: `272733713`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733713/2026x+Generic+table

- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1198506714

1198506717

1198506711

**On this page**

42

1198506716

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Generic Table gives you the ability to manage selected elements and their properties in a single place.

##### **Purpose**

A Generic Table displays a list of selected elements and their properties in a single place. Using Generic Tables enables you to:

- Edit property values of listed model elements.
- Create selected type elements.
- Create new derived properties or custom columns.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Generic Table project sample allows you to read about the Generic Table feature and analyze examples within that project sample. 
To open the sample, do either:

- On the Welcome screen, select Samples > Diagrams > Generic table .
- Go to < the modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams and open the generic table.mdzip file.

All the examples given in this section are based on the data from this sample.

##### **Specific tasks in Generic Table**

All procedures dedicated only for Generic Table are as follows:

1199743192

## PAGE 03160: (2026x) Generic table tool

- page_id: `272735385`
- space_key: `MT`

- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352031231

1352031232

1352031232

The Generic Table tool allows templates to:

- Access data of the Generic Table and other Table diagrams, e.g., Instance Table, Metric Table, and Glossary Table. See more samples in the tutorial.
- Use diagrams to get data from the Generic Table.
- Get the row elements.
- Get the column names.
- Get the cell values.
- Get column levels for the Generic Table tree view.
- Check for annotation values in table cells.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Generic Table Dialog.

Like other Custom Tools, the Generic Table tool '*generictabletool.jar*' must be presented in the 'extensions' folder of the Report Wizard plugin. For further information on Custom Tools and the installation, see section *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Custom tool' space=''].*

To import the Generic Table tool to a template, for example, type the following code in the template.

```text

```

## PAGE 03161: (2026x) Generic table tool API

- page_id: `272738860`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738860/2026x+Generic+table+tool+API
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:43.389+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Generic table tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### GenericTableTool diagram.

Class GenericTableTool:

- +getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String

#### Getting generic table data

A Generic Table is a special diagram. You can retrieve it by using the variable $Diagram. Thus, every method must accept the diagram instance or diagram name.

Use the following method to get the Generic Table instance. We use Generic Table instances to retrieve table information such as row elements and column names.

##### Getting generic table instances from diagram elements

To get a Generic Table instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.

**getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – the diagram element.

Return the instance of a Generic Table.

Example code:

```text

```

##### Getting generic table instances from diagram names

To get a Generic Table instance from a specific diagram name, use the following code.

**getTable( diagramName : String) : Table**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – the diagram name.

Return the instance of a Generic Table.

Example code:

```text

```

#### Closing tables

Use the following method to close a table diagram.

##### Closing all diagram tables

**CloseTables() : Void**

```text

```

##### Closing a specific table diagram

**closeTable(diagram: Diagram) : Void**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagram – a table diagram you want to close.

##### Closing a table diagram with a specific diagram name

**closeTable(diagramName: String) : Void**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- diagramName – the name of a table diagram you want to close.

#### Getting row elements

The Generic Table consists of a series of row elements and column names. Use the following methods to retrieve a list of row elements.

##### Getting all row elements

**getRows() : List<Element>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names.

```text

```

##### Getting all row elements including collapsed

**getRows() : List<Element>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names including collapsed.

```text

```

##### Getting all filtered row elements

**getFilteredRows() : List<Element>**

| $table.getFilteredRows() |
| --- |

The returned value is a list of Elements that have been filtered by the Filter field.

| #foreach($row in $table.getFilteredRows()) $row.name #end |
| --- |

The figure below shows the rows returned after applying the filter.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Rows returned after filtering.

##### Getting row elements in specific row numbers

**getRow( rowNumber : int ) : Element**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- rowNumber – the row number starts with 0.

The returned value is an Element.

#### Getting column names

Column names are referred by column IDs. A column is a QProperty ID that is not readable or accessible by the user. We need to create other methods that provide better usability than using QProperty ID. Use the following methods to retrieve column names.

The methods in this section retrieves column names from the list returned by Show Columns menu item from generic table diagram. The methods in the preceding section, however, retrieves column names from the diagram itself and not from the list returned by Show Columns menu item.

##### Getting column names from column ID

**getColumn( columnId : String) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnId – the ID of a column. Using column IDs has a benefit of consistency between different languages, for example, French and English. The column ID can be retrieved by the method getColumnIds().

The returned value is the name of a column.

##### Getting column names from column numbers

**getColumn(columnNumber : int) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the name of a column.

##### Getting all column names

**getColumnNames() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of column names.

Example code:

Prints all column names.

```text

```

##### Getting all column IDs

**getColumnIds() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of column IDs.

Example code:

Print all columns IDs

```text

```

#### Getting cell values

A cell value is the value in a row element with a column name. To get a cell value, you need to provide both the row element and column. A cell value is an Element or row number. Use the following methods to retrieve a cell value.

##### Getting values from row element and column ID

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnId – a column ID.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element. 
Example code:

Prints the row, column, and value.

```text
$row.name 
#foreach($col in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn$col) : $table.getValue($row, $col) 
#end
#end
#end]]>
```

##### Getting values from row element and column name

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column name.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row element and column number

**getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers

**getValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting values from row element and column IDs as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnId – a column ID.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row element and column name as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element
- columnName – a column name

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row element and column number as string

**getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers as string

**getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting values from row and column numbers as string with a full type of element in a property

**getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.
- showFullType – the default value is true for showing a full type of element in a cell in String form.

The returned value is the value in a cell, converted into String, and shown in a full type or normal form.

##### Getting cell value from returned object as string

**getCellValueAsString( object : Object ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- object – an object returned from the getValue function.

The returned value is the object in cell value converted into String.

For example:

```text

```

#### Getting visible column and cell values

A visible cell value is the column which is visible on a diagram. Unlike the methods described in section 23.1.5 Getting Cell Values, the following methods will retrieve only the value which is visible on a diagram.

##### Getting all visible column IDs

**getVisibleColumnIds() : List<String>**

```text

```

The returned value is a list of visible column IDs.

##### Getting visible column names from column numbers

**getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameter is:

- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the name of a visible column.

##### Getting visible values from row elements and column number

**getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting visible values from row number and column number

**getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.

##### Getting visible values from row element and column number as string

**getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowElement – a row element.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

##### Getting visible values from row and column numbers as string

**getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String**

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.
- columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.

The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe9fc602-4063-4fc7-aa70-76006b3386d5"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generic Table Tool Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generic table tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">GenericTableTool diagram.</h6><p>Class GenericTableTool:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">Methods:</p><ul><li>+getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table</li><li>+getTable( diagramName : String) : Table</li><li>+closeTables() : Void</li><li>+closeTable(diagram : Diagram) : Void</li><li>+closeTable(diagramName : String) : Void</li></ul><p>Class Table:</p><p style="margin-left: 60.0px;">Methods:</p><ul><li>+getRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</li><li>+getRow( rowNumber : int ) : Element </li><li>+getFilteredRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</li><li>+getColumn( columnId : String) : String </li><li>+getColumn( columnNumber : int) : String </li><li>+getColumnNames() : List&lt;String&gt; </li><li>+getColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt; </li><li>+getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object </li><li>+getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object </li><li>+getValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object </li><li>+getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String </li><li>+getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String </li><li>+getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</li><li>+getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String</li><li>+getVisibleColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt;</li><li>+getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String </li><li>+getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object </li><li>+getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object </li><li>+getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String </li><li>+getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</li></ul><h2>Getting generic table data</h2><p>A Generic Table is a special diagram. You can retrieve it by using the variable $Diagram. Thus, every method must accept the diagram instance or diagram name.</p><p>Use the following method to get the Generic Table instance. We use Generic Table instances to retrieve table information such as row elements and column names.</p><h3>Getting generic table instances from diagram elements</h3><p>To get a Generic Table instance from a specified diagram element, use the following code.</p><p><strong>getTable( diagram : Diagram) : Table</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="401326ad-61b0-4b2f-9b06-6b315d0790e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagram – the diagram element.</li></ul><p>Return the instance of a Generic Table.</p><p>Example code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3fd0d5a4-9003-4cff-b281-e42936590529"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Generic Table")) 
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram)) 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting generic table instances from diagram names</h3><p>To get a Generic Table instance from a specific diagram name, use the following code.</p><p><strong>getTable( diagramName : String) : Table</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2b7c206-ff7a-4972-bfe8-591384984d9b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>diagram – the diagram name.</li></ul><p>Return the instance of a Generic Table.</p><p>Example code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c44b1827-1a27-4be3-a2ab-e83a2080c4e0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($diagram = "diagram name") 
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Closing tables</h2><p>Use the following method to close a table diagram.</p><h3>Closing all diagram tables</h3><p><strong>CloseTables() : Void</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55b9c58c-e1d6-4a4e-bad5-e34597d195ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$generic.closeTables()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Closing a specific table diagram</h3><p><strong>closeTable(diagram: Diagram) : Void</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47029cbc-5629-4c72-aaf2-0284e0a17df4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$generic.closeTable($diagram)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagram – a table diagram you want to close.</li></ul><h3>Closing a table diagram with a specific diagram name</h3><p><strong>closeTable(diagramName: String) : Void</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff2ef186-cc73-403f-b341-8ee3af074835"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$generic.closeTable($diagramName)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>diagramName – the name of a table diagram you want to close.</li></ul><h2>Getting row elements</h2><p>The Generic Table consists of a series of row elements and column names. Use the following methods to retrieve a list of row elements.</p><h3>Getting all row elements</h3><p><strong>getRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37d80f8b-2a3b-4548-97a8-e7e82f7105dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getRows()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b13c6b9-344d-4be4-b80f-ef9f17db6d8c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($row in $table.rows)
$row.name 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting all row elements including collapsed</h3><p style="text-align: left;"><strong>getRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1efb64d5-3e85-4f36-be29-64b63f9c5fe3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getAllRows()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: left;">The returned value is a list of Elements. The following shows an example of how to print all row element names including collapsed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="069a96a6-6306-438a-83f9-d8aa09d6f933"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($row in $table.getAllRows())
$row.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Getting all filtered row elements</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">getFilteredRows() : List&lt;Element&gt;</span></strong></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><pre>$table.getFilteredRows()</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The returned value is a list of Elements that have been filtered by the Filter field.</span></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup class=""><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><td><pre>#foreach($row in $table.getFilteredRows())
$row.name 
#end</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The figure below shows the rows returned after applying the filter.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Rows returned after filtering" ac:alt="Rows returned after filtering"><ri:attachment ri:filename="107225_filter.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generic table tool API" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Rows returned after filtering.</span></h6><h3>Getting row elements in specific row numbers</h3><p><strong>getRow( rowNumber : int ) : Element</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c640a179-774f-4da8-9f3a-c2f0c2b991c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getRow($rowNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – the row number starts with 0.</li></ul><p>The returned value is an Element.</p><h2>Getting column names</h2><p>Column names are referred by column IDs. A column is a QProperty ID that is not readable or accessible by the user. We need to create other methods that provide better usability than using QProperty ID. Use the following methods to retrieve column names.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02dfa638-10bf-4d00-b492-65f7a750bf91"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The methods in this section retrieves column names from the list returned by Show Columns menu item from generic table diagram. The methods in the preceding section, however, retrieves column names from the diagram itself and not from the list returned by Show Columns menu item.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting column names from column ID</h3><p><strong>getColumn( columnId : String) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2e2d30a-8b3f-4485-b329-c15634cd6880"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getColumn($columnId)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li>columnId – the ID of a column. Using column IDs has a benefit of consistency between different languages, for example, French and English. The column ID can be retrieved by the method getColumnIds().</li></ul><p>The returned value is the name of a column.</p><h3>Getting column names from column numbers</h3><p><strong>getColumn(columnNumber : int) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="12c6ff41-2cef-4125-8727-29231eccd723"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getColumn($columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the name of a column.</p><h3>Getting all column names</h3><p><strong>getColumnNames() : List&lt;String&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="391985c6-c0fe-4f48-9af4-c06fc8ad0942"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getColumnNames()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of column names. </p><p>Example code:</p><p>Prints all column names.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5257286-f3e0-4c28-9db1-ccae9504d77c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($colname in $table.getColumnNames()) 
$colname 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting all column IDs</h3><p><strong>getColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54acafa0-3251-42bd-9946-35fa9a2b2878"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getColumnIds()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of column IDs.</p><p>Example code:</p><p>Print all columns IDs</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46810854-76df-416a-af46-5c962c95cef4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($colid in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn($colid) 
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Getting cell values</h2><p>A cell value is the value in a row element with a column name. To get a cell value, you need to provide both the row element and column. A cell value is an Element or row number. Use the following methods to retrieve a cell value.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column ID</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d596b18b-096f-4636-a62e-c7caa173605f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowElement, $columnId)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnId – a column ID.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element. <br />Example code:</p><p>Prints the row, column, and value.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="398c12e9-23d8-471a-9d49-28d353364645"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $project.getDiagrams("Generic Table")) 
#set($table = $generic.getTable($diagram)) 
#foreach($row in $table.getRows()) 
<h1>$row.name</h1> 
#foreach($col in $table.getColumnIds()) 
$table.getColumn$col) : $table.getValue($row, $col) 
#end
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Getting values from row element and column name</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd5c5321-cbfc-4a9e-839c-ccbd7815640c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowElement, $columnName)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnName – a column name.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column number</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e3b9762-9879-4e9e-a0bb-d3851a415da8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting values from row and column numbers</h3><p><strong>getValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc4bfae1-c329-4309-966c-2648beec4e70"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValue($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column IDs as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ef43774-c8d0-44eb-9ce7-753a8b8bdea7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowElement, $columnId)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnId – a column ID.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column name as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnIdOrName : String ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="761f3104-07b6-4da8-b947-a3db31c737c0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowElement, $columnName)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element</li><li>columnName – a column name </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row element and column number as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8dbe3278-4be0-403c-8ab6-87df908e1b90"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnName – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row and column numbers as string</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1d1e090-4dba-4e48-a1c6-0ae908c5b35f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting values from row and column numbers as string with a full type of element in a property</h3><p><strong>getValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int, showFullType : boolean ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96d189e3-9821-4731-81e4-caa568d08794"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber, showFullType)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li><li>showFullType – the default value is <strong>true</strong> for showing a full type of element in a cell in String form.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a cell, converted into String, and shown in a full type or normal form.</p><h3>Getting cell value from returned object as string</h3><p><strong>getCellValueAsString( object : Object ) : String<br /></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2361d9cf-dd0c-4bc2-a6b5-856a38528202"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getCellValueAsString($object)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is:</p><ul><li><p>object – an object returned from the getValue function.</p></li></ul><p>The returned value is the object in cell value converted into String.</p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59d0c265-e696-4d03-82ce-9cc6aca99734"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($result = $table.getValue($row, $id))
#if($list.isArray($result))
	#foreach($r in $result)
	- $table.getCellValueAsString($r)
	#end
#else
	$table.getCellValueAsString($result)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Getting visible column and cell values</h2><p>A visible cell value is the column which is visible on a diagram. Unlike the methods described in section 23.1.5 Getting Cell Values, the following methods will retrieve only the value which is visible on a diagram.</p><h3>Getting all visible column IDs</h3><p><strong>getVisibleColumnIds() : List&lt;String&gt;</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f10f816f-aba0-4ab2-b6b7-1544621f9deb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleColumnIds()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The returned value is a list of visible column IDs.</p><h3>Getting visible column names from column numbers</h3><p><strong>getVisibleColumn( columnNumber : int) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="064732bf-88e3-4d8b-851d-c512ef9fc6ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleColumn($columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameter is: </p><ul><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the name of a visible column.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row elements and column number</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValue( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e0c1a9c-f9f8-4575-8ba1-72772f971e82"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValue($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row number and column number</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValue( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : Object</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a44a419-d63e-4b05-97ce-4164d2fc9c26"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValue($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number. </li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell. If the cell contains an element, the value is the Element.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row element and column number as string</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValueAsString( rowElement : Element, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edda4a6c-9395-450d-90c5-b09cb8244bdd"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValueAsString($rowElement, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are: </p><ul><li>rowElement – a row element.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p><h3>Getting visible values from row and column numbers as string</h3><p><strong>getVisibleValueAsString( rowNumber : int, columnNumber : int ) : String</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e5fe694-94b0-4143-9945-7235cf9881f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$table.getVisibleValueAsString($rowNumber, $columnNumber)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are:</p><ul><li>rowNumber – a row number starts with 0.</li><li>columnNumber – a column number starts with 1. The column number 0 is the row number.</li></ul><p>The returned value is the value in a visible cell and is converted into String. The String value is created by the modeling tool text representation API.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737267 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03162: (2026x) Generic Table Wizard

- page_id: `272737267`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737267/2026x+Generic+Table+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:24.347+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Model Visualizer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Editions

639875940

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Editions

639875942

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Editions

639875941

#### NOTE: Editions

Editions

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generic table' space=''] allows you to display a list of selected elements and their properties in a single place.

The Generic Table Wizard helps you create a table when model elements and their relationships are already created and specified. You can select which elements (e.g., classes, packages, use cases, and relationships) will be included in a new table. You can also select properties of the selected element types, which will be displayed as columns in the table.

The Generic Table Wizard guides you through several steps and collects information along the way. It will automatically create a table with your selected elements, freeing you from creating the table manually.

To create a Generic Table using the Generic Table Wizard

1. Open the Generic Table Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Generic Table Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open The Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Generic Table Wizard and click Start .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

639875939

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generic table' space='']

## PAGE 03163: (2026x) Getting a value from a MagicDraw element to a template

- page_id: `272737626`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737626/2026x+Getting+a+value+from+a+MagicDraw+element+to+a+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:52.817+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x) Creating Your First Template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355429125

1355429128

1355429124

**On this page**

3

1355429128

#### Getting element values to a template

You can access the value of an element, and include it in a report in Report Wizard.

To get a value from a modeling tool element to a template

1. First you need to know the element type and attributes. Open the Specification dialog to see the type and attributes. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Specification dialog showing the element type on the title bar and the attributes in the properties specification table.
2. Open Microsoft Word and type, for example: text The example above shows that **$Class** is an array that contains all the Class elements in a **Selected Element Scope** (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard' space=''] for more information). **$class** is the individual Class element inside the array. To access the value of a property in **$class**, type: **$class** followed by **"."** and the attribute's name ("**.name**", "**.visibility**", and "**.isAbstract**" are the names of the **$class** attribute). The syntax for accessing the attribute value can be represented by: **$[Referenced object].[Attribute's name]**.

When you generate the output of the template for a project with a single Class element, the result will be shown as follows:

```text

```

#### Glossary

- Array An array is a container object that holds a fixed number of values of a single type. The length of an array is established when the array is created. After it is created, its length is fixed.
- Attribute Attribute defines values that can be attached to instances of the class or interface. For example, an attribute of a class represents the characteristics of the class.
- Class Class is the concrete realization of EncapsulatedClassifier and BehavioredClassifier. The purpose of a class is to specify a classification of objects, and to specify the Features that characterize the structure and behavior of those objects.
- Element Element is a constituent of a model. Every element has the inherent capability of owning other elements. When an element is removed from a model, all its ownedElements are also removed from the model. The abstract syntax for each kind of element specifies what other kind of elements it may own. Every element in a model must be owned by exactly one other element of that model, with the exception of the top-level Packages of the model.

#### More information about working with values in Report Wizard

To learn more about working with values in Report Wizard, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Retrieving Slot Values' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Getting property value' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5294ab9b-0dbd-4c35-8e21-a339488bd832"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429125</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1c9fce2-185b-4bb6-9dcb-d67262f12cce"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429128</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0309a6cd-d039-4d5b-856e-9ed3865c247f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429124</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c830c14c-1892-431b-a7f2-00db02d796c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d5422f7-7d55-4ac3-a689-fd1b84c6cd89"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355429128</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>Getting element values to a template</h2><p>You can access the value of an element, and include it in a report in Report Wizard.</p><p><br /></p><p>To get a value from a modeling tool element to a template</p><hr /><ol><li><p>First you need to know the element type and attributes. Open the Specification dialog to see the type and attributes.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="classspec.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Getting a value from a MagicDraw element to a template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>The Specification dialog showing the element type on the title bar and the attributes in the properties specification table.</h6></li><li><p>Open Microsoft Word and type, for example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="68018f45-ecc9-4f44-898d-539c24d3e41c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($class in $Class)
 Name: $class.name
 Owner: $class.owner.name
 Visibility: $class.visibility
 Is Abstract: $class.isAbstract
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The example above shows that <strong>$Class</strong> is an array that contains all the Class elements in a <strong>Selected Element Scope</strong> (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Wizard user guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more information). <strong>$class</strong> is the individual Class element inside the array. To access the value of a property in <strong>$class</strong>, type: <strong>$class</strong> followed by <strong>&quot;.&quot;</strong> and the attribute's name (&quot;<strong>.name</strong>&quot;, &quot;<strong>.visibility</strong>&quot;, and &quot;<strong>.isAbstract</strong>&quot; are the names of the <strong>$class</strong> attribute). The syntax for accessing the attribute value can be represented by: <strong>$[Referenced object].[Attribute's name]</strong>. </p></li></ol><p>When you generate the output of the template for a project with a single Class element, the result will be shown as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a810927-61b0-4b82-8bd9-273ff4b33faa"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Name: Customer
 Owner: com
 Visibility: public
 Is Abstract: false]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Array</strong><br />An array <span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);">is a container object that holds a fixed number of values of a single type. The length of an </span><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);">array</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> is established when the </span><span style="color: rgb(106,106,106);">array</span><span style="color: rgb(84,84,84);"> is created. After it is created, its length is fixed.</span></li><li><strong>Attribute</strong><br />Attribute defines values that can be attached to instances of the class or interface. For example, an attribute of a class represents the characteristics of the class.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Class</strong><br />Class is the concrete realization of EncapsulatedClassifier and BehavioredClassifier. The purpose of a class is to specify a classification of objects, and to specify the Features that characterize the structure and behavior of those objects.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Element</strong><br />Element is a constituent of a model. Every element has the inherent capability of owning other elements. When an element is removed from a model, all its ownedElements are also removed from the model. The abstract syntax for each kind of element specifies what other kind of elements it may own. Every element in a model must be owned by exactly one other element of that model, with the exception of the top-level Packages of the model.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with values in Report Wizard</h2><p>To learn more about working with values in Report Wizard, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Retrieving Slot Values" /></ac:link></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Getting property value" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 03164: (2026x) Getting comparison summary information and changes legend

- page_id: `272739514`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739514/2026x+Getting+comparison+summary+information+and+changes+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:27.688+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Comparing projects > (2026x) Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['summary-and-legend', 'comparison-summary', 'comparison-result', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see how many differences have been detected between the base and compared project version on the **Summary and legend** panel that is located at the top right of the **Difference Viewer** window. The panel also helps to understand the decorations and highlighting of elements in both the **Differences**tree and the **Specification** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can see how many differences have been detected between the base and compared project version on the <strong>Summary and legend</strong> panel that is located at the top right of the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window. The panel also helps to understand the decorations and highlighting of elements in both the <strong>Differences </strong>tree and the <strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fa21c6bb-741a-4318-83ff-bf732f4ae486">Specification</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fa21c6bb-741a-4318-83ff-bf732f4ae486"> panel.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:border="true" ac:title="Project comparison summary and legend" ac:alt="Project comparison summary and legend"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_legend.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Getting comparison summary information and changes legend" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 03165: (2026x) Getting merge summary information and changes legend

- page_id: `272735985`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735985/2026x+Getting+merge+summary+information+and+changes+legend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:29.831+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Model Merge > (2026x) Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merge-summary', 'summary-and-legend', 'merged-result', 'merge-legend', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1767089336

INLINE

1767089338

INLINE

1767089337

INLINE

The **Summary/Legend** panel is located at the bottom left of the **Merge** window. The panel helps to understand the marking and highlighting of elements in both the **Merged Result** tree/list and the **Specification** panel.

On the **Summary/Legend** panel, for 3-way merge, you can see how many differences have been detected between the ancestor and the source, as well as between the ancestor and the target.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For 2-way merge, you can see how many differences have been detected between two versions, and what kind of changes were detected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1767089335

INLINE
