&lt;operation name&gt; latency &lt;latency time&gt; </em></p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The latency (i.e., the time it takes for the ping signal sent from the tool to the server to return) is measured for the following operations:</p><ul><li data-uuid="83e58b0c-ab93-4087-9778-69fa33c697af">Login</li><li data-uuid="ac13a91b-8e0e-4c90-aab6-4ac042bbda75">Committing project to server</li><li data-uuid="cfbefb68-fbc1-44d8-965d-ccccafea3edd">Updating project</li><li data-uuid="029d6b99-6b2d-406f-a762-8de9d27afc17">Opening project<br /><br /></li></ul><p class="title">Example: INFO  NetworkPerformance - Login latency 1 ms</p><p><strong>Remote Operations Duration Statistics</strong></p><p><em>INFO  RemoteCallsAnalyzer - &lt;operation name&gt; all operations time &lt;total operation time&gt;</em></p><p><em>INFO  RemoteCallsAnalyzer - &lt;operation name&gt; calls total time: &lt;total time, spent in remote calls&gt;, start count: &lt;remote calls count&gt;, failed count: &lt;failed remote calls count&gt;</em></p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The remote and local operations duration statistics are collected for the following operations:</p><ul><li data-uuid="7192d014-9d1d-4b4c-a43b-4d98737a8fb1">Updating from local project</li><li data-uuid="f9c4a982-06c6-469b-9e55-6f0d7bc8da69">Opening<strong> Manage Projects</strong> dialog</li><li data-uuid="e01bad69-4140-4398-b004-0430f080a8d7">Opening <strong>Open Server Project</strong> dialog</li><li data-uuid="1f5c7d7d-26ea-4726-9abc-2671a0f488e0">Updating project</li><li data-uuid="b6571f29-ba65-45fd-820b-bbf5f649ce44">Committing changes to a server project</li><li data-uuid="6b2df5c4-d600-48c1-9170-413e7885450b">Setting selected version as latest</li><li data-uuid="a6350a99-4870-47c6-825a-39dec645eca7">Cloning project</li><li data-uuid="97cd3a33-45fd-47b9-ae08-cfce3f4531c8">Managing version properties</li><li data-uuid="7a0750be-bd29-4d3b-b748-72644f045a5b">Opening Teamwork Cloud project</li><li data-uuid="752714ae-ad38-4e45-a795-817c5fd626a1">Renaming project</li><li data-uuid="1dc6af7a-8950-47e4-b057-d5755fddfe67">Removing project</li><li data-uuid="adf6cdd5-b139-482b-9105-c4583adc3fb3">Adding project to server</li><li data-uuid="75775711-d887-4748-a160-ca7e67eca10b">Viewing element history</li><li data-uuid="86a99280-30d2-46ac-a901-c5f8b168efed">Comparing projects</li><li data-uuid="5b58d4b1-b8d2-4ac9-a06e-ad86828ab56b">Using server project</li><li data-uuid="977966c9-61cb-4027-bc72-86a6fd1a3373">Discarding changes</li><li data-uuid="bf64b80b-536f-438b-9e24-1a21c12a9b40">Locking and unlocking elements/diagrams</li><li data-uuid="e44c8740-72ea-477e-b985-bf3b7cd845d0">Changing used project version</li></ul><p class="title">Example: </p><ul><li data-uuid="4884fb67-7cc1-420a-8ae6-63551a779a0f">INFO  RemoteCallsAnalyzer - Manage projects all operations time 9797 ms</li><li data-uuid="5356167d-111b-4780-abe6-b54410899ff3">INFO  RemoteCallsAnalyzer - Manage projects calls total time: 2293 ms, start count: 47, failed count: 0</li></ul><h3>Common Features</h3><h4>Validation</h4><p>The validation engine helps to keep the model accurate, complete, and correct. Since model checking is a complicated task that greatly depends on the project size, please consider the following recommendations to keep the modeling tool running smoothly:</p><ul><li data-uuid="1c504bb6-7dc9-4864-bd48-cddfe1a35581">Set the <strong>Validation Scope</strong><em> </em>to narrow down the list of validated elements.</li><li data-uuid="27f1334d-0fd3-4348-b9db-4d7daea8916b">Set the <strong>Validate Only Visible Diagrams</strong><em> </em>project option to <em>true </em>to limit a validation scope to visible diagrams only. Since 2021x version, all projects are migrated to validate only visible diagrams. This is a key option to keep the modeling tool running smoothly while working on large models.</li><li data-uuid="bc923b76-4c90-4992-88ba-6dda9fcde5c1">Set the <strong>Exclude Elements from Used Read-Only Project </strong>project option to <em>true </em>to skip the validation of elements used projects contain.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_project_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Guidelines for working with large models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Querying the Model</h4><p style="margin-left: 0.0cm;text-align: justify;">Ineffectively written queries can cause showstopper problems, especially when a project becomes quite large. The most attention should be paid to Script, Find, and Find Usage in Diagrams operations. Be aware that the slow execution of queries can noticeably impact the modeling experience - some operations trigger an expression (e.g., smart packages and derived properties) recalculation. </p><ul><li data-uuid="f69ab820-f21f-4968-89f3-8cc7317b69bf">The Find operation should not be performed for the whole project scope when specifying expressions for smart packages, derived properties, validation rules, etc. Since the operation is highly dependent on the search scope - the wider it is, the longer it takes - it can noticeably impact the modeling experience by slowing down the common modeling tool commands that, as a result, cause the expression recalculation.</li><li data-uuid="be46d63c-0ded-4b2e-bbac-6bc88c408167"><p>It is not a good practice to use Find for searching common elements (e.g., Class, Package, Block, etc.).</p></li><li data-uuid="4bd0bf67-b467-45ea-9de9-af089fed813f"><p>Searching element usages in diagrams should be used carefully since the operation searches for element usages in dynamic content.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d7dd41f-de74-4add-be1c-69c023c0427f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">UsageInDiagrams is Deprecated</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: left;">This operation is very expensive. It not only checks symbolic diagrams for elements but also loads and builds Generic Tables, Dependency Matrices, Relationship Maps, etc. It should be best avoided and used only if you understand the possible consequence of building all diagrams in the project.</p><p style="text-align: left;">Instead of it, please use the <strong style="text-align: left;">UsageInDiagramsWithSymbols </strong>operation<strong style="text-align: left;"> </strong>that s<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">earches for usages only in symbolic diagrams. Other diagrams, such as Generic Tables, Dependency Matrices, Relationship Maps, etc., are not searched. This operation performs fast and does not load any diagrams to get the information.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="109ba95e-712c-4649-8ba5-00f542ecfccc">Smart Package specifics:<br /><ul><li><strong>How does this affect performance? </strong>The Smart Package content is calculated on demand and cached. Next time on content request, the cached value is used. The cache is cleared on any model change. So the impact on performance depends on the performance of the content calculation, content request frequency, and model change frequency.<br /><strong>Examples</strong>:<ul><li>Model Browser: if a Smart Package is visible in the Model Browser, it will be refreshed each time after the cache is invalidated (model change occurs, e.g., a new element is created or an existing element is removed). So if a Smart Package is added directly under the root, it will always be visible in the Containment Tree (after opening a project, the root is expanded by default).</li><li>Validation: if the Smart Package is within the validation scope and the &quot;Exclude elements from additional content&quot; option is disabled, running validation will recalculate Smart Packages if the cache is invalidated.</li><li>Suspect links: <strong>Show Suspicions</strong> action triggers the calculation of Smart Packages that fall within the Tracking Scope. Starting with 2021x, the calculation of Smart Packages is no longer triggered.</li><li>Tables/matrices: if the Smart Package is within the table/matrix scope, it will be calculated by default. Starting with 2021x Refresh1, the content of Smart Packages is not included by default - the <strong>Include Additional Content</strong> table/matrix option was introduced for that.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong>Use In Selection Dialogs</strong> option - set to <em>false</em> if a slow-running query is defined.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Use_In_Selection_Dialogs_option.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Guidelines for working with large models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Avoid nesting Smart Packages, i.e., using one Smart Package as part of another Smart Package's content.</li><li><strong>Freezing the contents of a Smart Package</strong>. If you do not want the contents of the Smart Package to be updated automatically, you can suspend the calculation of the Smart Package contents. As a result, elements gathered according to a set of criteria specified in the <strong>Query</strong> dialog are no longer automatically added to the Smart Package contents. You can undo this at any time by changing the <strong>Frozen</strong> state. <br /><ac:image ac:alt="frozen_enabled.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/70410160/frozen_enabled.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1616502291005&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul><p>The chart below shows how the Legend item condition execution time differs by performing the same expression in different languages. We advise choosing the most efficiently performing languages which are indicated as recommended (StructuredExpression, Groovy).</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Scripting_languages_performance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Guidelines for working with large models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">A chart comparing the Legend item condition execution time by language.</span></h6><h4>Troubleshooting</h4><p>You can use the Evaluate Operation Performance capability to see the performance diagnostics that help determine the reasons behind slow tool operations if they are caused by the execution of expressions (e.g., derived properties, smart packages, legends, validation rules) defined in the model. Matrix and table building can also be analyzed.</p><p><span>For example, you noticed that opening a particular diagram took longer than expected. A progress bar titled &quot;Adorning&quot; popped up for a few seconds before the diagram opened. To know what caused this performance issue, run the Evaluate Operation Performance tool. In its dialog, click </span> <span>the 'Start' button, then </span><span>open the diagram again reproducing the slow operation, and once </span><span>it is done, click the 'Stop' button to see the results. </span> <span>You can use this information to investigate the particular expression and improve its efficiency.</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Evaluating_operation_performance.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Guidelines for working with large models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">More information can be found <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Evaluating operation performance" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><h4>Diagrams</h4><p style="margin-left: 0.0cm;">The consumed memory increases while working with the project for some time. One of the reasons is the number of open diagrams. As high memory usage becomes a problem, we recommend that you do not keep unused diagrams open. To prevent keeping a large number of open diagrams, beginning with version 2021x, the open diagrams limit was introduced with 20 diagrams set as the default value.</p><p style="margin-left: 0.0cm;">Please find below how memory consumption increases depending on the size of a diagram/table:</p><p style="margin-left: 0.0cm;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="memory_consumption_of_tables_and_diagrams_with_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Guidelines for working with large models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 0.0cm;">Chart depicting memory consumption by diagram size (symbols/rows displayed in a diagram/table).</h6><ul><li data-uuid="8ee859c5-0c59-4662-bb9a-03bf29a44ca6">The matrix/table building time greatly depends on the specified scope - the wider it is, the more time it takes to build the matrix/table. </li><li style="list-style-type: none;" data-uuid="bd2b5de4-bc9e-4937-a75e-870c94cfcdcd"><p>Please avoid setting the entire model as the scope even if the matrix/table building performs well, as it can cause unacceptable performance in the following circumstances:</p><ul><li>a project containing such a table/matrix is used in another project;</li><li>the model evolves over time, so the table will eventually reach unscalable sizes.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="a32aecab-07ed-4c3e-bbef-1eacb15942ba">Also, it is not advisable to choose more than 10 element types to be displayed because this may lead to a decrease in the matrix/table building time. Since matrices/tables have an option to define a scope query, the Smart Packages should not be set as scopes as well.</li></ul><h4>Working with Large Tables</h4><p>The process of calculating all data in large tables (containing thousands of rows) can be slow and inefficient. For the most efficient performance, we suggest the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="4e4f0fa0-1233-44e0-b092-04162d627399">Avoid creating tables that exceed ±50 000 cells as that can affect the performance of actions performed on the table data; in such a case, it is recommended to create multiple tables instead.</li><li data-uuid="aa31587f-c171-491c-9577-9ae82d3d56f8">Switching to partial loading. Data is loaded only in the visible area, so the most common tasks are performed quickly. More information can be found <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Partial loading in tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="partial_loading_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Guidelines for working with large models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Working with Model Browser</h4><p class="p1">The organization of the model is determined by the package hierarchy you create to partition the elements in the model into logically cohesive groups. This affects the model browser performance as well - to avoid sluggish browsing in Containment Tree, you need to organize your model to avoid 10k and more directly owned elements by the particular element. Since the containment model browser is a lazy tree, it calculates and represents the nodes while expanding the owner. A large number of owned elements can cause unexpected waiting time to complete the task. So, we recommend splitting elements by creating different packages.</p><p class="p1"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_browser_structure.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Guidelines for working with large models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 0.0cm;">A model structured into packages.</h6><h3>Virtual Environments</h3><ul><li data-uuid="738ecb6b-e0c8-4251-913e-0b50ca485c6b">Two issues have been identified in VMWare ESX environments. To help prevent problems on the Teamwork Cloud side, we have come up with recommended hardware configurations.</li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="86b678f6-f9ea-443f-b204-a27f37d1d529"><ol><li><strong>Overcommitting of resources</strong>. The same host has an over-allocation of CPU/RAM. The bigger problem is the over-allocation of RAM. For example, let's say a machine has 1TB of physical RAM. You allocate more than 1TB across various VM's in the host. What happens is that the RAM is no longer physical RAM, but rather mapped to disk, so performance decreases. Xen (Citrix) and KVM do not allow you to over-commit. In these, you can allocate min/max but never exceed the physical RAM, and as more instances start using up the RAM, the system reduces the allocated memory.</li><li><strong>Thin disk provisioning</strong>. This arises from an over-allocation of disk resources. Let's say you have a given amount of disk space to be used among VM's. In many cases, the admin does not know how much the actual disk usage is required per machine. Some VM's may use more, some less. Therefore, you over-allocate the storage using thin provisioning. Under thin provisioning, only the amount of storage being used is allocated to the virtual disk. As utilization increases, the virtual disk (which is a file) grows. This, however, has a negative impact on the performance of input/output-intensive applications like databases. For Cassandra, it is a killer in performance because compaction can potentially temporarily double the on-disk size. All disks must be thick provisioned.</li></ol></li></ol><ul><li data-uuid="d8ab37f7-5290-4746-882c-142a101e42eb">The other item is that, at the very least, you need to be using SAS-SSD. If using SAS-SSD, the host needs to have high-end caching controllers. On the Teamwork Cloud side, the core count is important - the more cores, the better.</li><li data-uuid="e462b98c-13af-49d7-8441-5f2ec537197f">SAN's are not recommended. Only NVMe (all flash storage) will provide the necessary performance for large installations, provided that they are properly configured and that enough bandwidth is allocated to the instances.</li><li data-uuid="ffba01a4-0ca6-48ba-b577-05fac4457fa0">It is recommended that you stay away from Windows deployments. There is no freely available Cassandra Windows installer.</li><li data-uuid="4437ea2e-3461-4967-9b8d-5f4e77b696ff">If we look at cloud hosting, the older instances in Amazon use the Xen hypervisor (xcp), whereas the new ones use the kvm hypervisor. These are the instances you should use: preferably, the NVMe-based i3 instances, next would be r5, and finally m5. What is critical is to provision the filesystem properly.</li><li data-uuid="cd8f661d-f28d-475f-8261-9102284b6e8a">On the client side, the same issues of resources over-allocation can be a performance killer. Thin provisioning affects performance because disk caches are used extensively. Therefore, what is more, important when using VDI's is working on machines that have faster processors. When provisioning the VDI's, we do not recommend that you allocate a lot of CPU's because they will not be used. It is important to have the fastest processors.</li></ul><h3>Antivirus</h3><p>Since some antivirus software can cause a significant performance decrease upon opening a project and performing other actions, we recommend that you exclude the modeling tool's home directory from the antivirus scanning scope.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731981 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03181: (2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models

- page_id: `272731981`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731981/2026x+Handling+inconsistencies+between+structure+and+behavior+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:58.132+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

464453088

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

464453099

INLINE

464453086

INLINE

**On this page**

**3**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

464453089

INLINE

Three validation rules complement each other to check whether the structure model is compatible with the behavior model. If any inconsistencies are found, the structure model can be automatically synchronized with the behavior model using solvers. First, you need to synchronize interfaces so that you can handle Connectors and, finally, Item Flows. As a consequence, the manual work is reduced.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can handle detected inconsistencies using solvers directly in:

- Internal Block Diagram
- Validation Results panel

##### Handling missing Proxy Ports and Interface Blocks

The first validation rule checks whether Proxy Ports and Interface Blocks are compatible with flows that flow between the two different Part Properties in Activities. The missing Proxy Ports and Interface Blocks can be handled automatically using solvers.

To create missing Proxy Ports and Interface Blocks

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Block Diagram, select a Part Property highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the open **Validation Results** panel, right-click a validation result. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Select one of the following:

1. 
  - Create Port - creates a new Proxy Port typed by a compatible Interface Block.
  - - changes an already existing Proxy Port type to a compatible Interface Block.
  - Add Missing Flow Property - adds a new Flow Property to an Interface Block that types a Proxy Port.
  - Reverse Direction of Port - changes the Proxy Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of an Interface Block that types a Proxy Port.

A Proxy Port typed by a compatible Interface Block is created.

###### Choosing a compatible Interface Block

If you select the **Choose Compatible Interface Block** solver, the **Choose Proxy Port with Compatible Interface Block**dialog opens.

You can now choose a Proxy Port and type it with an Interface Block that has Flow Properties compatible with the type and direction of the missing flow.

Alternatively, you can create a new Proxy Port and an Interface Block to type the Proxy Port. For this, select **<NEW>**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Choosing a Proxy Port and specifying a compatible Interface Block as its type.

##### Handling missing Connectors

The second validation rule checks whether there is a Connector between the two compatible Proxy Ports owned by the Part Property. The missing Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.

To create missing Connectors

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Block Diagram, select a Proxy Port highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='btos_second_validation_rule.png']
  - In the open Validation Results panel, right-click a validation result. [ATTACHMENT filename='second_validation_rule_creating_connector_from_validation_results_panel.png']
2. Select Create Connector .

A Connector between the two compatible Proxy Ports is created.

##### Handling missing Item Flows

The third validation rule checks realized Item Flows in Activities and helps to ensure the correct Item Flows are realized on Connectors that connect Proxy Ports typed by compatible Interface Blocks. The missing Item Flows can be handled automatically using solvers.

To realize missing Item Flows on Connectors

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Block Diagram, select a Connector highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='third_btos_validation_rule_IBD.png']
  - In the open Validation Results panel, right-click a validation result. [ATTACHMENT filename='third_btos_validation_rule_validation_results_panel.png']
2. Select one of the following:
  - Realize Missing Item Flow - realizes the compatible existing Item Flow on the Connector that was found in the behavior model.
  - Open Item Flow Manager - opens the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Item Flow Manager dialog' space=''] dialog.
  - Change Item Flow Realization Compatibility Checking - opens the Project Options dialog to enable/disable the Item Flow realization checking.

A compatible Item Flow is realized on the selected Connector.

##### Navigating to elements

To learn more about the validated elements, in the smart manipulator toolbar of the Internal Block Diagram or Validation Results panel, select the **Navigate To** command to navigate to:

- Activity Edges in the Activity diagram from which the missing flow has been identified.
- Part Properties in the Internal Block Diagram between which items are flowing.

Elements are automatically selected on the diagram pane and in the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Navigating to an element from the smart manipulator toolbar.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can handle detected inconsistencies using solvers directly in:</p><ul><li>Internal Block Diagram</li><li><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Handling missing Proxy Ports and Interface Blocks</h3><p>The first validation rule checks whether Proxy Ports and Interface Blocks are compatible with flows that flow between the two different Part Properties in Activities. The missing Proxy Ports and Interface Blocks can be handled automatically using solvers. </p><p>To create toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span>.</span><br /><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_to_structure_solvers_on_IBD.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span>In the open <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel, right-click a validation result. </span><br /><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="btos_validation_results_panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul></li><li>Select one of the following:</li></ol><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Create Port </strong>- creates a new Proxy Port typed by a compatible Interface Block.</li><li><strong><ac:link ac:anchor="Choosing a compatible Interface Block"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Choose Compatible Interface Block]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>- changes an already existing Proxy Port type to a compatible Interface Block.<strong><br /></strong></li><li><strong>Add Missing Flow Property </strong>- adds a new Flow Property to an Interface Block that types a Proxy Port.</li><li><strong>Reverse Direction of Port </strong>- changes the Proxy Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of an Interface Block that types a Proxy Port.</li></ul></li></ol><p>A Proxy Port typed by a compatible Interface Block is created. </p><h4>Choosing a compatible Interface Block</h4><p>If you select the <strong>Choose Compatible Interface Block</strong> solver, the <strong>Choose Proxy Port with Compatible Interface Block </strong>dialog opens.</p><p>You can now choose a Proxy Port and type it with an Interface Block that has Flow Properties compatible with the type and direction of the missing flow.</p><p>Alternatively, you can create a new Proxy Port and an Interface Block to type the Proxy Port. For this, select <strong>&lt;NEW&gt;</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="choosing_proxy_port_with_compatible_interface_block.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Choosing a Proxy Port and specifying a compatible Interface Block as its type.</h6><h3>Handling missing Connectors</h3><p>The second validation rule checks whether there is a Connector between the two compatible Proxy Ports owned by the Part Property. The missing Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.</p><p>To create missing Connectors</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the Internal Block Diagram, select a Proxy Port highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="btos_second_validation_rule.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the open <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel, right-click a validation result. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="second_validation_rule_creating_connector_from_validation_results_panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Select <strong>Create Connector</strong>.</li></ol><p>A Connector between the two compatible Proxy Ports is created. </p><h3>Handling missing Item Flows</h3><p>The third validation rule checks realized Item Flows in Activities and helps to ensure the correct Item Flows are realized on Connectors that connect Proxy Ports typed by compatible Interface Blocks. <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="72d79904-596f-45f5-a489-96b50cce33de">The</ac:inline-comment-marker> missing Item Flows can be handled automatically using solvers.</p><p>To realize missing Item Flows on Connectors</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the Internal Block Diagram, select a Connector highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="third_btos_validation_rule_IBD.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the open <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel, right-click a validation result. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="third_btos_validation_rule_validation_results_panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Select one of the following:<ul><li><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="57125055-8fc2-4cba-b681-24a478761656">Realize Missing Item Flow</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="57125055-8fc2-4cba-b681-24a478761656"> - </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="57125055-8fc2-4cba-b681-24a478761656">realizes the compatible existing Item Flow on the Connector that was found in the behavior model.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><strong>Open Item Flow Manager</strong> - opens the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Item Flow Manager dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Item Flow Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</li><li><strong>Change Item Flow Realization Compatibility Checking</strong> - opens the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog to enable/disable the Item Flow realization checking. </li></ul></li></ol><p>A compatible Item Flow is realized on the selected Connector. </p><h3>Navigating to elements</h3><p>To learn more about the validated elements, in the smart manipulator toolbar of the Internal Block Diagram or Validation Results panel, select the <strong>Navigate To</strong> command to navigate to:</p><ul><li>Activity Edges in the Activity diagram from which the missing flow has been identified.</li><li>Part Properties in the Internal Block Diagram between which items are flowing.</li></ul><p>Elements are automatically selected on the diagram pane and in the Containment tree. </p><p> <ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="navigating_to_element_IBD.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Navigating to an element from the smart manipulator toolbar.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738715 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03182: (2026x) Handling inconsistencies between structure and behavior models in UAF

- page_id: `272738715`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738715/2026x+Handling+inconsistencies+between+structure+and+behavior+models+in+UAF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:30.161+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) UAF Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

464453088

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

464453099

464453086

**On this page**

**3**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

464453089

Three validation rules complement each other to check whether the structure model is compatible with the behavior model. If any inconsistencies are found, the structure model can be automatically synchronized with the behavior model using solvers. First, you must synchronize interfaces to handle Connectors and, finally, Exchanges. As a consequence, manual work is reduced.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

You can handle detected inconsistencies using solvers directly in:

- Internal Connectivity Diagram
- Validation Results panel

##### Handling missing Ports and Interfaces

The first validation rule checks whether Ports and Interfaces are compatible with flows between the Asset Roles. The missing Ports and Interfaces can be handled automatically using solvers.

To create missing Ports and Interfaces

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Connectivity Diagram, select an Asset Role highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the open **Validation Results** panel, right-click a validation result. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Select one of the following:

1. 
  - Create <viewpoint name> Port - creates a new Port typed by a compatible Interface.
  - Interface]]> - changes an existing Port type to a compatible Interface.
  - Add Missing Flow Property - adds a new Flow Property to an Interface that types a Port.
  - Reverse Direction of <viewpoint name> Port - changes the Port direction, thus changing the Flow Property direction of an Interface that types a Port.

A Port typed by a compatible Interface is created.

###### Choosing a compatible Interface

If you select the **Choose Compatible Interface**solver, the **Choose Port with Compatible Interface**dialog opens.

You can now choose a Port and type it with an Interface with Flow Properties compatible with the type and direction of the missing flow.

Alternatively, you can create a new Port and an Interface to type the Port. For this, select **<NEW>**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Choosing a Port and specifying a compatible Interface as its type.

##### Handling missing Connectors

The second validation rule checks whether a Connector exists between the two compatible Ports owned by the Asset Role. The missing Connectors can be handled automatically using solvers.

To create missing Connectors

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Connectivity Diagram, select a Port highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='UAF_behavior_to_structure_connectors_validation_rule.png']
  - In the open Validation Results panel, right-click a validation result. [ATTACHMENT filename='UAF_behavior_to_structure_connectors_validation_rule_panel.png']
2. Select Create <viewpoint name> Connector .

A Connector between the two compatible Ports is created.

##### Handling missing Exchanges

The third validation rule checks realized Exchanges in Activities and help to ensure the correct Exchanges are realized on Connectors that connect Ports typed by compatible Interface. The missing Exchanges can be handled automatically using solvers.

To realize missing Exchanges on Connectors

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Internal Connectivity Diagram, select a Connector highlighted in yellow, and then in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='triangle_symbol_smart_manipulator_toolbar.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='UAF_behavior_to_structure_exchanges_validation_rule.png']
  - In the open Validation Results panel, right-click a validation result. [ATTACHMENT filename='UAF_behavior_to_structure_exchanges_validation_rule_panel.png']
2. Select one of the following:
  - Realize Missing <viewpoint name> Exchange - realizes existing compatible Exchange found in the behavior model on the Connector.
  - Open <viewpoint name> Exchange Manager - opens the Exchange Manager* dialog .
  - Change Exchange Realization Compatibility Checking - opens the Project Options dialog to enable/disable the Exchange realization checking.

A compatible Exchange is realized on the selected Connector.

##### Navigating to elements

To learn more about the validated elements, in the smart manipulator toolbar of the Internal Connectivity Diagram or Validation Results panel, select the **Navigate To** command to navigate to:

- Activity Edges in the Process Flow diagram from which the missing flow has been identified.
- Asset Roles in the Internal Connectivity Diagram between which items are flowing.

Elements are automatically selected on the diagram pane and in the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Navigating to an element from the smart manipulator toolbar.

1851350132

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734804 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03184: (2026x) Hide Uncommon Elements filter

- page_id: `272734804`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734804/2026x+Hide+Uncommon+Elements+filter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:29.159+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Selecting elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1827084503

1827084505

1827084504

You can enable the *Hide Uncommon Elements* filter in the *Select Type* dialog to leave out uncommon element types and thus find the ones you need easier.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Hide Uncommon Elements filter in the Select Type dialog

Once *Hide Uncommon Elements*is enabled, the**filter returns only those elements whose types are based on:

- Class (except Metaclass and Term)
- Component
- Signal
- Interface
- DataType
- Enumeration
- PrimitiveType
- AssociationClass

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specified property type

If the SysML profile is loaded, only elements of*Block*and*ValueType*types are listed in the *Select Type* dialog.

If the Stereotype tag types are being specified, metaclasses are not filtered out.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Metaclasses in the *Select Type* dialog

1827084502

**Related Pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting port and part types' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732802 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03185: (2026x) Hiding direction arrow on port shape

- page_id: `272732802`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732802/2026x+Hiding+direction+arrow+on+port+shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:15.368+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x) Creating ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To hide the direction arrow on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED'] shape

1. Right-click the Proxy Port or Flow Port shape and select the Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, set the Show Port Direction property value to false.
3. Click Close . The direction arrow is hidden on the selected port shape.

The direction arrow of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'] shape is hidden by default. To display it, set the **Show Port Direction** property value to *true* in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To hide the direction arrow on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Flow Port" /></ac:link> shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Proxy Port or Flow Port shape and select the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Port Direction</strong> property value to <em>false.</em></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The direction arrow is hidden on the selected port shape.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8be4db4-b70c-46f9-b22d-14251cd8a000"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The direction arrow of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Full Port" /></ac:link> shape is hidden by default. To display it, set the <strong>Show Port Direction</strong> property value to <em>true</em> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732800 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03186: (2026x) Hiding the multiplicity

- page_id: `272732800`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732800/2026x+Hiding+the+multiplicity
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:15.217+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Defining interfaces using ports > (2026x) Creating ports
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can hide the multiplicity next to the port andPart Property type.

To hide the multiplicity

1. Right-click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] shape and select**Symbol Properties**.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog, set the **Show Multiplicity** property value to *false*.
3. Click **Close**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737277 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03187: (2026x) Hierarchy Diagram Wizard

- page_id: `272737277`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737277/2026x+Hierarchy+Diagram+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:26.270+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Model Visualizer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

The **Hierarchy Diagram Wizard** collects the largest hierarchies and allows each of them to be displayed either as separate diagrams or all in one diagram.

To create a diagram using the **Hierarchy Diagram Wizard**

1. Open the Hierarchy Diagram Wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the Diagrams menu, select Diagram Wizards > Hierarchy Diagram Wizard .
  - From the Analyze menu, select Model Visualizer . In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the Hierarchy Diagram Wizard and click **Start** .
2. Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33df9e1d-7457-4043-ac81-6afd9c3c69da"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong> collects the largest hierarchies and allows each of them to be displayed either as separate diagrams or all in one diagram.</p><p> </p><p>To create a diagram using the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong><strong> </strong>by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the <strong>Diagrams</strong> menu, select <strong>Diagram Wizards</strong> &gt; <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong>.</li><li>From the <strong>Analyze</strong> menu, select <strong>Model Visualizer</strong>. In the open Model Visualizer dialog, select to open the <strong>Hierarchy Diagram Wizard</strong> and click <strong><strong>Start</strong></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps as directed by the wizard.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730312 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03189: (2026x) How aspects match with parts and ports

- page_id: `272730312`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730312/2026x+How+aspects+match+with+parts+and+ports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:29.887+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The diagram aspects mechanism identifies whether or not the aspect name and name of the stereotypes applied for the parts and ports (or their types) match. The stereotype name must contain a matching aspect name. For example, a diagram aspect name is *Electrical,*which matches a port stereotype name W*Electrical.* When these conditions are met, you can display only the *Electrical* part of your model in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Composite Structure diagram' space='']. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying parts and ports according to aspects' space='']>]]>

If the diagram aspect name doesn't match the name of the part/port stereotype or its type, you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] with the same name as the aspect's and [apply it for parts and ports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype). Or you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating custom aspects' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Matching the diagram aspect *Electrical* with the port stereotype name *Electrical*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The diagram aspects mechanism identifies whether or not the aspect name and name of the stereotypes applied for the parts and ports (or their types) match. The stereotype name must contain a matching aspect name. For example, a diagram aspect name is <em>Electrical, </em>which matches a port stereotype name W<em>Electrical.</em> When these conditions are met, you can display only the <em>Electrical</em> part of your model in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports according to aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display parts and port according to aspect >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4704f5b7-46ac-40ca-a191-e433fd556328"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the diagram aspect name doesn't match the name of the part/port stereotype or its type, you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with the same name as the aspect's and <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype">apply it for parts and ports</a>. Or you can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating custom aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a custom diagram aspect]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aspects_and_stereotype_match.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How aspects match with parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Matching the diagram aspect <em>Electrical</em> with the port stereotype name <em>Electrical</em>.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737702 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03190: (2026x) How modeling tool adds its elements to velocity context

- page_id: `272737702`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737702/2026x+How+modeling+tool+adds+its+elements+to+velocity+context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:12.868+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x) Linking report templates to the modeling tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1358598954

1358598955

1358598955

In Velocity, whenever you want to reference to a Java object you need to manually add the object to the Velocity template and name it. In Report Wizard, however, this process is done automatically. What you need to understand is how it is done as it will help you to call the modeling tool elements.

All elements in the modeling tool such as Class, Use Case, and Activity are the sub-classes of elements. During report generation, the report engine will get all the elements from a project within a selected scope that you have defined in Report Wizard, and then classify and add them to a list of the same type. The list will then be added to the Velocity Context with its type as its name. For instance, in a project where there are two types of Element, Class, and Use Case, all the elements that are identified as Classes will be kept on the same list and the list will be added to the Velocity Context and labeled as "Class" during report generation. This process also applies to the Use Case elements. To reference to a specific Class element, first you will have to make a reference to **$Class** (list of Class elements), and then iterate through the list to find that particular Class element by using the **#foreach**, **#forrow**, **#forcol**, or **#forpage** directive, followed by the **#if** directive to search for the name of the class.

This section will use part of the "*Inventory Control System.mdzip*" file in *<install.root>/samples/casestudies* as an example to show you how to reference elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Use Case View.

You need to know the elements inside the "Use Case View" package before using it, see figure above. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating Your First Template' space='']*.

The "Use Case View" package contains three element types: (i) Actor, (ii) UseCase, and (iii) Diagram that you can call in a template by typing **"$Actor**," **"$UseCase**," and **"$Diagram**" respectively.

In this example you will create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram image.

1358598953

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd5026eb-b266-4e77-84e4-80d96f9f56b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598954</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e4490c1-171d-45cf-9578-7d18d05418f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598955</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="247ac079-b3f9-47c7-b124-207b4c96b153"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598955</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In Velocity, whenever you want to reference to a Java object you need to manually add the object to the Velocity template and name it. In Report Wizard, however, this process is done automatically. What you need to understand is how it is done as it will help you to call the modeling tool elements.</p><p>All elements in the modeling tool such as Class, Use Case, and Activity are the sub-classes of elements. During report generation, the report engine will get all the elements from a project within a selected scope that you have defined in Report Wizard, and then classify and add them to a list of the same type. The list will then be added to the Velocity Context with its type as its name. For instance, in a project where there are two types of Element, Class, and Use Case, all the elements that are identified as Classes will be kept on the same list and the list will be added to the Velocity Context and labeled as &quot;Class&quot; during report generation. This process also applies to the Use Case elements. To reference to a specific Class element, first you will have to make a reference to <strong>$Class</strong> (list of Class elements), and then iterate through the list to find that particular Class element by using the <strong>#foreach</strong>, <strong>#forrow</strong>, <strong>#forcol</strong>, or <strong>#forpage</strong> directive, followed by the <strong>#if</strong> directive to search for the name of the class.</p><p>This section will use part of the &quot;<em>Inventory Control System.mdzip</em>&quot; file in <em>&lt;install.root&gt;/samples/casestudies</em> as an example to show you how to reference elements.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Use Case and Actor" ac:alt="Use Case and Actor"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavb053991ffb02adecec8c94bef76403bd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How modeling tool adds its elements to velocity context" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">Use Case View.</h6><p>You need to know the elements inside the &quot;Use Case View&quot; package before using it, see figure above. To do this, follow the instructions in the previous section, <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating Your First Template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating your first template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>.</p><p>The &quot;Use Case View&quot; package contains three element types: (i) Actor, (ii) UseCase, and (iii) Diagram that you can call in a template by typing <strong>&quot;$Actor</strong>,&quot; <strong>&quot;$UseCase</strong>,&quot; and <strong>&quot;$Diagram</strong>&quot; respectively.</p><p>In this example you will create a report that will print the names of the Actors, UseCases, and diagram image.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5863349b-5f1c-4533-8b27-111ca2bfbf72"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1358598953</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8804a6f4-8982-4de8-b9e7-353a297adadb" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736378 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03191: (2026x) How to avoid triggering element recovery

- page_id: `272736378`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736378/2026x+How+to+avoid+triggering+element+recovery
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:11.057+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Project partitioning > (2026x) Maintaining decomposed model integrity > (2026x) Managing unresolved references
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:

1. Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.
2. Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).
3. Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.
4. Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions

To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , set the Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects property value to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

inspection

To select the relevant level of project composition inspection

1. Select Options > Projects to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog .
2. In the option group list, select the General > Validation .
3. In the Validation options list under the Composition Inspection , specify the Composition inspection option value:
  - Select Standard if you need the active validation to search for issues only in the project, but not in the projects it uses.
  - Select Advanced if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.
4. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following recommendations will help you avoid triggering element recovery in your project:</p><ol><li>Avoid unexpected modifications of shared elements, such as removing a shared element or moving the element to a non-shared location, etc.</li><li>Configure the modeling tool to prompt you for confirmation before proceeding with a potentially dangerous action, such as attempting to stop sharing a package, removing the content of a shared package, or moving elements from one module to another, when both modules are used in the read-write mode (shown in the following figure).</li><li>Select the relevant level of model integrity and project composition inspection.</li><li>Manage unresolved references as soon as possible, as they might produce other unresolved references. Remember that the fewer recovered elements your model has, the more valid it is.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="avoid_recovery.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How to avoid triggering element recovery" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Examples of messages prompting to confirm potentially unsafe actions</h6><p>To define a set of actions for modeling supervision to prevent recovered elements creation</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong> Composition Inspection</strong>, set the <strong>Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects </strong>property value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="detect_illegal_model_references_in_shared_projects.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How to avoid triggering element recovery" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb30e2f-6a0b-40da-97d1-2126e8b29b05"><ac:parameter ac:name="">inspection</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To select the relevant level of project composition inspection</p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Projects</strong> to open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Setting project options" /><ac:link-body><strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li style="text-align: left;">In the option group list, select the <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation </strong>options list under the <strong>Composition Inspection</strong>, specify the <strong>Composition inspection</strong> option value:<ul><li>Select <strong>Standard</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues <em>only</em> in the project, but not in the projects it uses.</li><li>Select <strong>Advanced</strong> if you need the active validation to search for issues in the project as well as in the projects it uses.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730503 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03192: (2026x) How to migrate

- page_id: `272730503`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730503/2026x+How+to+migrate
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:24.739+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before migrating your projects to the latest profile versions, we strongly advise you to make a backup first. After the migration is completed, you will not be able to reverse it.

For more information about migrating server projects, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migrating projects' space='CM'].

.

If you agree to migrate the project, an auto-check of the project usage is performed. You are warned if there are used projects of incompatible versions that, you need to convert. Two types of usages can interrupt migration:

- Cyclic usage
- Indirect usage

If the project contains cyclic usage, you will receivea warning with those usages listed. To continue with the migration, you need to eliminate the listed cyclic usages first. Repeat the migration.

For information about how to identify cyclic usages, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying suspicious usages' space='MCS']. The cyclic usages should be solved with the version you worked on before the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After cyclic usages are eliminated, and you continue with the migration, the check for indirect usages will be performed.

If the main project contains indirectly used projects, you will receive a warning message listing those projects. To continue with the migration, you need to migrate the indirectly used projects one by one **in the same order they are listed in the message**. Repeat the migration.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the cyclic and indirect usages are solved, you are informed about the directly used projects of incompatible versions. [Learn more >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Usedprojectsaremigrated,butnotupdated)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

More about migrating UAF and UPDM projects:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UAF projects migration manual' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) UPDM projects migration manual' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

After the migration is completed, you will not be able to reverse it.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="015ab40b-16ae-4330-a8ed-746e3ac71725"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about migrating server projects, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Migrating projects" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div><div>You are suggested to migrate each time you open a project created with the older version of UPDM / UAF with the modeling tool with the latest UAF version<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e05a89f-f8c4-440f-b94f-84f299446569"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="167a9859-8516-48fb-a158-b784804c3e6c">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><br /><br /></div><div><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9e05a89f-f8c4-440f-b94f-84f299446569"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="167a9859-8516-48fb-a158-b784804c3e6c"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="migration_question_new.png.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How to migrate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></div><div><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you agree to migrate the project, an auto-check of the project usage is performed. To continue with the migration, you need to eliminate the listed cyclic usages first. Repeat the migration.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4eeac4a9-7b3f-4916-b856-df9bd9a159ec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about how to identify cyclic usages, see <ac:link ac:anchor="Cyclic Usages"><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Identifying suspicious usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cyclic Usages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Repeat the migration.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="question2.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How to migrate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After the cyclic and indirect usages are solved, you are informed about the directly used projects of incompatible versions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Usedprojectsaremigrated,butnotupdated">Learn more &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="263" ac:width="807"><ri:attachment ri:filename="pic2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How to migrate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>  </p><div><p><br /></p></div><div><p class="auto-cursor-target">More about migrating UAF and UPDM projects:</p></div><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) UAF projects migration manual" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) UPDM projects migration manual" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734847 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03193: (2026x) HTML editor

- page_id: `272734847`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734847/2026x+HTML+editor
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:35.780+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

630043998

630044000

630043999

You can easily add or edit HTML text using the HTML editor. It also allows you to preserve the text format when copying formatted text.

To set the note, text box, or separator text as HTML

Do one of the following:

- Select a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Note' space='MED'] / [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Text Box' space=''] shape and click the HTML button when Switch To HTML Text appears on the lower-left corner of the shape. [ATTACHMENT filename='switch_text_to_html.png']

- Draw the note/text box, using the Note(HTML text) or Text Box(HTML text) buttons on the diagram palette.

Click the text area on the selected shape or start typing to open the HTML editor toolbar. The buttons on the HTML editor toolbar have the same functions as a normal editing toolbar.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar

You can write HTML text in various dialogs, but you must turn on the HTML mode first.

To turn on the HTML mode in a dialog

- Simply click to select the HTML check box. The following scenarios show the various ways of reaching the check box:
  - Element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] :
    1. Open an element's specification window.
    2. Click the Documentation/Comments tab.
    3. Check HTML. Click Close . See the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### HTML editor toolbar in Documentation/Hyperlinks tab of element Specification window

- In the Model Browser:
  1. Click the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MED'] .
  2. Click the **Documentation** tab.
  3. Click **HTML**, then click anywhere on the diagram pane. See the figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] HTML editor toolbar in Documentation panel on Model Browser
- Element Specification window:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. Click the Tags property group, when editing a tagged value.
  3. Click HTML . Then click Close . See the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='customer_user_is_added.png'] HTML editor toolbar in Tags tab of element Specification window when editing tag value
- In the **To Do** field:
  1. Open an element specification window.
  2. In the dialog, click the**To Do** field to edit it.
  3. Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to open the **To Do** text dialog.
  4. In the **To Do** text dialog, click **HTML**. Edit the text as you wish, then click **OK** and click **Close**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']HTML editor toolbar in To Do dialog

You can also use the HTML editor toolbar when editing a tagged value directly on the element shape.

To use the HTML editor toolbar for editing a tagged value

1. Click the HTML button on the element shape to switch to the HTML mode.
2. On the element shape, click the tagged value. The HTML editor toolbar opens for editing the tagged value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

630043997

**Related Pages:**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733093 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03194: (2026x) Humidifier example to Simulink model

- page_id: `272733093`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733093/2026x+Humidifier+example+to+Simulink+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:51.958+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Simulink export > (2026x) Simulating exported Simulink models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate the *Humidifier*example model in Simulink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Humidifier system sample' space='']Learn more about *Humidifier*sample model >>

You can find the *Humidifier*sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > Humidifier.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*Humidifier**.*

To simulate the *Humidifier System* model

1. Export the HumidifierSystem Block to Simulink file. How to >>
2. In the Simulink Export Options dialog select the following options:
  - **Format**: XML (.slx)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: S-Function version 2
  - Composite Signals : Bus Creators/Selectors
3. Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.
4. Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.
5. In the file directory select the location of your exported Humidifier Simulink files. They are displayed in the Current Folder panel. [ATTACHMENT filename='file_directory_simulink_files.png']
6. Double-click the file HumidifierSystem.slx . The model open in the Simulink window. [ATTACHMENT filename='simulink_window.png']
7. On the SIMULATION tab, click [ATTACHMENT filename='library_browser_button.png'] .
8. In the Simulink Library Browser dialog, select expand the Simulink group and select Sinks . See the figure below.
9. Drag and drop the Scope block onto the HumidifierSystem Simulink model. See the figure below.
10. Select signal line that connects the humidity-out port of the room block to the humidity-in port of the humidifier block and click on the Scope block. See the figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_scope_block.png']
11. On the SIMULATION tab, click the arrow on the PREPARE area and select the Model Settings . [ATTACHMENT filename='model_settings.png']
12. In the Configuration Parameters dialog, on the left side of the dialog select a Solver and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: - In the the Simulation time group change Start time to 0.0 , Stop time to 3600 . - In the Solver selection group select Type Variable-step or any other desirable solver that is suitable. - Click the arrow of the Solver details group and change the Max step size to 0.1 . [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_parameters_dialog.png']
13. Press Apply > OK .
14. Double-click the Scope block in the model. An empty black plot opens.
15. Click button. In the Configuration Properties: Scope dialog, select the Logging tab and clear the Limit data points to last box. [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_properties.png']
16. Press Apply > OK .
17. In the Scope plot toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and wait for the simulation results in the plot. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The model open in the <strong>Simulink</strong> window.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simulink_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>On the <strong>SIMULATION</strong> tab, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="71"><ri:attachment ri:filename="library_browser_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </li><li>In the <strong>Simulink Library Browser</strong> dialog, select expand the <strong>Simulink</strong> group and select <strong>Sinks</strong>. See the figure below.</li><li>Drag and drop the Scope block onto the <strong>HumidifierSystem </strong>Simulink model. See the figure below.</li><li>Select signal line that connects the<em> humidity-out</em> port of the room block to the<em> humidity-in</em> port of the<strong> </strong>humidifier block and click on the Scope block. See the figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_scope_block.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>On the <strong>SIMULATION</strong> tab, click the arrow on the <strong>PREPARE</strong> area and select the <strong>Model Settings</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Configuration Parameters</strong> dialog, on the left side of the dialog select a  <strong>Solver </strong>and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: <br /><strong>  </strong>-<strong> </strong>In the the <strong>Simulation time</strong> group change <strong>Start time </strong>to <strong>0.0</strong>, <strong>Stop time </strong>to <strong>3600</strong>.<br />  - In the <strong>Solver selection</strong> group select<strong> Type </strong><strong>Variable-step</strong> or any other desirable solver that is suitable.<br />  - Click the arrow of the <strong>Solver details</strong> group and change the <strong>Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.1</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="configuration_parameters_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Humidifier example to Simulink model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Press<strong> Apply</strong> &gt;<strong> OK</strong>.</li><li>Double-click the <strong>Scope</strong> block in the model.
