- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Enforce Ports Compatibility mode' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED']

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures on this page is the **Implied Connectors**sample model. Download [ATTACHMENT filename='Implied Connectors.mdzip'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The implied relation functionality allows you to analyze existing relations among parts in the Internal Block Diagrams. It let you know that parts are somehow related with each other without giving the detailed view of actual Connectors and ports. You can display implied relations between parts which are connected through ports/interfaces. By default, the implied relation is represented as a dashed line with a short summary in label of how many actual connectors are underneath. You can specify a different style of the implied relation by using its the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The implied relation functionality allows you to:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f42f4001-7636-4308-abe0-6ad6eb793d77">Display implied relations between <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <s> </s>through ports/interfaces.</li><li data-uuid="a901c316-744f-480e-97e6-0a52ddd14060">Display actual <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and ports from the implied relations level by level or all at once.</li><li data-uuid="832eec36-6b37-41a5-8d25-462c30370897">Find actual Connectors in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> directly from the implied relation.</li><li data-uuid="c268a5d7-6630-49ff-96c4-53d0d89eb655">Specify <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbol properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the implied relation.</li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="implied_relations_zoom_in.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Implied Connectors" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">On the left side: the implied relation shows that 18 connectors exist between TV and TV Tuner parts. On the right side: all 18 actual connectors and ports are shown by using the Semantic Zoom In, or Display Underlying Connectors command.</h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">How to work with implied Connectors read in the following pages:</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e1419f1-df1f-448f-92b2-d1448b471e51" /></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="2e5e9793-bce6-4f92-9732-0105143b9314"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="be6e77aa-bf49-44bc-bf27-16fdc39124f6"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Connecting parts through interface" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9b2b4b82-2e07-4227-a980-1edf64596d6a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Hiding direction arrow on port shape" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f20cc86a-3558-451a-a0ce-dfb7c0c73ced"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="54826777-7089-48f9-8dda-890c0f8eb755"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Autowire Parts" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="fbcad5c2-ec21-40df-8f04-1ae4dce6fab2"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Property path notation" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="54d17844-2dfd-44be-b329-d32fe5f30375"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Enforce Ports Compatibility mode" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="9d24dd5f-1d4f-4c4e-8564-fa8576ce0775"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li><li <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730183 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03215: (2026x) Import

- page_id: `272730183`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730183/2026x+Import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:37.984+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) XML schemas > (2026x) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Permission with the stereotype XSDimport. The permission client must be the schema class stereotypes*«XSDschema*» Component, supplier namespace Package *XSDnamespace*.

- namespace maps to the supplier name.
- annotation maps to the UML Attribute documentation.
- schemaLocation maps to the TaggedValue.

#### PANEL: import XML representation summary

import XML representation summary

```text
<import
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
namespace = anyURI
```

```text
schemaLocation = anyURI
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>	Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</import>
```

In the following example, the same namespace may be used both for real work, and in the course of defining schema components in terms of foreign components.

#### PANEL: The treatment of references as internal references to the names being defined in a schema document

The treatment of references as internal references to the names being defined in a schema document

```text
<schema xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns:html="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" targetNamespace="uri:mywork" xmlns:my="uri:mywork">
```

```text
<import namespace="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml"/>
```

```text
<annotation>
```

```text
<documentation>
```

```text
<html:p>[Some documentation for my schema]</html:p>		</documentation>
```

```text
</annotation>
```

```text
…
```

```text
<complexType name="myType">
```

```text
<sequence>
```

```text
<element ref="html:p" minOccurs="0"/>		</sequence>
```

```text
…
```

```text
</complexType>
```

```text
<element name="myElt" type="my:myType"/>
```

```text
</schema>
```

The treatment of references as ·**QNames**· implies that since the target namespace and the XML Schema namespace differ (with the exception of the schema for schemas), then without massive redeclaration of the default namespace either the internal references to the names being defined in a schema document or the schema declaration and definition elements themselves must be explicitly qualified. This example shows the first option, while most other examples in this specification showed the second.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']import UML model example.

#### PANEL: import XML code sample

import XML code sample

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com"›
```

```text
<xs:import namespace = "http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" schemaLocation = "http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml" />
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730427 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03217: (2026x) Import Maps migration from CSV Import Plugin

- page_id: `272730427`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730427/2026x+Import+Maps+migration+from+CSV+Import+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:34.716+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files
- labels: ['import-map-migration', 'show-excel-csv-import-migration-message', 'csv-import-map-migration', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page is for those who used the CSV Import Plugin to import data into the model. As of the 2021x version, this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by [Excel/CSV Sync](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files) (import to table) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space=''] (import to model) features.

All Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin can be reused by migrating them. The procedure outlined below shows how to import those maps:

To import data from the Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin

1. Open the project containing the Import Maps/Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin.
2. In the top left corner of the modeling tool, click the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map**** or Import Using Existing Map Group .
3. In the **CSV Import Map Migration** message, select **Yes**. To perform the migration of Import Maps or Map Groups later, make sure the **Do not ask again** check box is clear.
4. In the **Select Import Map** or **Select Map Group**dialog, select Import Map or Map Group.
5. Click OK . The **Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
6. Select the file, check mapping options. How to >>
7. Click the **Import** button. 
The data is imported. A Map Group is migrated only if all Import Maps belonging to that Map Group are migrated at the same time. If at least one is not migrated, the whole Map Group will not be migrated.

If the **CSV Import Map Migration** message does not show again when trying to migrate the Import Map or Map Group, you can enable this option in the **Project Options** dialog. For this, follow the procedure below.

To enable the **CSV Import Map Migration** message

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the search box of the Project Options dialog, type " mig ".
3. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, set the Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message option value to true to enable the functionality.
4. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page is for those who used the CSV Import Plugin to import data into the model. As of the 2021x version, this plugin is deprecated and import functionality is covered by <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files">Excel/CSV Sync</a> (import to table) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Import]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (import to model) features.</p><p>All Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin can be reused by migrating them. The procedure outlined below shows how to import those maps:</p><p><br /></p><p>To import data from the Import Maps or Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project containing the Import Maps/Map Groups created with the CSV Import Plugin.</li><li>In the top left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using Existing Map<strong> </strong></strong>or <strong>Import Using Existing Map Group</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message, select <strong>Yes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d34b7df2-dfb8-43dd-aa7f-895a7782a5b7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To perform the migration of Import Maps or Map Groups later, make sure the <strong>Do not ask again</strong> check box is clear.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Import Map</strong> or <strong>Select Map Group </strong>dialog, select Import Map or Map Group. </p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> opens. </li><li>Select the file, check mapping options. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea" rel="nofollow">How to &gt;&gt;</a></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Import</strong> button.<br />The data is imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="903c2d06-1978-4902-a2a1-54dc3404e22c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Map Group is migrated only if all Import Maps belonging to that Map Group are migrated at the same time. If at least one is not migrated, the whole Map Group will not be migrated.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p>If the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message does not show again when trying to migrate the Import Map or Map Group, you can enable this option in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. For this, follow the procedure below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable the <strong>CSV Import Map Migration</strong> message</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the search box of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, type &quot;<em>mig</em>&quot;.</li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, set the <strong>Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message </strong>option value to <em>true</em> to enable the functionality.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738826 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03218: (2026x) Import syntax

- page_id: `272738826`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738826/2026x+Import+syntax
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:50.018+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Import tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

715763235

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

715763237

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

715763236

To use the Import tool syntax

1. To declare Import tool in the template, type #import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool') in the document that you want to import its content to the report. The #import directive will load Import tool so that it can be accessed inside the template.
  - The first parameter 'import' is for identifying the alias of Import tool (in this case, 'import' , but it can be named anything).
  - The second parameter 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool' has to be copied as it is (do not rename it). Otherwise, Import tool will not be loaded.
2. To import any content from a child template, type the following in the document that you want to import its content to the report:

- $import.include('child.docx') to import a complete document at the current position of the template, and/or
- $import.includeSection('child.docx', 'Section A') to include only the text contained in the named section of the document.

****

**$import**

This part names the alias assigned to Import tool when declaring it to the template.

****

**$import.include(FileName)**

**FileName** in [$import.include(FileName)](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample1) specifies the location of a file in the file system. **FileName** can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. The **FileName** parameter can be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, **FileName** would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the **File** property.

****

**$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)**

**FileName** in [$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample2) functions the same as in the sub-section above. **FileName** can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. **SectionName** denotes a paragraph in the document named **FileName**, which is delimited by the **#sectionBegin(SectionName)** and **#sectionEnd** identifiers. The **SectionName** variable can also be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, **FileName** would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the **File** property.

#### INFO: Information

Information

You can also use **$attachedFile**in the parameter of **$import.include(FileName)** and **$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3207e9a8-b8f7-45e5-82d3-f4e215d37b1d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715763235</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33426169-fbae-4ee7-ae1b-ff54e4249423"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715763237</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d1e5f3f-4b1e-4e4c-a5da-a0bab2d38a7d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">715763236</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use the Import tool syntax</p><hr /><ol><li>To declare Import tool in the template, type <strong>#import ('import', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool')</strong> in the document that you want to import its content to the report. The <strong>#import</strong> directive will load Import tool so that it can be accessed inside the template.<br /><ul><li>The first parameter<strong> 'import'</strong> is for identifying the alias of Import tool (in this case, <strong>'import'</strong>, but it can be named anything).</li><li>The second parameter <strong>'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ImportTool'</strong> has to be copied as it is (do not rename it). Otherwise, Import tool will not be loaded.</li></ul></li><li>To import any content from a child template, type the following in the document that you want to import its content to the report:    </li></ol><ul><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>$import.include('child.docx')</strong> to import a complete document at the current position of the template, and/or</li><li style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>$import.includeSection('child.docx', 'Section A')</strong> to include only the text contained in the named section of the document.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import</strong></p><p>This part names the alias assigned to Import tool when declaring it to the template.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import.include(FileName)</strong></p><p><strong>FileName</strong> in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample1">$import.include(FileName)</a> specifies the location of a file in the file system. <strong>FileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. The <strong>FileName</strong> parameter can be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>FileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the <strong>File</strong> property.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</strong></p><p><strong>FileName</strong> in <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Import+usage#Importusage-UsageinExample2">$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</a> functions the same as in the sub-section above. <strong>FileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system. <strong>SectionName</strong> denotes a paragraph in the document named <strong>FileName</strong>, which is delimited by the <strong>#sectionBegin(SectionName)</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong> identifiers. The <strong>SectionName</strong> variable can also be set either statically or dynamically. If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>FileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File element in a project in the <strong>File</strong> property.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd51469a-e548-4f4f-83ef-ac7e3af3ab2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also use <strong>$attachedFile </strong>in the parameter of <strong>$import.include(FileName)</strong> and <strong>$import.includeSection(FileName, SectionName)</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735406 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03219: (2026x) Import tool

- page_id: `272735406`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735406/2026x+Import+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:21.372+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1175109811

1175109813

1175109812

Import tool is a module of functions that allows importing documents to be in generated reports. Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.

The Import tool allows you to:

- Parse and import RTF , DOCX, HTML , and text templates from any location in the file system
- Reuse the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard custom language' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard custom language' space=''] syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes #sectionBegin and #sectionEnd , introduced with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Wizard custom language' space=''] .

1175109810

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43a306aa-5cf3-487d-b5ab-7dc55be2e4b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109811</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5906bd29-43fe-4769-ba0e-a0555c875317"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109813</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9338f24e-8903-4191-82d2-3e615044c314"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109812</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Import tool is a module of functions that allows importing documents to be in generated reports. Import tool enables you to dynamically import whole or parts of documents into reports, giving you greater flexibility when generating reports that require dynamic resources. You can now include documents whose location is only known at the actual translation time.</p><p>The Import tool allows you to:</p><ul><li>Parse and import <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX, HTML</em>, and text templates from any location in the file system</li><li>Reuse the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionBegin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> and <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> syntax to import any part of a document. The Import tool uses the predefined syntaxes <strong>#sectionBegin</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong>, introduced with the <strong><ac:link ac:anchor="#includeSectionDirective"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Wizard custom language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[#includeSection Directive]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5c24317f-6306-485b-83a6-79ca6155bab8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1175109810</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="02c30e2f-93e2-4da8-9cd7-9b065513f43f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738823 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03220: (2026x) Import usage

- page_id: `272738823`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738823/2026x+Import+usage
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:39.944+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Import tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This section explains the examples of usage of the main and child templates. These examples allow you to create documents as the main template and reuse the content of child templates into it.

#### Preparatory Step

Define a child template that you will use as in Examples 1, 2, and 3. This child template contains the header line and named sections (**Section A** and **Section B**). Type the following lines in the document that you want to define the child template:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 1

This example shows how to statically include the complete child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include it, call the **$import.include('child.docx')** method. Note that there is no specified path. This path to the child template shows you that the main and child templates reside in the same directory. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include all of its content to the report:

```text

```

The output right below will include the entire text from the main template (right above) and from the child template minus the template directives. The included content will be then parsed and stripped of all velocity directives. The output is as follows:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 2

This example shows how to statically include a section of the child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. To include the section named **Section A** from the child template, call the **$import.includeSection('templates/child.rtf', 'Section A')** method. In this example, the main and child templates reside in different directories, which means that the child template will reside in a subdirectory called *templates*. This *templates* directory will thus reside in the same directory as the main template. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in **Section A** to the report:

```text

```

The output will be:

```text

```

#### Usage in Example 3

This example shows how to dynamically include the section of a child template as shown in the Preparatory Step. Before dynamically including the section of the child template named **Section B**, set a variable for the file location of the child template **[#set ($child = "***C:/ImportTool/child.rtf***")]** and another one for the section name to be included**[#set ($section = "Section B")]**. To include the **Section B** section from the child template, call the **$import.includeSection($child, $section)** method. In this example, the child's template location is provided by an absolute path. Note that dynamic values must not be specified in a pair of quote marks, otherwise the section will not be included. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include its content in **Section B** to the report:

```text

```

The output will be:

```text

```

The Import tool supports *DOCX, RTF, HTML, XML*, and*text* files only.

#### Usage in Example 4

This example shows how to include a document as an Attached File element in a project. First, you need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space=''] element in a project. The **File** property will then be used as a parameter to make a reference to the Attached File element. Type the following lines in the document that you want to include the content of the Attached File element to the report:

```text

```

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Including a document as an Attached File element in a project.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737635 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03221: (2026x) Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard

- page_id: `272737635`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737635/2026x+Importing+a+template+to+modeling+tool+using+Report+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:43:54.875+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Template Creation Tutorial > (2026x) Creating Your First Template
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1356305390

1356305392

1356305389

**On this page**

3

1356305392

#### Importing a template to the modeling tool

Once you have created a template, you will need to import it into the modeling tool by using Report Wizard.

To import a template into a modeling tool using Report Wizard

1. Click Tools > Report Wizard on the main menu.
2. Click the **New** button in the **Select a report template** wizard. The **New Template** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Opening the Report Wizard dialog and creating a new custom template by clicking the New button.
3. In the **New Template** dialog, type the name and description. Select a category, and then click**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Entering the new template name and description, and then selecting a template file in the New Template dialog.
4. Find the template file that you have created, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Select_button.png'] .
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Create_button.png'] to import the template.

To generate a report based on a template, select the template (in this example, **My template)**from the template list, and follow the steps as instructed by the wizard. See [Report Wizard User Guide](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Report+Wizard) for more information.

#### Glossary

- Report template Ready-made report configurations that you can use to quickly create a specific type of report in Report Wizard.

#### More information about working with templates and reports

For more information about working with templates and reports, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Template Management Pane' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Default templates' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Data Management Pane' space='']

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <strong>New Template</strong> dialog will open.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="newTemplate.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Opening the Report Wizard dialog and creating a new custom template by clicking the New button.</h6></li><li><p>In the <strong>New Template</strong> dialog, type the name and description. Select a category, and then click<strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="button-1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="New Template Dialog" ac:alt="New Template Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-template-a.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Entering the new template name and description, and then selecting a template file in the New Template dialog.</h6></li><li>Find the template file that you have created, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Select_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing a template to modeling tool using Report Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong> </strong>to import the template.</li></ol><p>To generate a report based on a template, select the template (in this example, <strong>My template) </strong>from the template list, and follow the steps as instructed by the wizard. See <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Report+Wizard">Report Wizard User Guide</a> for more information.</p><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Report template</strong><br />Ready-made report configurations that you can use to quickly create a specific type of report in Report Wizard.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with templates and reports</h2><p>For more information about working with templates and reports, visit the following pages:</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Template Management Pane" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Default templates" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Data Management Pane" /></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739502 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03222: (2026x) Importing Blocks and their properties

- page_id: `272739502`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739502/2026x+Importing+Blocks+and+their+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:35.870+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files > (2026x) Import examples
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets say, we need to import the engine structure including Blocks, their Part and Value Properties. Firstly, open an existing or create new project. In this case, we create new project. Next, do the following:

1. 
2. 
3. 
4. 

###### Importing Blocks

To import Block properties, the an *Engine* and *Engine Type* Blocks must be created in the model. In this case, we import those blocks. For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify the following options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Block as Element Type option value.
3. Specify the location of imported elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MED'] by selecting the Target Scope option value. In this case, the Structure Package is selected.
4. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 1.
5. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
6. Click Import . The Engine and Engine Type Blocks are created in the model. [ATTACHMENT filename='importing_engine_blocks.png']

###### Saving properties Import Maps

After the Blocks are imported, their all properties can be imported at once by creating a Map Group which consists of 3 Import Maps: *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties*. Follow the procedures below to save Import Maps.

The *Engine* Block is decomposed to *Engine Type* Part Property. Let's create an Import Map for the Part Property. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify the following options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with the structure. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Part Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select the Engine Block as the Target Scope option value.
4. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 2.
5. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
6. Click Save Map .
7. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
8. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Part . Click OK > Close . The Engine Part Import Map is saved. [ATTACHMENT filename='saving_engine_parts_import_map.png']

The *Engine* Block contains *serialNum* and *year*Value Properties. To save this Import Map, in the already opened [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Value Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select Name and Type properties as Properties to Map option value.
4. Select the Engine Block as the Target Scope option value.
5. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 3.
6. Create mapping between element property Name, Type and excel table cell Name, Type.
7. Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.
8. Click Save Map .
9. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
10. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Value Properties . Click OK > Close . The Engine Value Properties Import Map is saved.

Also, the E*ngine Type* Block contains *make*, *model*, *hp*, and *fuel***Value Properties. To save this Import Map, in the already opened [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with value properties. In this case, the Engine Excel file is selected.
2. Select Value Property as Element Type option value.
3. Select Name and Type properties as Properties to Map option value.
4. Select the Engine Type Block as the Target Scope option value.
5. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.
6. Create mapping between element property Name and excel table cell Name.
7. Select to search for references in entire model, to set types for Value Properties.
8. Click Save Map .
9. In the Save Import Map dialog, select a location wherein to save the Import Map. We highly recommend select the Excel/CSV Imports Package.
10. Click the Create button. In the opened Specification window, type the Import Map name - in this case - Engine Type Value Properties . Click OK > Close . The Engine Type Value Properties Import Map is saved.

###### Creating Map Group

After, the properties Import Maps are prepared, let's create the *Engine Properties Import* Map Group and add the following Import Maps: *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties.* For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Manage Map Groups.**In the **Manage Map Groups** dialog, do the following:

1. In the **Tree** tab, select the *Excel/CSV imports* Package as the owner for the new Map Group.
2. Click the **Create** button.
3. In the Specification window, type thename *Engine Properties Import* for the new Map Group and click **Close**. The newly created Map Group is selected as the Select Map Group for Editing option value.
4. Press Ctrl and in the Tree tab, select the *Engine Parts*, *Engine Value Properties*, *Engine Type Value Properties* Import Maps.
5. C lick [ATTACHMENT filename='add_import_map.png'] to add them to the *Engine Properties Import* Import Map.
6. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

###### Importing Map Group

Finally, import an *Engine Properties Import* Map Group. For this, in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map Group.**In the **Select Map Group** dialog, select the *Engine Properties Import* Map Group and click **OK**. All properties are created for the *Engine* and *Engine Type* Blocks at once.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739363 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03223: (2026x) Importing CA ERwin® data modeler projects

- page_id: `272739363`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739363/2026x+Importing+CA+ERwin%C2%AE+data+modeler+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:19.276+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Data-related modeling > (2026x) Entity-relationship (ER) modeling and diagrams
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1276634702

1276634704

1276634701

**On this page**

33

1276634703

The Cameo Data Modeler Plugin for MagicDraw provides import functionality for data models created using the CA ERwin® Data Modeler (henceforth will be referred as ERwin). ERwin is one of the leaders in the data modeling tools market.

Data models produced in ERwin have a two-layer structure, consisting of tightly synchronized logical and physical layers. The physical layer semantically corresponds to the SQL modeling / diagramming / generation functionality in MagicDraw. The logical layer corresponds to ER diagrams implemented by the Cameo Data Modeler Plugin.

The import functionality only imports logical layer data from ERwin into ER diagrams / data models in MagicDraw. The Cameo Data Modeler Plugin does not yet support importing physical layer data.

##### Importing data models

Cameo Data Modeler supports model files produced in ERwin version 7.x. It is recommended that you use the newest v7.3 since it has been heavily tested. Data models in ERwin must be saved in the *.xml format (choose the **XML Standard File**option in the **Save As**dialog).

To import an ERwin model

1. Start MagicDraw.
2. Click File > Import From > CA ERwin Data Modeler v7.x . The Open file dialog will open.
3. Select an ERwin model file (*.xml). A new MagicDraw project will be created and a logical model will be imported from the ERwin model file into that project.

After a successful import, you can proceed to edit or manage the model using MagicDraw features.

If you want to include the ER model as part of a larger project in MagicDraw, you can use either module linking functionality (click **File**> **Use Module**) to attach the ER model to your main project model or project import functionality (click **File**> **Import From**> **Another MagicDraw Project**) to transfer the contents of this ER model to your main project model.

If you want to update an imported and edited ER model (for example, you made changes to the ERwin model and want to import those changes into MagicDraw again), you can use the merge functionality (click **Tools**> **Project Merge**) to import the ERwin model into a new ER model and merge it with the model you have imported earlier.

##### Imported elements

Import mapping reviews and notes are in the following table.

| ERwin | Cameo Data Modeler | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Any element | Any Element | For each element, its name, definition, and notes are imported.Definitions are imported as MagicDraw documentation (special UML comments). Notes are imported as UML comments. |
| Entity | Entity |  |
| Attribute | Attribute | The Null / Not Null setting is imported as UML multiplicities [0..1] / [1].Attribute constraints and default value information is imported.Domain information is not imported because domains are not supported.Attribute type information is imported - the standard primitive types are mapped to the UML primitive types.Other types (those not found in the model) are created on the fly. |
| Key | Key Marking on Attributes | There is no separate standalone model element for a key in the Cameo Data Modeler ER diagrams. Instead, attributes belonging to a key are marked by applying a stereotype to them (PK, AK, or IE) as necessary. |
| Relationship | Association relationship | Simple relationships are mapped to UML associations.Verb phrases are mapped to role names.Cardinality and null / not null settings are mapped to UML multiplicities ([0..1], [1], [0..*], [1..*]).Referential integrity information is stored in a special stereotype / tag.Key information is not imported since the current ER diagrams do not support FK modeling. |
|  | Generalization relationship | ERwin relationships participating in the generalization tree are mapped to UML generalizations.Generalizations are joined into generalization trees.Complete / incomplete and overlapping / exclusive settings are imported / supported.Discriminating columns are imported / supported.Referential integrity information is stored in a special stereotype / tag.Verb phrase information is not imported. |
| Default Value | Instance Specification | A standalone UML instance specification is created to hold value definition. This instance specification is (or can be) then referenced from attributes, default value fields. |
| Domain | - | Domains are not yet supported in Cameo Data Modeler. |
| Validation Rule | Constraint | The Validation rule is stored as constraint body text. |
| Display | ER diagram | Due to geometric constraints and element size changes, the diagram layout will be slightly different.Paths between elements can be re-routed. |
| User Defined Properties Dictionary | Profile / Stereotypes / Tags | A custom UML profile is created for the user's property definitions. |
| User Defined Properties | Tag Values | A custom profile generated from the UDP dictionary is applied and user property information is stored in the tag values of the applied custom stereotypes. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Notes are imported as UML comments.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Entity</td><td>Entity</td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td>Attribute</td><td>Attribute</td><td><ul><li>The <strong>Null </strong>/ <strong>Not Null </strong>setting is imported as UML multiplicities [0..1] / [1].</li><li>Attribute constraints and default value information is imported.</li><li>Domain information is not imported because domains are not supported.</li><li>Attribute type information is imported - the standard primitive types are mapped to the UML primitive types.</li><li>Other types (those not found in the model) are created on the fly.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Key</td><td><p>Key Marking on Attributes</p></td><td><ul><li>There is no separate standalone model element for a key in the Cameo Data Modeler ER diagrams. Instead, attributes belonging to a key are marked by applying a stereotype to them (PK, AK, or IE) as necessary.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Relationship</td><td>Association relationship</td><td><ul><li><p>Simple relationships are mapped to UML associations.</p></li><li><p>Verb phrases are mapped to role names.</p></li><li><p>Cardinality and null / not null settings are mapped to UML multiplicities ([0..1], [1], [0..*], [1..*]).</p></li><li><p>Referential integrity information is stored in a special stereotype / tag.</p></li><li><p>Key information is not imported since the current ER diagrams do not support FK modeling.</p></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>Generalization relationship</td><td><ul class="_mce_tagged_br"><li>ERwin relationships participating in the generalization tree are mapped to UML generalizations.</li><li>Generalizations are joined into generalization trees.</li><li>Complete / incomplete and overlapping / exclusive settings are imported / supported.</li><li>Discriminating columns are imported / supported.</li><li>Referential integrity information is stored in a special stereotype / tag.</li><li>Verb phrase information is not imported.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Default Value</td><td><p>Instance Specification</p></td><td><ul><li>A standalone UML instance specification is created to hold value definition. This instance specification is (or can be) then referenced from attributes, default value fields.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Domain</td><td>-</td><td><ul><li><p>Domains are not yet supported in Cameo Data Modeler.</p></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Validation Rule</td><td>Constraint</td><td><ul><li>The Validation rule is stored as constraint body text.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Display</td><td>ER diagram</td><td><ul><li>Due to geometric constraints and element size changes, the diagram layout will be slightly different.</li><li>Paths between elements can be re-routed.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>User Defined Properties Dictionary</td><td><p>Profile / Stereotypes / Tags</p></td><td><ul><li>A custom UML profile is created for the user's property definitions.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Defined Properties</p></td><td>Tag Values</td><td><ul><li>A custom profile generated from the UDP dictionary is applied and user property information is stored in the tag values of the applied custom stereotypes.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729962 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03224: (2026x) Importing custom Jython libraries

- page_id: `272729962`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729962/2026x+Importing+custom+Jython+libraries
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:17.745+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Macro engine > (2026x) Supported scripting languages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is possible to import custom Python libraries.

To import and use a custom *.py* file

1. Create a Lib directory in the <tool_installation_directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib\engines\jython directory.
2. Add the custom .py file to the Lib directory.
3. To use the custom *.py* script, execute the following (e.g. *myscript.py* is the name of the *.py* file):

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is possible to import custom Python libraries.</p><p>To import and use a custom <em>.py</em> file</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <em>Lib</em> directory in the <em>&lt;tool_installation_directory&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib\engines\jython</em> directory.</li><li>Add the custom <em>.py</em> file to the <em>Lib</em> directory.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To use the custom <em>.py</em> script, execute the following (e.g. <em>myscript.py</em> is the name of the <em>.py</em> file):</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="114a7d2c-f334-462d-a92c-d86e3afd2bb9"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[import myscript
myscript.calculate(1, 5)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738955 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03225: (2026x) Importing custom tool to template

- page_id: `272738955`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738955/2026x+Importing+custom+tool+to+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:52.124+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard uses the import directive for importing a Custom Tool to a template.

An example of a directive:

```text

```

The import directive declares that the template uses the custom tool, names the tool that defines it, and specifies its tag prefix before the custom tool is used in a template page. You can use more than one import directive in a template, but the prefix defined in each template page must be unique.

#### Attributes

**name** (type:String) 
 The uniform or full qualified class name that locates the Tool class, for example, mytool.HelloTool

**prefix** (type:String) 
 The prefix that precedes the custom tool name such as a string in $hello.getHello(). Empty prefixes are not allowed. When developing or using custom tools, do not use tag prefixes such as bookmark, sorter, template, file, array, group, map, iterator, list, date, report, exporter, profiling, and project, as they are reserved by Report Wizard.

The code fragment indicated below declares a custom tool.

```text

```

The output from the above template is:

```text

```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard uses the import directive for importing a Custom Tool to a template.</p><p>An example of a directive:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd50b953-d474-470e-a26b-45c8eefbac29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('prefix', 'name')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The import directive declares that the template uses the custom tool, names the tool that defines it, and specifies its tag prefix before the custom tool is used in a template page. You can use more than one import directive in a template, but the prefix defined in each template page must be unique.</p><h2>Attributes</h2><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>name</strong> (type:String)<br />            The uniform or full qualified class name that locates the Tool class, for example, mytool.HelloTool</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><strong>prefix</strong> (type:String)<br />            The prefix that precedes the custom tool name such as a string in $hello.getHello(). Empty prefixes are not allowed. When developing or using custom tools, do              not use tag prefixes such as bookmark, sorter, template, file, array, group, map, iterator, list, date, report, exporter, profiling, and project, as they are reserved                by Report Wizard.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The code fragment indicated below declares a custom tool.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a6f2762-f7a5-40ea-9382-07cadf641971"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('hello', 'mytool.HelloTool')
Get Hello
$hello.getHello()
Get Hello Foo
$hello.getHello('foo')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output from the above template is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfe379ac-2d2c-4a4a-98ff-bd3a4fbdaf5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Get Hello
Hello World
Get Hello Foo
Hello foo]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730345 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 03226: (2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files

- page_id: `272730345`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730345/2026x+Importing+data+from+Excel+and+CSV+files
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-02-23T15:49:50.871+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can import data from Excel (*.xls*and*.xlsx*) or*CSV*(*.csv*and*.txt*) format files to your model. You are able to specify the import mapping options between file columns and element properties of the model. Additionally, you can save your maps, collect them in groups, manage those groups, and use an entire group instead of a single map for data import. Before importing data from Excel or CSV files to model, create new or open an existing project.

You can choose the following import commands:

- 
  - Import Using New Map - to import data from Excel or CSV file by specifying new mapping options and/or save an Import Map. >]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
  - Import Using Existing Map - to import data from an Excel or CSV file by selecting a previously saved map. >]]> [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
  - **Import Using Existing Map Group** - to import data from Excel or CSV file by selecting the map group that collects as many Import Maps as you added. 
>]]>
  - Manage Map Groups - to create, delete or change the order of the m ap groups. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Map Groups' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All Excel/CSV file import commands from the File menu.

##### Preparing Excel or CSV file to import

To import data from Excel or CSV files, the files must meet the following prerequisites:

- Heading is located in the first row.
- When creating relationships and connectors, the spreadsheet must contain the source and target columns. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are:
  - the client and supplier of the relationship.
  - the roles of the connector ends A and B.
- When creating connectors, the spreadsheet may optionally contain the owner column. The owner of the connector is determined in the following ways:
  - If the owner property is mapped, the connector is imported to the mapped owner.
  - If there is no owner property, the connector is imported to the element that owns the source and target elements indicated in the file.
    - If no such element exists in the model, the connector is imported to the owner of the element that is indicated as the source in the file.
  - If no qualified owner is found, the element selection dialog opens to select the owner for the connector.

Recommended prerequisites:

- Eliminate merged cells.
- Every column has a unique column name.
- Every row has the same number and name of columns.
- Every cell has the element name or qualified element name.

##### Importing data by using a new map

To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using a new map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure:
  - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. >]]>
  - the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
4. Select the file and specify mapping options. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea) You must select the file in the **Select Excel/CSV file** areaand specify the**Element Type**,**Properties to Map,**and **Target Scope** mapping option values to import data.
5. In the Mapping area, create mapping between the Excel/CSV file data and modeling tool properties. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
6. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
7. Click the Import button. The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.

##### Importing data by using an existing map

To import data from Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure:
  - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. >]]>
  - the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map .
4. In the **Select Import Map** dialog, select existing Import Map. If you cannot see any Import Maps, you need to save it first. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
5. Click OK . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog opens.
6. (Optional) Select the file, change mapping options. [How to >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelectExcel/CSVfilearea)
7. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to override the selected Import Map or create a new one. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Saving an Import Map' space='']>]]>
8. Click the Import button. The data from the Excel or CSV file is imported to the model according to the specified options.

##### Importing data by using an existing map group

To import data from an Excel or CSV file to model by using an existing map group

1. Create new or open an existing project.
2. Make sure:
  - the auto usages of Excel Import Plugin are removed. >]]>
  - the Excel or CSV file met the basic prerequisites. >]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c lick the File > Import From > Excel/CSV File > Import Using Existing Map Group .
4. In the **Select Map Group** dialog, choose the map group. If you cannot see any map group, you need to create it first. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Managing Map Groups' space='']>]]>
5. Click **OK**. 
The data from Excel or CSV files are imported to the model according to Import Maps and their order in the Map Group. The import of a map group can fail because of the following reasons:the Excel or CSV file is missing;the element type does not exist in the model;properties do not exist in the selected element;the target scope does not exist in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can import <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">data from Excel (</span><em>.xls </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and</span><em> .xlsx</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">) or </span><em>CSV</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> (</span><em>.csv</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> and </span><em>.txt</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">) format files to your model. The source and target columns contains the names/qualified names of elements respectively that are:<ul><li>the client and supplier of the relationship.</li><li>the roles of the connector ends A and B.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="d40daf27-0ba9-4e0c-944b-da9f3a9c25eb">When creating connectors, the spreadsheet may optionally contain the owner column. connector is imported to the element that owns the source and target elements indicated in the file.<ul><li>If no such element exists in the model, the connector is imported to the owner of the >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="4a91f3b6-baa4-430d-8023-70c28adba8a4"><span ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8b55090-d00b-494c-b107-4c36ef33b9f0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The import of a map group can fail because of the following reasons:</p><ul><li>the Excel or CSV file is model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731723 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03227: (2026x) Importing data from other UML tools and formats

- page_id: `272731723`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731723/2026x+Importing+data+from+other+UML+tools+and+formats
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:34.637+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: ['importing-uml', 'importing-data', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you need to open a model of a different format or created with another UML tool, you have to import it to the modeling tool instead of simply opening it. The modeling tool allows importing projects from the following file formats:

- Another .mdzip project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']
- UML 2.1/2.5 XMI File
- MagicDraw Native XML File
- MOF XMI File
- CA ERwin Data Modeler v7.x
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing ReqIF files' space='']
- OWL Ontology File
- Eclipse UML2 (v2.x, v3.x, v4.x, v5.x) XMI file
- XPDL File
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) FMU import' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Simulink import' space='']
- Modelica Library

- Enterprise Architect UML 2.1 XMI 2.1 File. Data can be imported from an Enterprise Architect 7.1, 7.5, or 8.0 file. Please note that Enterprise Architect does not export 100% standard UML 2.1 XMI, and this causes some data loss during the import.

- Use[Sodius](https://www.sodiuswillert.com/en/home)to import data from the following products:
  - Publisher for Rational Software Architect
  - Publisher for System Architect
  - Publisher for Rhapsody

- UML diagrams from Microsoft Visio using XMI files. Microsoft Visio 2007 and earlier requires extra add-ons for exporting UML model to XMI files.

To import an external model

1. Select File > **Import** From .
2. Select a file format you want to import a model from.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732359 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03228: (2026x) Importing Diagrams

- page_id: `272732359`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732359/2026x+Importing+Diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:30.081+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Importing data from other UML tools and formats > (2026x) Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Enterprise Architect Import allows you to import diagrams. The imported diagram information includes:

33

##### Geometry Properties

The geometry properties imported to a UML model are:

- Positions on a diagram (for shape elements)
- Width and height (for shape elements)
- Path break points (for link elements)

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Other display properties can override geometry information. For example, if an imported element width is shorter than the required width to display text on the element, the width will be adjusted automatically.

##### Color Properties

Color properties will be imported along with the diagrams to a modeling tool developed by NoMagic. Each color property has a different name in a modeling tool. The following table shows color properties mapping.

| EA | Modeling tool by NoMagic |
| --- | --- |
| Background | Fill color |
| Border color (for shape element) | Pen color |
| Font color | Text color |
| Line color (for link element) | Pen color |

##### Display Properties

Display properties in EA can be categorized into three groups: shape, link, and diagram. Only those properties corresponding to the modeling tool will be imported. For example, the **Show Diagram Details**property in EA will be imported as the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Table with diagram information' space='']**Show Diagram Info**property in a modeling tool developed by NoMagic.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739554 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03230: (2026x) Importing from ArchiMate

- page_id: `272739554`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739554/2026x+Importing+from+ArchiMate
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:31.066+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Introduction to ArchiMate

The ArchiMate language is an open and independent modeling language for Enterprise Architecture that enables Enterprise Architects to describe, analyze, and visualize the relationships among architecture domains in an unambiguous way. The ArchiMate supports an exchange format in XML, which allows model and diagram exchange between tools.

https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate

##### Importing ArchiMate model from open exchange file

You can import ArchiMate model to your UAF project. The supported file format for importing is ArchiMate *.xml*.

To import the model from ArchiMate .xml file

1. In the main menu, click File > Import From > ArchiMate XML File .
2. In the open dialog, choose the ArchiMate .xml file you want to import.
3. Click Open .
4. In the Select Owner dialog, choose or create the owner and click OK .

After the import is completed, the notification is displayed with the import results. The imported ArchiMate elements are converted to the UAF elements and added to your model. You can find the imported elements in the Containment tree. How ArchiMate model elements and relationships map to the UAF elements described in the following pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Element mapping' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Relationship mapping' space='']

Also, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Mapping issues' space=''] chapter to learn about known mapping issues.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The ArchiMate supports an exchange format in XML, which allows model and diagram exchange between tools.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e997923-3556-47be-aa3e-91a4a374d395"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information, see <a href="https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate">https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate</a>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Importing ArchiMate model from open exchange file</h3><p>You can import ArchiMate model to your UAF project. The supported file format for importing is ArchiMate <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.xml</em>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import the model from ArchiMate .xml file</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>ArchiMate XML File</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, choose the ArchiMate<em> .xml</em> file you want to import.</li><li>Click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog, choose or create the owner and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>After the import is completed, the notification is displayed with the import results. The imported ArchiMate elements are converted to the UAF elements and added to your model. You can find the imported elements in the Containment tree. How ArchiMate model elements and relationships map to the UAF elements described in the following pages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Element mapping" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Relationship mapping" /></ac:link></li></ul><p>Also, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Mapping issues" /></ac:link> chapter to learn about known mapping issues.</p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1044"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.2 horizontal-white-background copy 2.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing from ArchiMate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="625"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2022-11-3_14-59-16.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing from ArchiMate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p>
