<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739517 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03231: (2026x) Importing instances

- page_id: `272739517`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739517/2026x+Importing+instances
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:37.105+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files > (2026x) Import examples
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets say, we need to import the nested instances with slots in the already prepared Engine model structure where the *Engine* is decomposed to *Engine Type*, as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The *Engine* Block will be a classifier for three engine instances. Each engine instance will contain *serialNum*, *year*slots with defined values and nested *engine Type* instances*.* Each nested *engine Type* instances will contain four *fuel*, *hp*, *make* and *model* slots with defined values. In the following figure, the Excel spreadsheet is prepared for the instances import. [Learn more how to prepare the Excel or CSV file for import >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Next, begin the import: in the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**>**Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog) specify options:

1. Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with instances. In this case, the Vehicle model import Excel file is selected.
2. Select Instances as Import Type option value.
3. Select Engine Block as Instance Classifier option value.
4. Select Value Properties of the Engine Block and engine Type Part Property as the Nested Properties To Map option values. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_nested_properties.png']
5. Specify the location of imported elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MED'] by selecting the Target Scope option value. In this case, the Instances Package is selected.
6. Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.
7. Create mapping between model properties and excel table cells.
8. Click Import . [ATTACHMENT filename='instances_import_dialog_options.png']

As a result, three nested engine instances and their slots are created with defined values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Lets say, we need to import the nested instances with slots in the already prepared Engine model structure where the <em>Engine</em> is decomposed to <em>Engine Type</em>, as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="engine_decomposition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The <em>Engine</em> Block will be a classifier for three engine instances. Each engine instance will contain <em>serialNum</em>, <em>year </em>slots with defined values and nested <em>engine Type</em> instances<em>.</em> Each nested <em>engine Type</em> instances will contain four <em>fuel</em>, <em>hp</em>, <em>make</em> and <em>model</em> slots with defined values. In the following figure, the Excel spreadsheet is prepared for the instances import. <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Importing+data+from+Excel+or+CSV+files#ImportingdatafromExcelorCSVfiles-PreparingExcelorCSVfiletoimport">Learn more how to prepare the Excel or CSV file for import &gt;&gt;</a></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="engines_excel_spreadsheet.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Next, begin the import: i<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, c</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">lick the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">File</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Import From</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. In t</span>he <a style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a> specify options:</p><ol><li>Select the Excel file which contains the spreadsheet with instances. In this case, the <em>Vehicle model import</em> Excel file is selected.</li><li>Select Instances as <strong>Import Type</strong> option value.</li><li>Select <em>Engine</em> Block as <strong>Instance Classifier</strong> option value.</li><li>Select Value Properties of the <em>Engine</em> Block and <em>engine Type</em> Part Property as the <strong>Nested Properties To Map</strong> option values.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_nested_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Specify the location of imported elements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link> by selecting the<strong> Target Scope</strong> option value. In this case, the <em>Instances </em>Package is selected.</li><li>Specify the Excel file sheet in which the table is prepared to import. In this case, the table is in the Sheet 4.</li><li>Create mapping between model properties and excel table cells.</li><li>Click <strong>Import</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instances_import_dialog_options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>As a result, three nested engine instances and their slots are created with defined values. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="imported_instances.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732623 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03232: (2026x) Importing models

- page_id: `272732623`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732623/2026x+Importing+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:58.715+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Project partitioning > (2026x) Composing model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Importing models from used projects

If you no longer need to have the set of elements in a separate project, you can import these elements into the main project.

To import a used project into the main project

1. In the Model Browser, select the package of this used project and open the shortcut menu .
2. Select Project Usages > Import .

All the elements of this used project are copied into the main project, and links to this used project are removed from the main project. The unlinked project and its elements continue to exist in the file system or server after the import.

- If the used project you want to import uses other projects, after the import these projects become directly used in the main project.
- The Standard/System Profiles cannot be imported according to our tool policy.

##### Importing models from other projects

Another way of composing your model, is to import a model from another project.

To import a model from another project

1. In the main menu, select File > Import From > Another Project .
2. Select a project you want to import.
3. Click **Import**. All elements from another project will be imported. If the model that you are importing also uses projects, these projects will be reused directly in your project after the model import.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Importing models from used projects</h3><p>If you no longer need to have the set of elements in a separate project, you can import these elements into the main project.</p><p> </p><p>To import a used project into the main project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, select the package of this used project and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cd108d97-84da-4865-8ccf-44b486b6e0c6">open the shortcut menu</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Import</strong>.</li></ol><p>All the elements of this used project are copied into the main project, and links to this used project are removed from the main project. The unlinked project and its elements continue to exist in the file system or server after the import.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4790239a-deb3-4f59-ba25-1ce2e1cdf086"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the used project you want to import uses other projects, after the import these projects become directly used in the main project.</li><li>The Standard/System Profiles cannot be imported according to our tool policy.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Importing models from other projects</h3><p>Another way of composing your model, is to import a model from another project.</p><p> </p><p>To import a model from another project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Another Project</strong>.</li><li>Select a project you want to import.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Import</strong>. All elements from another project will be imported.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8f81a64-5f1e-4400-a66f-28ac9ff8134f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the model that you are importing also uses projects, these projects will be reused directly in your project after the model import.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739485 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03233: (2026x) Importing nested Requirements

- page_id: `272739485`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739485/2026x+Importing+nested+Requirements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:35.248+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files > (2026x) Import examples
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Lets' say we need to import nested structure of requirements from the Excel file. For this, you must define the Owner property in the Excel spreadsheet with the name or id of each requirement. As example, see the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Requirements in the Excel spreadsheet with defined Owner property.

To import nested Requirements

1. Open the project in the modeling tool.
2. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Sync with Excel or CSV files' space=''] functionality.
3. In the [**Excel/CSV Import** dialog](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog), select the Excel file. Make sure the Excel file contains Owner column. See the example above.
4. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the Requirement type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - select Name, Text, id and Owner properties you want to fill in with data from Excel file.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported Requirements. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
5. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the column on the right to the property on the left. [Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
6. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
7. Click the Import button. The Requirements are imported. If any of the imported Requirement already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

For this, you must define the Owner property in the Excel spreadsheet with the name or id of each requirement. As example, see the following figure. </span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_req_excel_spreadsheet.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing nested Requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Requirements in the Excel spreadsheet with defined Owner property.</h6><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To import nested Requirements</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Open the project in the modeling tool.</span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="550f0d57-9ef5-4e47-be80-3ea69ef5a3be"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For element import via tables, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exce/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> functionality.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a>, select the Excel file. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d12b70c5-f63a-40ff-b71b-6c5c7968ddbf"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Make sure the Excel file contains Owner column. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732037 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03234: (2026x) Importing projects from Ecore files

- page_id: `272732037`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732037/2026x+Importing+projects+from+Ecore+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:10.846+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Ecore support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. 
 
To import an Ecore file

1. In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.
2. Use the Importing projects from MOF files procedure.

This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no possibility to import Ecore files directly. Ecore files should be imported using the EMOF import feature. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> To import an Ecore file</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Eclipse environment, open the *.ecore file and save it as *.emof.</li><li>Use the <a href="http://documentation.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Importing+projects+from+MOF+files">Importing projects from MOF files</a> procedure.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32276341-4f32-43be-be16-e7105068a796"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This way of importing Ecore files may cause some Ecore-specific model details that don't exist in EMOF to be lost.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732065 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03235: (2026x) Importing projects from MOF files

- page_id: `272732065`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732065/2026x+Importing+projects+from+MOF+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:14.899+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) MOF support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To import a project from a MOF file

1. From the main menu, select File > Import From > MOF XMI File .
2. In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click Open . The file is imported as a separate project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To import a project from a MOF file</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF XMI File</strong>.</li><li>In the dialog, select the EMOF or CMOF file you want to import, and click <strong>Open</strong>. The file is imported as a separate project.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739497 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03236: (2026x) Importing relationships

- page_id: `272739497`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739497/2026x+Importing+relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:36.532+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files > (2026x) Import examples
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Main conditions

You can create relationships between existing model elements by importing them from Excel/CSV files. For thisthe following conditions must be met:

- 
  - The source/client and target/supplierelements you want to create relationships between must exist in the project.
  - The Excel/CSV source and target columns must be mapped with the Client/Source and Supplier/Target properties in the mapping area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space=''].
  - The source and target elements must be legal in terms of Metamodel, DSL and code rules, e.g., the satisfy relationship client must be a requirement.

##### Preparing the Excel file to import relationships

When creating relationships in the model by importing them, you need to make sure the Excel file contains the source and target columns. The source column contains the names of elements that are the client of the relationship. The target column contains the names of the elements that are the suppliers of the relationship. In the example below, the Source column contains the names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Excel file content prepared with element names for the relationships import.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing Blocks and their properties' space='']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing nested Requirements' space='']>]]>

If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, the elements are not under the same target scope, or you want to increase the import speed, use the following:

- Select to search elements and references in the Target Scope Only in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']**Excel/CSV Import** dialog when importing data.
- Define your source and target columns in the Excel file with qualified names of elements. In the example below, the Source column contains the qualified names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the qualified names of Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The The Excel file content prepared with qualified element names for the relationships import.

##### Relationships import procedure

To import relationships

1. Prepare the Excel file for relationships import. >]]>
2. Open the project in the modeling tool. Make sure the elements you want to create relationships between exist in the project. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing Blocks and their properties' space='']>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing nested Requirements' space='']>]]>
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the **File** > **Import From** > **Excel/CSV File > Import Using New Map**. For element import via tables, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Sync with Excel or CSV files' space=''] functionality.
4. In the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog , select the Excel file. How to >>
5. Specify the mapping options:
  - Import Type - select the Element value.
  - Element Type - select the relationship type you want to create.
  - Properties to Map - the Client and Supplier properties are selected by default.
  - **Target Scope**- select the Package you want to contain imported relationships. If the Owner property is selected as the **Properties to Map** option value, the data is imported according the owner. You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. [Learn more about mapping****options >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea)
6. Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the Source column on the right to the Client property on the left. Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other columns/properties.[Learn more about mapping area >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea)
7. (Optional) Click the Save Map button to save your map options as the Import Map. Learn more how to save Import Map >>
8. Click the Import button. The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify **Identification Property** value or **I****dentify Relationship (End) Source by** and **Identify Relationship (End) Target by** values. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog' space='']>]]>

The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:

- The relationship end is empty in the data file.
- The source or the target elements do not exist in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.

In the following example, you can see specified import options in the **Excel/CSV Import** dialog before the import. After the import the Satisfy relationships are created between the defined Source and Target elements: Blocks and Requirements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The import of Satisfy relationship.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space='']
- Saving an Import Map
- Managing Map Groups
- Excel and CSV Import dialog
- [Sync with Excel or CSV files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files)

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3> Main conditions</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can create relationships between existing model elements by importing them from Excel/CSV files. For this </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the following conditions must be met:</span></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="c7da3f44-6383-4771-8cb9-42577aae5f46"><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The source/client and target/supplier </span>elements you want to create relationships between must exist in the project.</span><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Excel/CSV source and target columns must be mapped with the Client/Source and Supplier/Target properties in the mapping area of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Import dialog]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The source and target elements must be legal in terms of Metamodel, DSL and code rules, e.g., the satisfy relationship client must be a requirement.</span></li></ul></li></ul><h3>Preparing the Excel file to import relationships</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When creating relationships in the model by importing them, you need to make sure the Excel file contains the source and target columns. The source column contains the names of elements that are the client of the relationship. The t</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">arget column contains the names of the elements that are the suppliers of the relationship.  In the example below, the Source column contains the names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the names of Requirements.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_file_content_element_names.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Excel file content prepared with element names for the relationships import.</span></h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8537137-5dd5-4660-9a7c-210ae5426b85"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing Blocks and their properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Blocks and their properties >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing nested Requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Requirements >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br />If you have to deal with a large a scope of information, the elements are not under the same target scope, or you want to increase the import speed, use the following:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="04eab9e3-dc31-478b-b5f7-fb99c4cba6ac">Select to search elements and references in the <strong>Target Scope Only</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> when importing data.</li><li data-uuid="25e37199-d26a-4e1d-9dbb-25cab74fc90c">Define your source and target columns in the Excel file with qualified names of elements. In the example below, the Source column contains the qualified names of the Blocks and the Target column contains the qualified names of Requirements.</li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_file_content_qualified_names.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The The Excel file content prepared with qualified element names for the relationships import.</span></h6><h3>Relationships import procedure</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To import relationships</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="5954eadc-f0be-40ae-b290-34d98b51a97e"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Prepare the Excel file for relationships import. <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to prepare the Excel for the relationships import >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></li><li data-uuid="05e60138-04b5-4dea-9a69-0a6cea78ec66"><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Open the project in the modeling tool. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c49ee855-2c1f-4fc8-b6b9-d286c8518d83"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Make sure the elements you want to create relationships between exist in the project.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing Blocks and their properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Blocks and their properties >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing nested Requirements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to import Requirements >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="47833492-9c5f-4e96-8d2a-d50dcdd54cb7"><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>Excel/CSV File &gt; Import Using New Map</strong>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="550f0d57-9ef5-4e47-be80-3ea69ef5a3be"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For element import via tables, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Exce/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> functionality.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="d36cc076-ca66-40fc-b07e-18b0e80d2568">In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog"><strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog</a>, select the Excel file. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-SelecttheExcel/CSVfilearea">How to &gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="38c135c6-a122-4bfd-9435-9c0c5bf3b160"><span>Specify the mapping options:<br /></span><ul><li><strong>Import Type </strong>- select the Element value.</li><li><strong>Element Type </strong>- select the relationship type you want to create.</li><li><strong>Properties to Map</strong> - the Client and Supplier properties are selected by default.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Target Scope </strong>- select the Package you want to contain imported relationships. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If the Owner property is selected as the <strong>Properties to Map</strong> option value, the data is imported according the owner.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="980f374f-bd9c-4ec1-aa28-1eccd62e0348"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> You can specify more mapping options according to your needs. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-MappingOptionsarea">Learn more about mapping<strong> </strong>options &gt;&gt;</a></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="7886e03b-1407-464c-9a0e-64724d7bd277"><p>Create the mapping between Excel file columns and element properties by dragging the Source column on the right to the Client property on the left. Do the same with the Target and Supplier or other columns/properties.<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog#ExcelandCSVImportdialog-Mappingarea"> Learn more about mapping area &gt;&gt;</a></p></li><li data-uuid="c5eb9fbe-6204-48ff-a06f-5e1796c54566">(Optional) Click the<strong> Save Map</strong> button to save your map options as the Import Map. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Saving+an+Import+Map">Learn more how to save Import Map &gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="a8ed08f1-e400-4986-ae34-ab8fcf8ca10d">Click the <strong>Import</strong><span> button.<br /></span>The relationships are imported. If any of the imported relationships already exist in the model t<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">hey are updated. To avoid duplication of elements specify <strong>Identification Property</strong> value</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> or <strong>I</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>dentify Relationship (End) Source by</strong> and <strong>Identify Relationship (End) Target by</strong> values. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Excel and CSV Import dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about mapping options in the Excel/CSV Import dialog>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11b36b6e-52a8-475f-92cc-71a4f243e323"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The relationship import can fail due to the following reasons:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="6010d621-7113-41f8-908b-bf33cceb6169"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The relationship end is empty in the data file.</span></li><li data-uuid="5c61116c-9957-4e70-8a90-6342160076b6"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The source or the target elements do not exist in the model or the mechanism didn't find any.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the following example, you can see specified import options in the <strong>Excel/CSV Import</strong> dialog before the import. After the import the Satisfy relationships are created between the defined Source and Target elements: Blocks and Requirements. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="result_of_relationships_import.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing relationships" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The import of Satisfy relationship.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="f11b0090-f4eb-4ef5-a455-f9c5f314c926"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing data from Excel and CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing data from Excel or CSV files]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="5e0575a7-8ec4-4394-8266-e065ee41472c"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Saving+an+Import+Map" class="current">Saving an Import Map</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="1caecd53-5725-4691-bcb5-d963df23ba93"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Managing+Map+Groups" class="current">Managing Map Groups</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="76b2d164-7b9c-4cdd-a8d9-6871a1773bbb"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog" class="current">Excel and CSV Import dialog</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="72458b2c-df78-4cb9-ae30-4925b52e3bf6"><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Sync+with+Excel+or+CSV+files" class="current">Sync with Excel or CSV files</a></p></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729928 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03237: (2026x) Importing ReqIF files

- page_id: `272729928`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729928/2026x+Importing+ReqIF+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:08.278+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management > (2026x) Importing requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

818054109

INLINE

##### Importing ReqIF files

You can import ReqIF files from:

- IBM DOORS 9.4, 9.5, and 9.6
- IBM DOORS Next Generation
- PTC Integrity
- Polarion REQUIREMENTS
- Siemens Teamcenter
- Dassault Systemes Reqtify

You can import any ReqIF file – with or without specifications.

To import a ReqIF requirement file

1. Open a project wherein you want to import requirements.
2. From the File menu, select Import From > Requirement Interchange Format (ReqIF) File .
3. In the opened **Load** dialog, browse for your ReqIF file and click **Open**.
4. The ReqIF file is imported.

If Requirements in the ReqIF file have ID prefixes, they are set automatically after import only for imported Requirements. Manage ID prefixes when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Requirements numbering' space=''].

800700

When the ReqIF file is imported, a notification message appears, stating the quantity of imported or updated elements. If you import a ReqIF file without specification, the message doesn't contain the line that indicates new or updated specifications. You also see no option **Show imported specification table(s)**. 
 
If you imported a ReqIF file with the specification, you can view the list of imported elements. To do that, click the **Show imported specification table(s)** link in the opened notification message. The list is displayed in a generic table format.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change import options, click the **You can change import and mapping options here** link in the opened notification message.

The notification message is displayed for a fixed duration, but you can find all project notification messages in the Notification Window. To open the Notification Window, click **Window** > **Notification Window**, or press Ctrl + M.

Imported requirements are presented in the **Containment** tree. The requirement specification is represented as a model package and requirements are represented as model elements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there are images or files attached to the ReqIF file, they are marked with the «AttachedFile» stereotype and placed in a separate package as model elements after the import.

All images in the *BMP* file format are automatically converted to the *PNG*format.

If there are any deleted elements in the source file, they are automatically marked with the «deprecated» stereotype once imported onto the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing requirements importing options

Changing requirements importing options is beneficial if you have a custom requirement type defined, or you are importing another type of data instead of requirements, for example, test cases.

A requirement type is limited to class and class extended types.

You can change the following import options, among others:

- Specify an element type to create for the objects you are importing;
- Specify a relationship type to create for the relationships you are importing;
- Specify a path to the resource location;
- Choose whether to import all properties into the model or not and form a list of ignored and not imported properties;
- Choose which properties that, when modified, will not change the elements' status to 'updated';
- For a full list, see the 'To change ReqIF file importing options' procedure or the ReqIF section in the Project Options table on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Setting project options' space=''] table.

To change ReqIF file importing options

We recommend changing importing options before requirements are imported and no changes are made in the project, because after you have changed importing options, you need to remove the requirements you have imported and re-import it.

1. Click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the options group list, select General , then scroll down to the ReqIF options group.
3. Click a desired option specification cell to change it's value.
4. Click OK after you are finished.

##### Updating requirements

It is common to modify requirements after importing them. Importing requirements in a ReqIF format allows updating the requirements present in the model without any data loss – all changes are identified. 
 
To update ReqIF file by importing a new file

1. Open a project wherein you want to update requirements.
2. Import a ReqIF file with updated requirements.

To update requirements from the project

1. Open the project containing imported requirements.
2. In the Containment tree, select a requirement specification package and open it's shortcut menu.
3. Click Tools > ReqIF > Update From Requirements Interchange Format (ReqIF) File .
4. In the opened Load dialog, browse for your ReqIF file and click Open . The ReqIF file is updated.

After requirements have been updated, you get a notification message informing about an update status. If you update a ReqIF file without specification, the message doesn't contain the line that indicates new or updated specifications. You also see no option **Show imported specification table(s)**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
In the change log table, you can see the detailed list of updated elements. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
During update, no elements are removed, only new elements are added and statuses of all elements are updated. Elements not present in an updated ReqIF file are marked as obsolete.

##### Managing obsolete requirement elements

In the model, obsolete elements are marked with an exclamation mark in a yellow triangle. A «deprecated» stereotype is applied to such requirement elements automatically. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
**Removing Obsolete Elements** 
In the project, you can choose to remove only the selected obsolete element or all obsolete elements at a time. To remove one selected obsolete element, use the that is described in the following section. 
 
To remove all obsolete elements

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the requirements specification package.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > ReqIF > Remove Obsolete Elements .

****

**Searching for obsolete elements**

#validationTo search for obsolete elements

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the requirements specification package.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Validation > Obsolete Elements and one of the following:

- 
  - Remove Obsolete Elements – removes the selected obsolete elements from a project
  - Ignore – ignores obsolescence from the element and leaves it in the model
  - Select in Validation Results – opens the Active Validation Results panel wherein you can perform appropriate validation actions.

/></ac:link> table.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To change ReqIF file importing options</p><hr /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="083995ad-0d2d-4366-af56-981ad15fb47c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>We recommend changing importing options before requirements are imported and no changes are made in the project, because after you have changed importing options, you need to remove the requirements you have imported and re-import it.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ol><li>Click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the options group list, select <strong>General</strong>, then scroll down to the <strong>ReqIF</strong> options group.</li><li>Click a desired option specification cell to change it's value.<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> after you are finished.</li></ol><h3>Updating requirements</h3><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />It is common to modify requirements after importing them. Importing requirements in a ReqIF format allows updating the requirements present in the model without any data loss – all changes are identified. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To update ReqIF file by importing a new file</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a project wherein you want to update requirements.</li><li>Import a ReqIF file with updated requirements.</li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To update requirements from the project</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project containing imported requirements.</li><li>In the <strong>Containment</strong> tree, select a requirement specification package and open it's shortcut menu.</li><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>ReqIF</strong> &gt; <strong>Update From Requirements Interchange Format (ReqIF) File</strong>.</li><li>In the opened <strong>Load</strong> dialog, browse for your ReqIF file and click <strong>Open</strong>. The ReqIF file is updated.</li></ol><p>After requirements have been updated, you get a notification message informing about an update status. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> If you update a ReqIF file without specification, the message doesn't contain the line that indicates new or updated specifications. You also see no option <strong>Show imported specification table(s)</strong>.</span> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:title="Notification window about updated requirements" ac:alt="Notification window about updated requirements" ac:height="221" ac:width="398"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddaveb8528a09ea20172fba7cd204846dbe6.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />In the change log table, you can see the detailed list of updated elements. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:title="Change log table" ac:alt="Change log table" ac:height="166" ac:width="443"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddavc67674f1d31deffe5f80226c2f459353.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />During update, no elements are removed, only new elements are added and statuses of all elements are updated. Elements not present in an updated ReqIF file are marked as obsolete.</p><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Managing obsolete requirement elements</span></h3><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />In the model, obsolete elements are marked with an exclamation mark in a yellow triangle. A «deprecated» stereotype is applied to such requirement elements automatically.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:title="Obsolete elements in Containment tree" ac:alt="Obsolete elements in Containment tree" ac:height="65" ac:width="393"><ri:attachment ri:filename="worddav0c7c2dd9585346d2c1f4826df65557fa.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Importing requirements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Removing Obsolete Elements</strong><br class="atl-forced-newline" />In the project, you can choose to remove only the selected obsolete element or all obsolete elements at a time. To remove one selected obsolete element, use the <ac:link ac:anchor="validation"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[validating procedure]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that is described in the following section. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To remove all obsolete elements</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Containment</strong> tree, right-click the requirements specification package.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>ReqIF</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove Obsolete Elements</strong>. </li></ol><p><strong> </strong></p><p><strong>Searching for obsolete elements</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="54bb4677-4d0f-43f2-aab5-3db2a64661ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#validation</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To search for obsolete elements</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Containment</strong> tree, right-click the requirements specification package.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <strong>Obsolete Elements</strong> and one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Remove Obsolete Elements</strong> – removes the selected obsolete elements from a project<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li><strong>Ignore</strong> – ignores obsolescence from the element and leaves it in the model<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> <br /></span></li><li><strong>Select </strong><strong>in</strong><strong> Validation </strong><strong>Results</strong> – opens the <strong>Active</strong><strong> Validation</strong><strong> Results</strong> panel wherein you can perform appropriate validation actions.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1978d6d7-7e29-40a0-aa0b-071b8dc33c03"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">818054108</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="cc68c6e1-2b04-4b80-bf7d-409c8f5de624" /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739195 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03238: (2026x) Importing ReqIF files from the command-line interface

- page_id: `272739195`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739195/2026x+Importing+ReqIF+files+from+the+command-line+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:03.147+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management > (2026x) Importing requirements > (2026x) Importing ReqIF files
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Importing from the command-line interface is useful when you want to import files automatically, e.g., once a day at midnight.

To import a ReqIF file from the command-line interface

1. Open the Command Prompt window
2. Go to the <model editor installationdirectory>/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements directory containing the *reqifimport.exe* file.
3. At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the values of the required arguments: reqifimport project_descriptor=<project URL> reqif_file=<path to ReqIF file> owner=<element hyperlink or qualified name> Where:
  - **project_descriptor** is the URL of a project. How to get a project descriptor?Start your modeling tool and open the required project.In the main menu, go to **File** > **Project Properties** and select the **General** tab.Find the project descriptor specified in the **Project Descriptor** box at the bottom of the **Project Properties** dialog and copy it to the clipboard.
  - **reqif_file** is the path to the ReqIF file you want to import. If there is a space in a path, surround the argument and its value with escaped quote(\").
  - **owner** is the hyperlink or qualified name of the owner element. If newly imported elements are not contained by the Specification element, they are placed under the specified owner. Note that the **owner** argument is optional. How to get the hyperlink of an element?Start your modeling tool and open the required project.In the Containment tree, right-click an element and select **Copy Element Hyperlink**.The hyperlink of the element is now copied to the clipboard.
4. Press **Enter**.

Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.

##### Using a properties file

To make importing from the command-line interface quicker you can specify arguments in a properties file, which can be easily edited or reused.

To import a ReqIF file from the command-line interface using a properties file

1. Create a properties file and specify the values of the following arguments: See the example of a properties file below. text1trueProject Properties > General.
#
reqif_file=C:/Users/johnsmith/Documents/project_requirements.reqif
#
# Specify the path to the ReqIF file.
# NOTE: If there is a space in a path, surround the argument and its value with escaped quote (\").
#
owner=mdel://eee_1045467100313_135436_1
#
# Specify the hyperlink or qualified name of the owner element. 
# To get the hyperlink of an element, open a project, right-click the element in the Containment tree and select "Copy Element Hyperlink".
# NOTE: This argument is optional.
#]]> When creating a properties file on Windows OS, use only / or \\ as a path separator, otherwise the command will fail.
  - project_descriptor
  - reqif_file
  - owner
2. Open the Command Prompt window.
3. Go to the <model editor installationdirectory>/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements directory containing the *reqifimport.exe* file.
4. At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the value of the **properties_file** argument. reqifimport properties_file=<path to properties file> Where **properties_file** is the path to the properties file to be used.
5. Press **Enter**.

Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

reqif_file=&lt;path to ReqIF file&gt; owner=&lt;element hyperlink or qualified name&gt;</pre><p>Where:</p><ul><li><p><strong>project_descriptor</strong> is the URL of a project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc06a5ad-5a37-4ffe-bbdf-b3d946ace80e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">How to get a project descriptor?</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Start your modeling tool and open the required project.</li><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>File</strong> &gt; owner=mdel://eee_1045467100313_135436_1
#
# Specify the hyperlink or qualified name of the owner element. 
# To get the hyperlink of an element, open a project, right-click the element in the Containment tree and select "Copy Element Hyperlink".
# NOTE: This argument is optional.
#]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a747b836-052e-4bff-a358-e5edaed77878"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>When creating a properties file on Windows OS, use only / or \\ as a path separator, otherwise the command will fail.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Open the Command Prompt window.</li><li>Go to the <em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">&lt;model editor installa</span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">tion </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">directory&gt;/plugins/com.nomagic.requirements</span></em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> directory containing the <em>reqifimport.exe</em> file.</span></li><li><p>At the command prompt, type the following command and specify the value of the <strong>properties_file</strong> argument.</p><pre>reqifimport properties_file=&lt;path to properties file&gt;</pre><p>Where <strong>properties_file</strong> is the path to the properties file to be used.<br /><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);line-height: 1.42857;">Press <strong>Enter</strong>.</span></span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);line-height: 1.42857;">Now after opening the project you have imported the ReqIF file into, the imported data is already present in the Containment tree of the project.<br /></span></span></span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737939 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03240: (2026x) Improving performance

- page_id: `272737939`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737939/2026x+Improving+performance
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:24.763+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427324068

1427324070

1427324069

INLINE

When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Memory allocation' space=''] (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Memory monitor' space=''] to track memory usage.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Active validation period' space=''] and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.
- Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project partitioning' space=''].
- Use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Memory monitor' space=''] to free unused memory.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Background Task Manager' space=''] that run too long.
- Set the limit of open diagrams in the Environment Options dialog ( Options > Environment > General > General > Diagram Tabs Limit ).

1427324067

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Getting started' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing and Selecting Perspective' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Configuration files' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Disabling inbound network activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a61e022-7559-4d3f-b56c-3dee6baa15de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324068</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="163095b8-9d1f-4648-bcc3-550ea595c28e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324070</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d690d15-ffd8-4de9-b158-88970fb6091a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea99695-1d3c-4cd0-93b9-438f4b087bb8"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When working with very large models or a number of diagrams at a time, the modeling tool performance may decrease. To increase efficiency, we suggest the following solutions:</p><ul><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Memory allocation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase memory allocation value]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (java heap size). If you want to make sure you have a sufficient amount of memory allocated to your modeling tool, use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[memory monitor]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to track memory usage.</p></li><li><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Active validation period" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Increase an active validation period]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and/or do not keep unused diagrams open.</p></li><li><p>Split a project into several separate project files, and load only the files that you work with. This may help only if your project contains several parts with minimal dependencies between them. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project partitioning" /></ac:link>.</p></li><li><p>Use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Memory monitor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Garbage Collector]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to free unused memory.</p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Background Task Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cancel the background tasks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that run too long.</li><li>Set the limit of open diagrams in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog (<strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagram Tabs Limit</strong>). </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bee057bf-13ac-4c87-a9c6-d0ef312493f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427324067</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Getting started" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing environment options" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Customizing and Selecting Perspective" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Configuration files" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Disabling inbound network activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Component with the stereotype XSDinclude. This component must be added into an xsd file component.

- annotation maps to the UML Component documentation
- schemaLocation maps to the UML Component name.

#### PANEL: include XML representation summary

include XML representation summary

```text
<include
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
schemaLocation = anyURI
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</include>
```

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### include UML model example.

#### PANEL: include XML code sample

include XML code sample

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com">	<xs:include schemaLocation = "http://nomagic.com/schema.xsd" /></xs:schema>
```

## PAGE 03242: (2026x) Including external images

- page_id: `272735758`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735758/2026x+Including+external+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:48.878+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Helper tools > (2026x) Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050285233

2050285245

2050285231

**On this page**

43

2050285234

You can include images from an external source in a report. The supported image types are *BMP*, *JPG*, *WBMP*, *GIF*, and *PNG*.

###### **$image.include(location)**

Include an external image from a local file or *URL* in a report.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | location | java.lang.String | An image’s location. The location format can be either a URL or a local file. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image included in a report |

For example:

```text

```

The supported image types are <em>BMP</em>, <em>JPG</em>, <em>WBMP</em>, <em>GIF</em>, and <em>PNG</em>.</p><h4><strong>$image.include(location)</strong></h4><p>Include an external image from a local file or <em>URL</em> in a report.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>location</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An image’s location. <br />The location format <br />can be either a URL <br />or a local file.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image included <br />in a report</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd86fd2e-acc9-4364-82e6-34794a984140"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$image.include("C:\Users\username\images\logo.gif")
$image.include("http://www.magicdraw.com/images/product_boxes/MD.gif")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738465 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03243: (2026x) Inclusive Gateway

- page_id: `272738465`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738465/2026x+Inclusive+Gateway
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:20.015+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x) Gateways
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485738387

1485738387

1485738387

**Description**

A diverging Inclusive Gateway (Inclusive Decision) is used to create not only alternative but also parallel paths within a process flow. Unlike an Exclusive Gateway, it evaluates all condition expressions. The true evaluation of one condition expression does not exclude the evaluation of the other condition expressions. All of the sequence flows with true evaluation will be traversed by a token.

Since each path is considered to be independent, all combinations of the paths may be taken, from zero to all. However, it should be designed in such a way that at least one path is taken.

A converging Inclusive Gateway is used to merge a combination of alternative and parallel paths. A control flow token arriving at an Inclusive Gateway may be synchronized with some other tokens that arrive later at this Gateway.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diverging Inclusive Gateway

1485738386

**Related elements**

- Gateways
- Sequence Flow

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using a Sequence Flow

Unlike an Exclusive Gateway, it evaluates all condition expressions. The true evaluation of one condition expression does not exclude the evaluation of the other condition expressions. All of the sequence flows with true evaluation will be traversed by a token.</p><p>Since each path is considered to be independent, all combinations of the paths may be taken, from zero to all. However, it should be designed in such a way that at least one path is taken.</p><p>A converging Inclusive Gateway is used to merge a combination of alternative and parallel paths. A control flow token arriving at an Inclusive Gateway may be synchronized with some other tokens that arrive later at this Gateway.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gateway_inclusive.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inclusive Gateway" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Diverging_inclusive_gateway.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inclusive Gateway" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Diverging Inclusive Gateway</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca9df4a0-8117-467a-9230-51a9a9d2126f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485738386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Gateways">Gateways</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Sequence+Flow">Sequence Flow</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Creating+and+Using+a+Sequence+Flow">Creating and Using a Sequence Flow</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735898 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03244: (2026x) Incompatible properties and related elements

- page_id: `272735898`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735898/2026x+Incompatible+properties+and+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:23.359+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Refactoring > (2026x) Converting Elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the conversion, a converted element owns the same compatible specification properties and is connected with the same related elements as the original element was connected. The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.

Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.

For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.

To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.

##### Changes after conversion

###### Converting a property to a port

If the property visibility is *private*, *protected,* or *package* , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to *public*.

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The incompatible properties of the original element are lost, that is, are deleted from the project.</p><p>Incompatible means that the converted element can NOT own particular properties or can NOT be associated with particular related elements of the original element.</p><p>For example, let’s convert the component to package. See an example in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)" ac:title="Example of conversion when original and converted elements has incompatible property (Port)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="converting_elements_incompatible_elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Incompatible properties and related elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>As you see, the component has the assigned port. According to UML guidelines, a package cannot own ports. So, in this case, ports are incompatible with the packages. After the conversion, the package, that is, a converted element, has no assigned port. The port is deleted from the project.</p><p>To prevent from losing model data, you always get a message with a warning that incompatible properties will be lost after the element conversion.</p><h3>Changes after conversion</h3><h4>Converting a property to a port</h4><p>If the property visibility is <em>private</em>, <em>protected,</em> or <em>package</em> , then after the conversion visibility of the created port will be set to <em>public</em>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67b51616-1257-45d8-b179-df6555185bfc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2fc359cb-dee4-4291-9163-fcd677e10dd5" /> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731844 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03245: (2026x) Indexing

- page_id: `272731844`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731844/2026x+Indexing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:44.104+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Searching
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model elements representation text indexing allows users to find elements in the model more quickly. It starts automatically when a Teamwork Cloud project is opened for the first time; you can track the progress in the status bar (see the image below). The index is automatically updated when the model changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The indexing progress shown in the status bar.

Please note that indexing may take some time depending on the project size. The more elements the project contains, the longer it takes to index the model elements representation text.

If representation text building is in progress, searches performed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting elements' space=''] dialog may be slower. In this case, a warning appears next to the search progress bar (see an image below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If a search does not return expected results, we recommend that you rebuild the model elements representation text index (see below).

The**Rebuild Text Index** action is available in the Expert mode only.

instructionsTo rebuild the text index

- In the main menu , click Tools > Rebuild Text Index . [ATTACHMENT filename='rebuild_text_index_dialog.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In this case, a warning appears next to the search progress bar (see an image below).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="slower_search_indexing.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Indexing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>If a search does not return expected results, we recommend that you rebuild the model elements representation text index (see <ac:link ac:anchor="instructions" /> below). </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59322706-47e1-4ac6-96fe-675b548ba543"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="99e54bea-6193-477a-83c0-588d6cb31e0a">The </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="99e54bea-6193-477a-83c0-588d6cb31e0a">Rebuild Text</ac:inline-comment-marker> Index</strong> action is available in the Expert mode only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cfe44b4-56c1-477a-918c-b182e36c7c1c"><ac:parameter ac:name="">instructions</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To rebuild the text index</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a74fc97e-c7c5-470a-b2c2-54b78e2722cd">menu</ac:inline-comment-marker>, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Rebuild Text Index</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rebuild_text_index_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Indexing" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p>
