<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732618 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03246: (2026x) Indexing of unloaded projects

- page_id: `272732618`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732618/2026x+Indexing+of+unloaded+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:56.434+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Project partitioning > (2026x) Decomposing model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Proxy

628699399

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Proxy

628699401

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Proxy

628699400

If you have a large project and, due to performance issues, some used projects are not loaded, you may use indexing to work with the project smoothly.Indexing can be considered as an intermediate form of work, falling between working with a fully loaded used project and working with a not-loaded used project.

#### INFO: Proxy

Proxy

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']true

When a used project is unloaded/not loaded in the project, only proxies of necessary elements are shown in place of the used project. Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources. If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.

A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:

A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.

To specify elements for indexing

1. Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.
2. From the Options menu, select Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
3. Select the Indexing property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_options_indexing.png']
4. Select the **Common Indexing** option. This enables indexing of the used project and determines what information will be indexed.
5. When common indexing is chosen, classifiers and their inheritance relationships will be indexed. If you want more elements to be indexed, select the **Custom Indexing** option and fine-tune which element types (properties, methods, etc) should be indexed. The more elements you select, the more elements will be accessible in the project using them as proxies. However, your gains in performance from the used project unloading will also diminish. Thus, a balance is needed when customizing the index. It is usually better to use the common indexing variant. Click **OK**.
6. In the project using the indexed project, go to the options of the used indexed project (from the main menu, select **Options** > **Project Usages**) and select the **Use Index** check box.

628699398

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']

Using indexing allows you to show more proxy elements from the unloaded project. These proxies can be used as normal model elements in the project using them without loading the used project. Keeping the used project not loaded saves a considerable amount of computer resources.  If you ever need full information from that used project, you can load it at any time.</p><p>A typical case where this functionality is useful is outlined below:</p><p>A large software library is used in a project. Only certain classes are important for the main project: some library classes are set as types of properties in the model classes, some model classes inherit from the library classes, etc. In this case, the sub-elements of the library classes (their properties and methods) are not important, and we can index classes in the library and keep it not loaded in the main project. Indexing will still show all the classes in the main project from the library.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify elements for <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="108124cd-4ada-4792-92e5-e3120682817a">indexing</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="77374505-fd83-4671-b796-37de755e139c">Open the used project (that is, not loaded) separately from the using project.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>Indexing</strong> property group.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_options_indexing.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Indexing of unloaded projects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Select the <strong>Common Indexing</strong> option. 1487609551

1487609552

1487609552

**Description**

An Influencing Organization is an organization that is external to the enterprise modeled in a given enterprise BMM, and that influences that enterprise.

An Influencing Organization is the source of Influencer. The Influencer may have multiple sources, or none.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487609550

- Influence Concepts Relationship

An Information System is a type of hardware or a software, e. g., international sales system, ticket reservation system, etc. Information system can be used by Process or Process Activity.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729792 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03251: (2026x) Inheritance tab

- page_id: `272729792`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729792/2026x+Inheritance+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:35:44.172+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425598369

1425598371

1425598370

INLINE

The Inheritance tab represents classifiers (e.g., packages, data types, and stereotypes) hierarchy within your project. Inheritance according to the UML Specification is shown using the generalization relationship.

If a model element has no generalization relationship, it will not be represented in the Inheritance tab.

To open a new Inheritance tree

1. Open the Containment , Structure , or Inheritance tab.
2. In the open tab toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='open_new_tab_button.png'] and then select Open in New Inheritance Tree .

You can change some Inheritance tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

The Inheritance tab consists of the following two components:

- Inheritance tab toolbar
- Inheritance tree

##### Inheritance tab toolbar

The Inheritance tab toolbar contains the following buttons

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all currently expanded elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Open in New Tree | Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.You can choose to open elements in:New Containment TreeNew Structure TreeNew Inheritance Tree |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favorites Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Invert Tree - inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the childelement at the top of the hierarchy.Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree. |

##### Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree

You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Structure Tree | Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML v1). |

1425598368

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space='']

/></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Button</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Tooltip text</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Collapse All</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">C</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">ollapses all currently expanded elements in the Containment tree.</span></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Collapse_selected_recursively.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Containment tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Collapse Selected Recursively</td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="18" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_new_tab_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><p>Open in New Tree</p></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.</p><p>You can choose to open elements in:</p><ul><li>New Containment Tree</li><li>New Structure Tree</li><li>New Inheritance Tree</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_tab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="17" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorite_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Favorites</p></td><td><p>Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Add a selected element to favorites</li><li>Open the dialog for managing favorites<span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span></li><li>Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="19" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Quick Find</p></td><td><p>Opens the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inheritance tab" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Options</td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to open the Options menu and then select:</p><ul><li><strong><strong>Invert Tree</strong> </strong>- inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the child<br />element at the top of the hierarchy.<strong><br /></strong></li><li><strong>Show </strong><strong>Applied Stereotypes</strong> - shows or hides the applied <br />stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree</h3><p><br /></p><p>You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 39.6427%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 28.0677%;" /><col style="width: 71.9323%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Select in Containment</strong> <strong>Tree</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Containment tab and selects the <br />current element in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Select in Structure Tree</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af49f33a-5b79-474a-834d-d8ee5e6a182b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML v1).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b778001b-c783-474f-92d7-aaa6e02f945c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425598368</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Model Browser" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738758 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03252: (2026x) Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges

- page_id: `272738758`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738758/2026x+Inheritance+Aggregation+Composition+and+Redefinition+of+Exchanges
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:32.921+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures > (2026x) Working with exchanges
- labels: ['inheritance-of-exchanges', 'aggregation-of-exchanges', 'composition-of-exchanges', 'redefinition-of-exchanges', 'aggregated-information', 'display-all-operational-exchanges', 'hide-inherited-exchanges', 'see-inherited-exchanges', 'redefine-operational-exchange', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational/Resource/Service Exchanges can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.

You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Service Exchange Manager dialog' space=''] dialog
- Operational/Resources/Services/Personnel/Security (Operational and Resource) Role-based Connectivity tables.

The simplified scenarios illustrating the Aggregation and Inherited Exchanges are described in the following cases:

###### Case 1. Aggregated Exchanges

Let's say, we have Operational Performers *Helicopter*and *Signal Receiver* related to the Operational Exchange *Reported Condition*. Then we draw an association between *Medical Assistance* and *Signal Receiver*. In this case, the drawn association can realize the *Reported Condition* exchange, since the Operational Performer *Medical Assistance* aggregates the Operational Performer *Helicopter*.

If Composition is used instead of Aggregation to connect Medical Assistance and Helicopter, the Exchanges will be collected the same way as in the Aggregation case.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide aggregated exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Aggregation property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Aggregation or Exchange is removed.

All the aggregated exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The aggregated exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the aggregated exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows.
2. In the open menu, click the Aggregated Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Case 2. Inherited Exchanges

Let's say, we have an Operational Performers *Search**Node* and *Place Of Safety* related to the Operational Exchange *Warning Order*. The Operational Performer *Aircraft* specifies the Operational Performer *Search**Node*. Then we draw an association between *Aircraft* and *Place O**f**Safety*. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized and redefined.

Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:

- By clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='display_all_operational_exchanges.png'] in the association smart manipulator toolbar.
- By selecting the check box in the Realize column of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.

All the inherited exchanges can also be seen in the role-based connectivity tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the inherited exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows.
2. In the open menu, click the Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To redefine the Operational Exchange

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog , select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the Redefine button. The new Operational Exchange is created.

All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731244 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03253: (2026x) Initial Values

- page_id: `272731244`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731244/2026x+Initial+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:38.929+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['initial-values', 'context-specific-values', 'default-values', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1345490194

1345490196

1345490195

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Compartments' space=''] with a label of *initial values* is used to show values of properties belonging to a containing block. These values override any default values that may have been previously specified on these properties on their originally defining block. Initial value compartments may be specified within nested properties, which then apply only in the particular usage context defined by the outermost containing block.

The purpose of initial value compartments is to show various values as a result of a reconfigured selected context. Initial value compartments allows to:

- Create different configurations for the same structure and display them directly in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] .
- Have different values for the same part in different contexts.
- Assign a different initial value to an inherited property.

The *initial value*s compartment can be shown on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] shape compartment and display the values of the properties reconfigured in a selected context.

The procedures below describes how to work with initial values and its compartments:

43

##### Defining initial values

You can define context-specific initial values by overriding any default values that was previously specified on Value Properties on their originally defining Block. Before defining a context-specific initial value, the followingprerequisites must be met:

- The Value Property must be created for a Block which is a type of Part Property.
- The Type and Default Value are defined for the Value Property in its Specification window.

To defineinitial value

1. Right-click the Part Property shape with default value defined.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > **Define Context-Specific Initial Value**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. From the **Select Property** dialog, select Value Property which you want to redefine. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] After selecting the Value Property, the structure of instances is created automatically in the Model Browser.
4. Type the value directly on the Part Property shape. If you can not specify the new value, check if the context of that Part Property is correct. ]]>****
5. Press Enter. The slot with newly defined value is created for the Instance Specification. The default value is not changed. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying/hiding *initial values* compartments

You can display/hide initial values on the Part Property Compartments. Read more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML v1 specific compartments' space=''].

To show/hide Initial Values on Part Property compartment, do one of the following

- Select the Part Property shape, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Smart manipulators' space=''] , and select Initial Values. [ATTACHMENT filename='initial_value_compartments.png']
- Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the Expert or All mode, and set the Suppress Context Specific Values property value to true to show initial values, or false - to hide them.

##### contextSelecting the context of initial values

Initial values which are shown in the Part Property compartments depends from the selected context. For example, if the selected context is A then the compartment show values of A.

To select a context of initial values

1. Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the All mode.
3. Select the Context of Context-Specific Values property value box, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_value_button_in_specification_window.png'] .
4. From the Select Element dialog, choose the element which will be the context.
5. Click OK .
6. Click Close . The context for initial values is specified.

##### Displaying/hiding element type in the *initial values* compartments

You can display or hide the elements types in the *initial values* compartments of a part using either the Symbol(s) Properties dialog or the part shortcut menu.

To display/hide element type in the *initial values* compartments

1. Right-click the Part Property shape and select the Symbol Properties command.
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, select the Expert or All mode.
3. Select the Show Slot Type property value from drop-down list:

- 
  - None - to hide types.
  - Name - to display names of the element types.
  - Qualified Name - to display qualified names of the element types.

1345490192

**Related pages**

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Phone Camera** sample model. To open this sample download [ATTACHMENT filename='PhoneCamerasInitialValues.mdzip'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47d19cb7-47f1-4b00-93b4-d9b22d2a457b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1345490194</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1eb25bb-910e-4f04-b6dd-063e66529a5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1345490196</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d9fd3ad-d5f3-47ca-87fd-c98ba8dbf1a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1345490195</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[compartment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with a label of <em>initial values</em> is used to show values of properties belonging to a containing block. These values override any default values that may have been previously specified on these properties on their originally defining block. Initial value compartments may be specified within nested properties, which then apply only in the particular usage context defined by the outermost containing block.</p><p>The purpose of initial value compartments is to show various values as a result of a reconfigured selected context. Initial value compartments allows to:</p><ul><li>Create different configurations for the same structure and display them directly in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block Definition diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Have different values for the same part in different contexts.</li><li>Assign a different initial value to an inherited property.</li></ul><p>The <em>initial value</em>s compartment can be shown on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> shape compartment and display the values of the properties reconfigured in a selected context<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.</span><br /></span></p><p>The procedures below describes how to work with initial values and its compartments:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1390d4bb-dd62-455b-abe4-5f48a6963948"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Defining initial values</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can define <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">context-specific</span></span> initial values by overriding any default values that was previously specified on Value Properties on their originally defining Block. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Before d<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">efining a context-specific initial value</span></span>, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the following </span></span></span>prerequisites must be met:</span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Value Property must be created for a Block which is a type of Part Property.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Type and Default Value are defined for the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Value Property</span> in its Specification window.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br style="color: rgb(51,51,51);" /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To define<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> initial value</span></span></span></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Part Property shape with default value defined.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select<strong> Tools</strong> &gt; <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Define Context-Specific Initial Value</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="define_context_specific_initial_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Initial Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the <strong>Select Property</strong> dialog, select Value Property which you want to redefine.</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_value_property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Initial Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="859840e1-2833-42ff-9ee9-5ad663573c04"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the Value Property, the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">structure of instances is created automatically in the Model Browser</span>.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Type the value directly on the Part Property shape.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b4c080f-31ca-4155-96c2-47d992684b8a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you can not specify the new value<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, check if the context of that Part Property is correct. <ac:link ac:anchor="context"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to select the context of initial value >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><strong> </strong></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Press Enter.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span>The slot with newly defined value is created for the Instance Specification. The default value is not changed.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="defined_initial_value.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Initial Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></li></ol><h3>Displaying/hiding <em>initial values</em> compartments</h3><p>You can display/hide initial values on the Part Property Compartments. Read more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML v1 specific compartments" /></ac:link>.</p><p>To show/hide Initial Values on Part Property compartment, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the Part Property shape, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and select Initial Values.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="initial_value_compartments.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Initial Values" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties. In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Expert</strong> or <strong>All</strong> mode, and set the <strong>Suppress Context Specific Values</strong> property value to true to show initial values, or false - to hide them.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e05be940-64f5-4589-a6ba-a9ee129722b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="">context</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Selecting the context of initial values</h3><p>Initial values which are shown in the Part Property compartments depends from the selected context. For example, if the selected context is A then the compartment show values of A.</p><p>To select a context of initial values</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Part Property shape and from the shortcut menu select Symbol Properties.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>All</strong> mode.</li><li>Select the <strong>Context of Context-Specific Values</strong> property value box, and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_value_button_in_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>From the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, choose the element which will be the context.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The context for initial values is specified.</li></ol><h3>Displaying/hiding element type in the <em>initial values</em> compartments</h3><p>You can display or hide the elements types in the <em>initial values</em> compartments of a part using either the Symbol(s) Properties dialog or the part shortcut menu.</p><p>To display/hide element type in the <em>initial values</em> compartments</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Part Property shape and select the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> command.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Expert</strong> or <strong>All</strong> mode.</li><li>Select the <strong>Show Slot Type</strong> property value from drop-down list:</li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>None</strong> - to hide types.</li><li><strong>Name</strong> - to display names of the element types.</li><li><strong>Qualified Name</strong> - to display qualified names of the element types.</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70fa2bb3-0cc2-4788-8fe3-6c09aa836f15"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1345490192</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="08d7c3dc-6584-461a-8cf3-c0be512dea3e" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Phone Camera</strong> sample model. To open this sample download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="PhoneCamerasInitialValues.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734063 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03254: (2026x) Inserting a shape on the path

- page_id: `272734063`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734063/2026x+Inserting+a+shape+on+the+path
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:17.020+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['split-transition', 'insert-shape-on-path', 'split-path', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) State Machine diagram' space='']StateMachine and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity diagram' space=''] diagrams.

In the State and Activity diagrams, you can split a path into two paths by dropping a shape on it. This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. 
 
To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane

1. Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.
2. Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.
3. In the **Split Transition** dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:

- **Before Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn. For example, *Password read* transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to*Verification* State. If you want to insert the*Read password* State before the *Password read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **Before Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **After Transition**button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn. For example, *Name**read* Transition is drawn from *Read Name* State to *Verification* State. If you want to insert the *Read**password* State after the *Name read* Transition, drop the *Read password* State on the Transition, and in the **Split Transition** dialog, choose the **After Transition** button.[IMAGE alt='' src=''][IMAGE alt='' src='']
- **Do Not Insert**button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.

In the **Split Transition** dialog, select the **Remember my choice** check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

This is valid for Transition/ Control Flow/ Object Flow relationships and allows you to connect the element shape with the elements connected via the path. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To insert a new shape by splitting the path on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select the shape you want to insert or create a new one.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Drag the shape on the path (the path is highlighted in blue) and drop it.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> In the <strong>Split Transition</strong> dialog, click the appropriate button according to where you want to insert the shape on the path:</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li></ol><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Before Transition </strong>button. The shape is inserted before the path. It means that a new path is created, then the dropped shape is drawn and then the existing path is drawn.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b22b0b0c-fd35-4399-84a2-38e5a213de0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Password read</em> transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to<em> Verification</em> State. If you want to insert the<em> Read password</em> State before the <em>Password read</em> Transition, drop the <em>Read password</em> State on the Transition, and in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog, choose the <strong>Before Transition</strong> button.</span></span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="200" ac:width="160"><ri:attachment ri:filename="state_machine_diagram_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="242" ac:width="163"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Insert_symbol_before_path_with_state.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>After <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Transition </span></strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">button</span>. The shape is inserted before the path. It means the existing path is created, the dropped element symbol is drawn, and then a new path is drawn.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="125c74a4-a702-4fa7-af1e-ad6533f7bf0f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">For example, <em>Name </em><em>read</em> Transition is drawn from <em>Read Name</em> State to <em>Verification</em> State. If you want to insert the <em>Read</em><em> password</em> State after the <em>Name read</em> Transition, drop the <em>Read password</em> State on the Transition, and in the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog, choose the <strong>After Transition</strong> button. </span></p><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="222" ac:width="159"><ri:attachment ri:filename="state_machine_diagram_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><ac:image ac:title="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:alt="Inserting a shape on the path" ac:height="252" ac:width="154"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Insert_symbol_after_path_with_state.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Do Not Insert </strong>button. Action is canceled and the dialog is closed.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69da0fcd-52d9-42db-9ab9-6f589edb6390"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Split Transition</strong></span> dialog</span>, select the <strong>Remember my choice</strong> check box and the next time the shape will be inserted before or after the path, depending on your selection.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Split Transition dialog" ac:alt="Split Transition dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="split_transition_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inserting a shape on the path" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong><br /></strong></span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736741 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03255: (2026x) Inserting images into HTML text

- page_id: `272736741`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736741/2026x+Inserting+images+into+HTML+text
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:38.197+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) HTML editor
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### EXCERPT: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

INLINE

To insert an image into HTML text

#### EXPAND: From the Containment tree

From the Containment tree

- When the image is already an AttachedFile, please refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with attached files' space=''] .

#### EXPAND: By using the Advanced HTML Editor

By using the Advanced HTML Editor

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Advanced HTML Editor dialog' space=''] .
2. On the toolbar, click Insert Image button [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] , then click one of the following:
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_image_button.png'] From File – to browse the image in your file system or paste the attached image file URL into the Picture source field.
  - [ATTACHMENT filename='insert_attached_image.png'] From Attached File – to open the Select Elements dialog and select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached.
  - [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **From Image Library** – to use images from a bundled image collection or your own added sets of images.
3. Click OK twice to close both dialogs.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

By pasting or dragging the image file from file system

1. Open the image file location, select the file(s) you want to insert, then do one of the following:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the **Paste Special**menu, select **Image**to paste image and attach it to the project or **Image (without attaching)** to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the **Select Image Owner**, select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click **Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click**OK**to close the dialogs. If you chose to not attach the image, it is displayed in the HTML Editor, but when you delete the file from your file system, it is no longer displayed. That also means other users you want to share the project with won't be able to see the image if they open the project from another device.This option is very useful when the file you want to insert is large and you don't want to increase the project size. This feature works on both the HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste the image to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste image and attach it to the project or HTML Text to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on the HTML Editor. This feature works only with Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox.

#### EXPAND: By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

By pasting or dragging rich text with an image

1. Do either:
  - copy/paste content to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select HTML Text to choose whether paste it only for referencing in the diagram or attach it to the project, or select Image to paste and attach it to the project, or select Plain Text to paste only text without an image.
  - drag the image to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click**Cancel**, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.
2. Click **OK** to close the dialogs. This feature works only on HTML Editor. Only rich text from MS Office files is supported. If the image from the rich text document is not stored in your file system, you are not asked to choose whether to attach the file or not.

To insert multiple image files

1. Open the image files location, select the files you want to insert, then do either:
  - copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the Paste Special menu, select Image to paste images and attach them to the project or Image (without attaching) to paste them only for referencing in the diagram.
  - drag them to the HTML Editor. In the Select Image Owner , select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile images should be attached.
2. Click OK to close the dialogs.

This feature works on both HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

the image file from file system</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Open the image file location, select the file(s) you want to insert, then do one of the following:<br /><br /><ul><li>copy/paste <span>them to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Paste Special </strong>menu, select <strong>Image </strong>to paste image and attach it to the project or <strong>Image (without attaching)</strong> to paste it <span>only for referencing in the diagram.</span></span></li><li><span><span>drag them to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Select Image Owner</strong>, select <span>the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. If you click <strong>Cancel</strong>, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.<br /></span></span></span><span> </span></li></ul></li><li><p><span>Click </span><strong>OK</strong><span> to close the dialogs.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b646714-975a-4f24-a3d6-4e0cf5e55428"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you chose to not attach the image</span><span>, it is displayed in the HTML Editor, but when you delete the file from your file system, it is no longer displayed. That also means other users you want to share the project with won't be able to see the image if they open the project from another device. </span>This option is very useful when the file you want to insert is large and you don't want to increase the project size.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47f1a584-94e6-4a50-a47c-7934abbd0568"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works on both the HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="145a8115-0b56-4910-b30f-a0c0c178fb48"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">By pasting or dragging an image from a web browser</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Do either:<br /><br /><ul><li>copy/paste the image to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Paste Special </strong>menu, select <strong>Image </strong>to paste image and attach it to the project or <strong>HTML Text </strong>to paste it only for referencing in the diagram.</li><li>drag the image to the HTML Editor. <br /> </li></ul></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialogs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fecd06b5-48f2-45a1-8f1a-c89fe4fd751f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works only on the HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7c7466d-6f09-4cfa-a546-ea83bdc9c375"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works only with Google Chrome and Mozilla Firefox.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a98311b7-6cb5-4786-8159-e7ee54a5b5c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">By pasting or dragging rich text with an image</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Do either:<br /><br /><ul><li>copy/paste content to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Paste Special </strong>menu, select <strong>HTML Text </strong><span>to choose whether paste it only for referencing in the diagram or attach it to the project, or select </span><strong>Image </strong>to paste and attach it to the project, or select <strong>Plain Text </strong>to paste only text without an image.</li><li>drag the image to the HTML Editor. <span>In the </span><strong>Select Image Owner</strong><span>, select </span><span>the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile image should be attached. <span>If you click </span><strong>Cancel</strong><span>, the image will be displayed in the diagram, but not attached to the project.</span></span><br /> </li></ul></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialogs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b066056-1c53-491b-a8aa-79fee0ae44c3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works only on HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="129a3559-d8b8-4743-8106-3d12af578491"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only rich text from MS Office files is supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cc5590d-0202-4fb4-bfa2-630e7250d04d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the image from the rich text document is not stored in your file system, you are not asked to choose whether to attach the file or not.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To insert multiple image files</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the image files location, select the files you want to insert, <span>then do either:<br /> <br /></span><ul><li>copy/paste them to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Paste Special </strong>menu, select <strong>Image </strong>to paste images and attach them to the project or <strong>Image (without attaching)</strong> to paste them only for referencing in the diagram.</li><li>drag them to the HTML Editor. In the <strong>Select Image Owner</strong>, select the appropriate parent element to which the AttachedFile images should be attached.<br /> </li></ul></li><li><span>Click </span><strong>OK</strong><span> to close the dialogs.</span><br /><span><span> </span></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6bc8859-213d-46a9-a4ce-ecb246d51a95"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature works on both HTML Editor and Advanced HTML Editor.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736035 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03256: (2026x) Inspecting changes in the Change details panel

- page_id: `272736035`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736035/2026x+Inspecting+changes+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:32.958+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Model Merge > (2026x) Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['change-details', 'merge-result', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1991256206

INLINE

1991256208

INLINE

1991256207

INLINE

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the changes that occurred either on the element selected in the **Merged** **Result** tree/list or on the property selected in the **Specification** panel. The panel is located under the **Specification** panel on the **Merge** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has two top-level branches – Source changes and Target changes – in 3-way merge, and 1 top-level branch – Source changes – in 2-way merge. In this panel, you can accept or reject changes using the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding change concept' space=''].

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed to numerical/text types accordingly.

1991256204

INLINE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739569 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03257: (2026x) Inspecting differences in the Change details panel

- page_id: `272739569`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739569/2026x+Inspecting+differences+in+the+Change+details+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:32.424+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Comparing projects > (2026x) Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['change-details', 'differences', 'difference-viewer', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Change details** panel displays a tree structure reflecting the differences between the two versions of either the element selected in the **Differences** tree or the property selected in the **Specification** panel. If several elements or properties are selected, then all changes that have been detected in these elements or properties accordingly will be displayed in the **Change** **details** panel. The panel is located above the **Specification** panel on the **Difference Viewer** window.

The panel contains a tree structure that has 1 top-level branch – Changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the **Dependent changes** branch under an element with dependent changes.

For the dependent changes description, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding change types' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Change details</strong> panel displays a tree structure reflecting the differences between the two versions of either the element selected in the <strong>Differences</strong> tree or the property selected in the <strong>Specification</strong> panel. If several elements or properties are selected, then all changes that have been detected in these elements or properties accordingly will be displayed in the <strong>Change</strong> <strong>details</strong> panel. The panel is located above the <strong>Specification</strong> panel on the <strong>Difference Viewer</strong> window.</p><p>The panel contains a tree structure that has 1 top-level branch <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="edfa489e-d703-4e81-84d4-4e561ff6619a">– Changes.</ac:inline-comment-marker>   <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> <br /> </span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Change details dialog" ac:alt="Change details dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="change_details_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inspecting differences in the Change details panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>In this panel, you can see dependent changes. In order to see them, expand the <strong>Dependent changes</strong> branch under an element with dependent changes.</p><p>For the dependent changes description, r<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="319a5311-84d4-4531-8f99-20cc6ed2e97e">efer t</ac:inline-comment-marker>o <ac:link ac:anchor="dependentchange"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding change types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dependent changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739572 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03258: (2026x) Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel

- page_id: `272739572`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739572/2026x+Inspecting+element+property+changes+in+the+differences+Specification+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:32.795+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Model analysis > (2026x) Comparing projects > (2026x) Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['property-changes', 'compare-specification-panel', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can see all the details of the element property changes in the **Specification** panel that is located at the bottom of the **Difference Viewer** window. All you need to do is selecting the appropriate element in the **Differences** tree.

As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both base and compared projects.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To navigate from the **Specification** panel to the **Differences** tree, do either

- In the Properties column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to Select in Differences tree and choose an element to which you want to navigate.
- I n the Base or Compared column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 **Inspecting differences between textual values**

You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post- conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are appropriately highlighted. As of version 18.3, the differences between compared text written in HTML are now represented as rich text, enabling you to see all changes in formatting as well.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
 To see the differences of compared texts, do either

- Select a property with a modified text on the Specification panel and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_property_values_button.png'] on the toolbar.
- Double-click the property on the Specification panel. The Compare property dialog opens.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

All you need to do is selecting the appropriate element in <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a2033ad1-57fd-4c86-95d8-852c0e08f869">the </ac:inline-comment-marker> <strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a2033ad1-57fd-4c86-95d8-852c0e08f869">Differences</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a2033ad1-57fd-4c86-95d8-852c0e08f869"> tree.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes t<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="027b7e7d-5dca-4150-815d-e43fed998a3a">hat have been d</ac:inline-comment-marker>etected in both base and compared projects.</p><p><ac:image ac:alt="Displaying element property changes in Specification panel" ac:title="Displaying element property changes in Specification panel"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_specification_panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><p>To navigate from the <strong>Specification</strong> panel to the <strong>Differences</strong> tree, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Properties</strong> column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. The <strong>Compare p<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d112cebf-7f31-45f8-a49d-efa7ed5fd504">roperty</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d112cebf-7f31-45f8-a49d-efa7ed5fd504"> dialog opens.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735996 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03259: (2026x) Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel

- page_id: `272735996`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735996/2026x+Inspecting+element+property+changes+in+the+Specification+panel
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:30.503+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Tools > (2026x) Model Merge > (2026x) Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['merge-specification-panel', 'property-changes', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1086956613

1086956615

1086956614

You can see all the details of the element property changes in the **Specification** panel that is located at the top right of the **Merge** window. All you need to do is selecting the element in the **Merged Result** tree or list.

As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both source and the and target. Third column Merge Results Preview shows the final merge result. Properties changes can be accepted or rejected in this panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To navigate from the **Specification** panel to the **Merged Result** tree, do either

- In the Properties column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to Select in Merged Result Tree and choose an element to which you want to navigate.
- In the Source , Ancestor , Target or Merge Result Preview column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Ancestor** column by default is hidden. To show it in the **Specification**panel, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the **Merge**toolbar and select **Show Ancestor**.

The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed into numerical/text types accordingly.

**Inspecting differences between textual values**

You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post-conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are highlighted.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
To see the differences of compared texts, do either

- Select a property with a modified text on the Specification panel and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_property_values_button.png'] on the toolbar.
- Double-click the property on the Specification panel. The Compare property dialog opens.

1086956612

**Related pages**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Merge' space='']**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42c73278-e0ac-4af9-baf4-f60c5d8d41a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1086956613</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53b90e4c-5e54-41f0-bfc8-eb338435272c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1086956615</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b7acdc9f-ea28-4f36-b426-b6e0b0d3ef53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1086956614</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="527afc11-f044-4d81-8354-777b9b3658dc">You</ac:inline-comment-marker> can see all the details of the element property changes in the <strong>Specification</strong> panel that is located at the top right of the <strong>Merge</strong> window. All you need to do is selecting the element in the <strong>Merged Result</strong> tree or list. </p><p>As you can see in the following figure, the first column of the panel contains a list of element property names, and other columns display their value changes that have been detected in both source and the and target. Third column Merge Results Preview shows the final merge result. Properties changes can be accepted or rejected in this panel. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Speification_pnel.PNG"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To navigate from the <strong>Specification</strong> panel to the <strong>Merged Result</strong> tree, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Properties</strong> column, right-click the name of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu, point to <strong>Select in Merged Result Tree</strong> and choose an element to which you want to navigate.</li><li>In the <strong>Source</strong>, <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8003c2bf-ac4a-4600-9ea2-8cf0c433339a">Ancestor</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong>, <strong>Target</strong>  or <strong>Merge Result Preview </strong>column, right-click the value of a property, which references other elements. Then on its shortcut menu choose an element to which you want to navigate. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Using property name shortcut menu to navigate from Specification panel to Merged Result tree" ac:alt="Using property name shortcut menu to navigate from Specification panel to Merged Result tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="merge_specification_panel_shortcut.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d0e411c-a637-4371-bad1-75f3d1034917"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Ancestor</strong> column by default is hidden. To show it in the <strong>Specification </strong>panel, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="merge_options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the <strong>Merge </strong>toolbar and select <strong>Show Ancestor</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf7040c7-d539-4d2e-9200-a5fe8257dafa"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Multiplicity attribute is merged as a string, meaning that Lower Value and Upper Value are merged and transformed into numerical/text types accordingly.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Inspecting differences between textual values</strong></span></p><p>You can see exact changes between textual values of element properties, such as element documentation, comments, pre- and post-conditions of a use case. Differences of compared texts can be displayed in a single dialog, where inserted and deleted parts of the text are highlighted.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Displaying differences between long textual property values" ac:alt="Displaying differences between long textual property values"><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_property_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Inspecting element property changes in the differences Specification panel" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" />To see the differences of compared texts, do either</p><hr /><ul><li>Select a property with a modified text on the <strong>Specification</strong> panel and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compare_property_values_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the toolbar.</li><li>Double-click the property on the <strong>Specification</strong> panel. The <strong>Compare property</strong> dialog opens.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6377b1e-13f8-49c1-a178-f4b81f42ac6a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1086956612</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0cd9eacb-fae7-4d1c-a555-09f2ddeb0192"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Model Merge" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737696 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03260: (2026x) Installation

- page_id: `272737696`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737696/2026x+Installation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:10.432+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Report Wizard template editor
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To install Report Wizard Template Editor in Microsoft Word

1. Copy all files and subdirectories from the <install.dir>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\vba folder to %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP**** (%APPDATA% is the location where user data is stored). For example,
  - On Windows 2000 and Windows XP: C:\Documents and Settings\User Name\Application Data\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
  - On Windows Vista: C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
  - On Windows 7, 8, and later: C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP
2. Restart Microsoft Word.

Microsoft Word 64bit may require mscomctl.ocx for this add-in. To install mscomctl.ocx:

1. Go to [https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019](https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019)

2. Download Microsoft Visual Basic 6.0 Common Controls

3. Install mscomctl.ocx by extracting VisualBasic6-????.exe and then copy mscomctl.ocx to C:\Windows\System32 or C:\Windows\SysWOW64

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To install Report Wizard Template Editor in Microsoft Word</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy all files and subdirectories from the <em>&lt;install.dir&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard\vba</em> folder to <em>%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP<strong> </strong></em>(%APPDATA% is the location where user data is stored). For example,<br /><ul><li>On Windows 2000 and Windows XP: <em>C:\Documents and Settings\User Name\Application Data\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li><li>On Windows Vista: <em>C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li><li>On Windows 7, 8, and later: <em>C:\Users\UserName\AppData\Roaming\Microsoft\Word\STARTUP</em></li></ul></li><li>Restart Microsoft Word.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="edbea50e-de80-4591-a358-d537c22b71e9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Microsoft Word 64bit may require mscomctl.ocx for this add-in. To install mscomctl.ocx:</p><p>1. Go to <a href="https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019">https://www.microsoft.com/en-au/download/details.aspx?id=10019</a></p><p>2. Download Microsoft Visual Basic 6.0 Common Controls</p><p>3. Install mscomctl.ocx by extracting VisualBasic6-????.exe and then copy mscomctl.ocx to C:\Windows\System32 or C:\Windows\SysWOW64</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````
