<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738963 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03262: (2026x) Installing custom tool

- page_id: `272738963`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738963/2026x+Installing+custom+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:53.208+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To install the extension, copy the *JAR* file containing a custom tool class into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. Report Wizard will automatically load a new *JAR* file when a new report is generated.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To install the extension, copy the <em>JAR</em> file containing a custom tool class into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. Report Wizard will automatically load a new <em>JAR</em> file when a new report is generated.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731609 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03263: (2026x) Instance Specifications export to the Modelica file

- page_id: `272731609`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731609/2026x+Instance+Specifications+export+to+the+Modelica+file
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:54.240+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Modelica export
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export to the Modelica file a particular configuration with initial values and subtype instances as specified in the instance model. The mechanism generates Modelica (.mo) file with an Instance Specification name and save it to your file directory. In the following figure, the *p* Instance Specification is exported to the Modelica file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The export of*p* Instance Specification to the Modelica file.

****

To export a configuration with initial values

1. In the Model Browser , select an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] you want to export and do one of the following:
  - In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click File > Export To > Modelica .
  - Right-click the selected Instance Specification and then click Tools > Export to Modelica .
2. Specify export options in the Modelica Export Options dialog. Option descriptions >>
3. Click OK . The Modelica (.mo) file is generated and saved to your file directory.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can export to the Modelica file a particular configuration with initial values and subtype instances as specified in the instance model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The mechanism generates Modelica (.mo) file with an Instance Specification name and save it to your file directory. In the following figure, the <em>p</em> Instance Specification is exported to the Modelica file.</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_export_to_modelica.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Instance Specifications export to the Modelica file" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The export of<em> p</em> Instance Specification to the Modelica file.<br /></span></h6><p><strong> </strong></p><p>To export a configuration with initial values</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="610b09e8-ae83-4af6-ba38-6359e1ae4370">In the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Model+Browser">Model Browser</a>, select an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link> you want to export and do one of the following:<ul><li>In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Export To &gt; Modelica</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the selected Instance Specification and then click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Export to Modelica</strong>.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="74adf39c-1abc-4872-a7c0-afc0769e6f2e">Specify export options in the <strong>Modelica Export Options</strong> dialog. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Modelica+export#Modelicaexport-ModelicaExportOptions" rel="nofollow">Option descriptions &gt;&gt;</a></li><li data-uuid="4aef65ad-de83-481c-9e79-241458d6be63">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The Modelica (.mo) file is generated and saved to your file directory.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 03264: (2026x) Instance table

- page_id: `272733733`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733733/2026x+Instance+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:51.823+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

221798943

221798946

221798941

**On this page**

221798945

##### **Description**

This feature is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

The Instance Table is one of four tables which allow you to manage Instance Specifications of your model in a spreadsheet-like form. Each row in the table represents an instance.****

##### **Purpose**

An Instance Table allows you to:

- Create Instance Specifications (instances) for the classifiers.
- Review the instances of one or more of the classifiers in a single place.
- Edit Slot values of the instances displayed in the table.
- Customize the representation of the table.
- Export the data into .html , .csv, or * .xlsx files.

##### **Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### **Specific tasks in the Instance Table**

The following outlines the specific procedures when working with the Instance Table:

221837881

Each row in the table represents an instance.<strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>Purpose</strong></h3><p>An Instance Table allows you to:</p><ul><li>Create Instance Specifications (instances) for the classifiers.</li><li>Review the instances of one or more of the classifiers in a single place.</li><li>Edit Slot values of the instances displayed in the table.</li><li>Customize the representation of the table.</li><li>Export the data into <em>.html</em>, <em>.csv,</em> or *<em>.xlsx</em> files.</li></ul><h3><strong>Example</strong></h3><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Instance table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3><strong>Specific tasks in the Instance Table</strong></h3><p>The following outlines the specific procedures when working with the Instance Table:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="92c20da9-bc9d-453a-acda-884f64ca0f32" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13f10737-34f5-4087-ad3c-e06ad59cedd9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">221837881</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Table toolbars" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Basic tasks in tables" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Hierarchy in tables" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Generic table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Glossary table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Metric table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Legends" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739537 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03265: (2026x) Instantiating structures

- page_id: `272739537`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739537/2026x+Instantiating+structures
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:29.560+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard', 'create-instance', 'instantiate-proposed-typical-structure', 'instant-structures', 'typical-uaf-element', 'actual-uaf-element', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF' space='']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard to instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture automatically.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.
2. Right-click them and choose Tools > Create Instance . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF' space='']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard opens.

To instantiate the proposed typical structure

1. Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_4.png']
2. Right-click them and select **Tools** > **Create****Instance**.
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF' space='']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done. A new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space=''] Actual diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For instantiable UAF elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.

| Typical UAF element | Actual UAF element |
| --- | --- |
| Project | Actual Project |
| Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone |
| Project Theme | Project Status |
| Project Milestone Role | Actual Project Milestone Role |
| Organization | Actual Organization |
| Sub Organization (Resource Role typed by and owned by Organization) | Actual Organization Role |
| Post | Actual Post |
| Post Role (Resource Role typed by Post and owned by Organization) | Actual Organization Role |
| Person | Actual Person |
| Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set |
| Measurement | Actual Measurement |
| Location | Actual Location |
| Capability Configuration | Fielded Capability |
| Environment | Actual Environment |
| Condition | Actual Condition |
| Resource Performer | Actual Resource |
| Enterprise Phase | Actual Enterprise Phase |
| Enduring Task | Actual Enduring Task |
| Responsibility | Actual Responsibility |
| Risk | Actual Risk |
| Natural Resource | Actual Resource |
| Resource Architecture | Actual Resource |
| Resource Artifact | Actual Resource |
| Software | Actual Resource |
| Technology | Actual Resource |
| System | Actual Resource |
| Resource Mitigation | Actual Resource |
| Security Enclave | Actual Resource |
| Known Resource | Actual Resource |
| Operational Performer | Actual Property Set |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> to instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture automatically.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To open the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.</li><li>Right-click them and choose <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Instance</strong>.<br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To instantiate the proposed typical structure</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser.</p><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Typical" ac:alt="OV-4 Typical"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ov_4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Instantiating structures" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Right-click them and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create</strong><strong> Instance</strong>.<span> </span></p></li><li><p>Follow the steps of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF" /><ac:link-body><strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />A new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> Actual diagram is created.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="OV-4 Actual" ac:alt="OV-4 Actual"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OV-4_actual.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Instantiating structures" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>For instantiable UAF elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.</p><p> </p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Typical UAF element</strong></p></td><td><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <strong>Actual</strong> <strong>UAF</strong> <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739535 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 03267: (2026x) Integration with the Concept Modeler Plugin

- page_id: `272739535`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739535/2026x+Integration+with+the+Concept+Modeler+Plugin
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:51:29.276+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Concept Modeler Plugin (CCM) is integrated into the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, UPDM2, and UAF plugins. This plugin replaces the Soa+ Plugin. 
You can make the ontology modeling available in the UAF projects by creating the **Concept Modeling Diagram** from the **Create Diagram** dialog. After the diagram is created, the CCM profiles are loaded and CCM features are available in the project.

For more information about how to work with the Concept Modeler plugin, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Concept Modeler Documentation' space='MCM'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Concept Modeler Plugin (CCM) is integrated into the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, UPDM2, and UAF plugins. This plugin replaces the Soa+ Plugin.<br />You can make the ontology modeling available in the UAF projects by creating the <strong>Concept Modeling Diagram</strong> from the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> dialog. After the diagram is created, the CCM profiles are loaded and CCM features are available in the project.</p><p>For more information about how to work with the Concept Modeler plugin, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCM" ri:content-title="Concept Modeler Documentation" /></ac:link>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733669 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03268: (2026x) Interaction Overview diagram

- page_id: `272733669`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733669/2026x+Interaction+Overview+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:42:44.113+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

308856586

308856588

308856587

**Overview**

An Interaction Overview diagram is a type of Interaction diagram, which falls under the behavioral diagramming family.An Interaction Overview diagram focuses on the flow of control between interactions. It is based on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity diagram' space=''] notation. Interactions in the Interaction Overview diagram are represented using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MED'] element. All elements in the diagram, except for the Interaction Use, are the same as in the Activity diagram.

**Purpose**

An Interaction Overview diagram is intended to be used to model the flow of logic in a series of interactions. To put it another way, the Interaction Overview diagram uses blocks of interactions and individual interactions instead of modeling a series of discrete behaviors (activities).

**Usage**

Interaction Overview diagrams visualize a sequence of activities just like Activity diagrams do; however, they picture each individual entry as a frame which can contain a nested interaction diagram.

**Summary**

Interaction Overview diagrams are valuable because they:

- provide a high-level view of the logical progression through a set of interactions
- keep the focus on the "big picture" and away from implementation details
- encourage the reuse of interactions

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of an Interaction Overview diagram

308856585

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating diagrams' space='']

Interactions in the Interaction Overview diagram are represented using the<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Interaction Use" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ Interaction Use]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> element. All elements in the diagram, except for the Interaction Use, are the same as in the Activity diagram.</p><p><strong>Purpose</strong></p><p>An Interaction Overview diagram is intended to be used to model the flow of logic in a series of interactions. To put it another way, the Interaction Overview diagram uses blocks of interactions and individual interactions instead of modeling a series of discrete behaviors (activities). </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>Interaction Overview diagrams visualize a sequence of activities just like Activity diagrams do; however, they picture each individual entry as a frame which can contain a nested interaction diagram. </p><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Interaction Overview diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>provide a high-level view of the logical progression through a set of interactions</li><li>keep the focus on the &quot;big picture&quot; and away from implementation details</li><li>encourage the reuse of interactions </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="interaction_overview_diagram_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Interaction Overview diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of an Interaction Overview diagram<br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2a23b37-52a4-4422-9417-7709aa16f0f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">308856585</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736326 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03269: (2026x) Interaction Overview diagram elements

- page_id: `272736326`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736326/2026x+Interaction+Overview+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:47:02.276+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Importing data from other UML tools and formats > (2026x) Enterprise Architect Import > (2026x) Special Transformation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

560251696

560251698

560251694

**On this page**

3

560251697

The following sections describe additional EA specific Interaction Overview diagram information.

##### Interaction Overview Diagram

An [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Interaction Overview diagram' space=''] is one of four types of Interaction diagrams. The other three are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Time diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Sequence diagram' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Communication diagram' space='']diagrams. An Interaction Overview diagram, just like an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity diagram' space=''], visualizes a sequence of activities. Most of the notation elements for Interaction Overview diagrams are the same as those for Activity diagrams (such as Initial, Decision, Fork, Join, and Final [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Node' space='MED']). Interaction Occurrences and Interaction elements are two new elements in the interaction overview diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Overview Diagram.

Afterconversion, the Interaction Overview diagram will be placed within anActivityelement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Overview Diagram Placement.

###### Interaction Element

Interaction elements display an inline Interaction diagram (Interaction Overview, Timing, Sequence, or Communication diagram). In EA, Interaction elements can be created to display other diagrams than those classified as Interaction diagrams.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Element in Interaction Overview Diagram.

###### Interaction Occurrence

Interaction Occurrence elements refer to an existing Interaction diagram. They are visually represented by a rectangular frame, which can be created by dragging the Interaction diagram from the project Browser to an Interaction Overview diagram and selecting the **Interaction Occurrence** command from the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Interaction Occurence element.

560258099

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Interaction Overview diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729925 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03270: (2026x) Interchanging of requirements using Cameo DataHub

- page_id: `272729925`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729925/2026x+Interchanging+of+requirements+using+Cameo+DataHub
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:28.947+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Requirements management > (2026x) Importing requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Cameo DataHub is a dedicated solution for the requirements synchronization between different sources, such as MagicDraw, Rational DOORS®, CSV files, and other. Cameo DataHub is a data bridge that allows for data importing and exporting, automatic synchronization and creation of references between requirements and other artifacts. It allows to use different import and synchronization methods while importing or exporting requirements.

Came DataHub allows to:

- Associate and synchronize data.
- Change the monitoring statuses such as active, pending update, and pending delete whenever associated data items are modified or deleted.
- Impact monitoring. Whenever the referenced data item changes, Cameo DataHub reports the suspect status to direct traceability from each requirement to the respective design artifact.
- Define mappings between data and its’ properties.

The Cameo DataHub must be installed before taking the following actions. For more information refer to, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='CDH'].

To interchange of requirements using Cameo DataHub

1. Open an existing project or create a new one.
2. On the main menu, click Tools > Requirements > DataHub and select a desired action.

For more information about working with Cameo DataHub, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User Guide' space='CDH'].

800700

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='CDH']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='User Guide' space='CDH']
- Importing requirements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729175 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03271: (2026x) Intermediate Catch Event

- page_id: `272729175`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729175/2026x+Intermediate+Catch+Event
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:34:07.813+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Intermediate Catch Event indicates that something is happening between the start and end of a process. Intermediate Events affect the flow of a process, but do not start or directly terminate the process.

You can use Intermediate Catch Event to:

- Show where messages are expected or sent within a process.
- Show delays that are expected within a process.
- Interrupt normal flow through exception handling.

Types of Intermediate Catch Events are the following:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Intermediate Catch Event indicates that something is happening between the start and end of a process. Intermediate Events affect the flow of a process, but do not start or directly terminate the process.</p><p>You can use Intermediate Catch Event to:</p><ul><li>Show where messages are expected or sent within a process.</li><li>Show delays that are expected within a process.</li><li>Interrupt normal flow through exception handling.</li></ul><p>Types of Intermediate Catch Events are the following:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3979d68d-a16d-4b70-82c8-54bf334f0d8b" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

An Intermediate Throwing Event indicates that something is happening between the start and end of a process. Intermediate Events affect the flow of a process, but do not start or directly terminate the process.

You can use Intermediate Throwing Events to show extra work required.

Types of Intermediate Throwing Events are as follows:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738241 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03274: (2026x) Internal Influencer

- page_id: `272738241`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738241/2026x+Internal+Influencer
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:46:19.601+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x) Business Motivation Diagram > (2026x) Influencer Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487581411

1487581411

1487581411

**Description**

Internal Influencer is an enterprise that can impact its employment of Means or achievement of Ends.

Internal Influencer is usually categorized the following:

- Assumption: Something that is taken for granted or without proof.
- Explicit Corporate Value: An ideal, custom, or institution that an enterprise promotes or agrees with that is explicitly set forth and declared.
- Implicit Corporate Value: A corporate value that is not explicitly declared but nonetheless understood by some or all of the people in an enterprise.
- Habit: A customary practice or use.
- Infrastructure: The basic underlying framework or features of a system.
- Issue: A point in question or a matter that is in dispute as between contending partners.
- Management Prerogative: A right or privilege exercised by virtue of ownership or position in an enterprise.
- Resource: The resources available for carrying out the business of an enterprise, especially their quality.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1487581410

**Related elements**

- Influencer Concepts
- Influence Concepts Relationship

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735638 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03275: (2026x) Introducing Macro Engine

- page_id: `272735638`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735638/2026x+Introducing+Macro+Engine
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:36.024+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Macro Engine (previously called Script Engine) allows you to create your own macro (script) by using BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript Rhino, or Jython. With Macro Engine, you can control everything that is allowed in Open API, for example, transforming and manipulating models. Learn more about Open API in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Developer Guides Home' space='DEVG'] and find sample macros in *<modeling tool installation directory>/samples/product features/macros*.

Macro Engine comes with Professional, Architect, and Enterprise Editions starting from version 16.6 and later.

##### Changing Javascript Nashorn to Javascript Rhino

The Macro Engine supports Javascript Rhino. If you have any macros written in Javascript Nashorn, which is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn' space=''], you can change it to Javascript Rhino.

To change to Javascript Rhino

- Click Tools > Macros > Change JS Nashorn to Rhino

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Macro Engine (previously called Script Engine) allows you to create your own macro (script) by using BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript Rhino, or Jython. With Macro Engine, you can control everything that is allowed in Open API, for example, transforming and manipulating models. Learn more about Open API in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Developer Guides Home" ri:space-key="DEVG" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Developer Guide]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and find sample macros in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;/samples/product features/macros</em>.</p><p>Macro Engine comes with Professional, Architect, and Enterprise Editions starting from version 16.6 and later.</p><h3>Changing Javascript Nashorn to Javascript Rhino</h3><p>The Macro Engine supports Javascript Rhino. If you have any macros written in Javascript Nashorn, which is <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deprecated JavaScript Nashorn" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[deprecated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can change it to Javascript Rhino.</p><p>To change to Javascript Rhino</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Macros</strong> &gt; <strong>Change JS Nashorn to Rhino</strong></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737629 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03277: (2026x) Introduction

- page_id: `272737629`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737629/2026x+Introduction
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:53.554+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Template Creation Tutorial
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355068225

1355068226

1355068226

This user guide demonstrates how to create a report template using Report Wizard and use it in the modeling tool. To make the most of Report Wizard, you need to understand Apache Velocity and the modeling tool elements. Report Wizard uses Apache Velocity, this means that you will need to know how to create a Velocity template and that every rule that applies to Velocity will also apply to the Report Wizard.

Velocity is a Java-based template engine that processes templates and references Java objects to produce output documents. A basic Velocity template can contain static text, layouts, conditional statements, and placeholders for each referenced Java object. When a template is being read by Velocity, conditional statements will be processed and placeholders will be replaced with the value from the referenced Java objects. You can use Velocity, for example, to generate web pages, emails, and read *XML* documents, but with Report Wizard it is now possible to use Velocity to read other types of templates, such as Rich Text Format documents. This user guide covers some basic aspects of Velocity, modeling tool elements, and Report Wizard directives.

1355068224

**Related page**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

To make the most of Report Wizard, you need to understand Apache Velocity and the modeling tool elements. Report Wizard uses Apache Velocity, this means that you will need to know how to create a Velocity template and that every rule that applies to Velocity will also apply to the Report Wizard.</p><p>Velocity is a Java-based template engine that processes templates and references Java objects to produce output documents. A basic Velocity template can contain static text, layouts, conditional statements, and placeholders for each referenced Java object. When a template is being read by Velocity, conditional statements will be processed and placeholders will be replaced with the value from the referenced Java objects. You can use Velocity, for example, to generate web pages, emails, and read <em>XML</em> documents, but with Report Wizard it is now possible to use Velocity to read other types of templates, such as Rich Text Format documents. This user guide covers some basic aspects of Velocity, modeling tool elements, and Report Wizard directives.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2794887a-6f98-48f2-9b85-a3f47c2851b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355068224</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4dc280e8-5e54-4745-a5b5-4e96822291c6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730467 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03282: (2026x) Issues related to collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `272730467`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730467/2026x+Issues+related+to+collaboration+powered+by+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:37:15.655+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are several known issues when working with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform:

- Migration from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.3 is not available.
- The Change Library Version action is unavailable for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Using libraries' space='']
- Migration of UPDM 2.1 project containing UJTL or DISR libraries from version 2022x to 2024x (upgrading profiles) is not available on Power'By.

For more information about working with the platform, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are several known issues when working with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Migration from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.3 is not available.</li><li>The <strong>Change Library Version</strong> action is unavailable for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using libraries" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UJTL and DISR libraries.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Migration of UPDM 2.1 project containing UJTL or DISR libraries from version 2022x to 2024x (upgrading profiles) is not available on Power'By.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd7e78b6-ea0a-4e98-bb73-e0ed5c2cbe1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with the platform, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272728942 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03283: (2026x) Item Flow management

- page_id: `272728942`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272728942/2026x+Item+Flow+management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:01.264+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create Item Flows in two modes:

- Direct
- Between Part Types

The *Direct* creation mode allows you to create the Item Flow between ports or parts in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

The *Between Part Types* creation mode allows you to create the Item Flow between Part Property types. In this mode, you can quickly realize and synchronize Flows on Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages.

To choose the creation mode

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project . The Project Option dialog opens.
2. Expand the General options group and select SysML .
3. From the Item Flow Creation Mode option, choose one of the following:
  - Direct
  - Between Part Types
4. Click OK when you are done.

For more information on working with Item Flows, see the following chapters:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create Item Flows in two modes:</p><ul><li>Direct</li><li>Between Part Types</li></ul><p>The <em>Direct</em> creation mode allows you to create the Item Flow between ports or parts in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p>The <em>Between Part Types</em> creation mode allows you to create the Item Flow between Part Property types. In this mode, you can quickly realize and synchronize Flows on Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages.</p><p><br /></p><p>To choose the creation mode</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Option</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Expand the <strong>General</strong> options group and select <strong>SysML</strong>.</li><li>From the<strong> Item Flow Creation Mode</strong> option, choose one of the following:<ul><li><strong>Direct</strong></li><li><strong>Between Part Types</strong></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For more information on working with Item Flows, see the following chapters:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="0d2a8234-7674-45bd-913f-a27021c8f7f3" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272728968 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03284: (2026x) Item Flow Manager dialog

- page_id: `272728968`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272728968/2026x+Item+Flow+Manager+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:06.549+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Item Flow management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

The **Item Flow Manager** dialog enables you to analyze and manage Item Flow data. You can:

- Edit existing Item Flows by adding new Conveyed Items
- Realize or hide existing Item Flows on selected relationships
- Change the Conveyed Item
- Review or delete existing Activity Pairs
- Review or update diagrams [ATTACHMENT filename='item_flow_manager_dialog.png']

##### Opening the Item Flow Manager dialog

To open the **Item Flow****Manager** dialog

1. Select Association, Connector, Association,Control Flow , Object Flow, or Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='item_flow_manager_icon.png'] . The Item Flow Manager dialog opens.

##### Item Flow Manager buttons

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Click to add a new Item Flow. |
|  | Click to delete an Item Flow.The button is disabled if no Item Flow is selected in the dialog. |
|  | Toggle to either show or hide inherited Item Flows.The child element (subtype) in a generalization relationship contains inherited Item Flows from the parent element (supertype). |
|  | Toggle to either show or hide aggregated Item Flows. The parent (composite) element in a composition relationship contains aggregated Item Flows from all child elements (composite parts). |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle to either show or hide full element paths. |
|  | Select to review, preview, or update diagrams in which the realizing elements are shown. Clicking the button opens the Affected Diagrams dialog, which displays the diagrams that use the elements realizing the selected Item Flow.To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see .The button is only available when a realized Item Flow is selected in the dialog. |
|  | Enter keyword(s) to filter the data displayed in the dialog.To open the menu with more filter options, click the button in the Filter box. |
|  | Click to realize all Item Flows displayed in the dialog. |
|  | Click not to realize any Item Flows displayed in the dialog. |
|  | Select to review, preview, or update diagrams in which the realizing elements are shown. Clicking the button opens the All Affected Diagrams dialog, which displays the diagrams that use the elements realizing the Item Flows displayed in the Item Flow Manager.To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see .The button is only available when there is at least one realized Item Flow in the dialog. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729177 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03285: (2026x) Items and Data

- page_id: `272729177`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729177/2026x+Items+and+Data
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:45:26.354+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) BPMN modeling > (2026x) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x) BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485899704

1485899706

1485899706

The traditional requirement of process modeling is to be able to model the items (physical or information items) that are created, manipulated, and used during the execution of a process. This requirement is fulfilled in BPMN through various constructs: Data Objects, Item Definition, Properties, Data Inputs, Data Outputs, Messages, Input Sets, Output Sets, and Data Associations.

1485899704

**Related elements**

- Data Object
- Data Store
- Data Input and Data Output
- Data Association

This requirement is fulfilled in BPMN through various constructs: Data Objects, Item Definition, Properties, Data Inputs, Data Outputs, Messages, Input Sets, Output Sets, and Data Associations.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8243c56e-5140-4aa7-936a-40c20d286da1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485899704</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Data+Object">Data Object</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Data+Store">Data Store</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Data+Input+and+Data+Output">Data Input and Data Output</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x) Data+Association">Data Association</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
